name: Edouard Delaunay 'Septembre' Chardonnay 2019, Burgundy
description: One half of the Abbotts & Delaunay powerhouse, Laurent Delaunay, has returned to Burgundy to resurrect his family's winemaking estate. And he's made some excellent wine on the way, like this Chardonnay. It's barrel-aged and stirred on texture-building lees. This results in a gloriously indulgent wine that stands with some of the finest oaked whites. Expect expressive hints of citrus, white chocolate, butter and hazelnut. Pair with an equally buttery lobster dish.

name: The Ned Waihopai River Sauvignon Blanc 2020 Marlborough
description: Our bestselling white wine. Winemaker Brent Marris’ father was the first person to plant Sauvignon vines in Marlborough. It seems extraordinary that in a single generation a whole new type of wine could have been developed. Brent has had just as big an impact on Kiwi Sauvignon as his dad. He’s got rafts of awards, and has launched multiple famous wine brands. Suffice to say, he really knows what he’s doing and that comes through in The Ned Waihopai River Sauvignon Blanc. Its magic is that it is so consistently, definitively a Marlborough Sauvignon. It’s tropical, it’s citrussy – it’s the most refreshing thing you could pour into your glass at 6pm. Full of gooseberry and grapefruit flavours, it’s grassy, tropical and seriously aromatic. Fantastic with Thai-style fish cakes.

name: The King's Favour Sauvignon Blanc 2019/20 Marlborough
description: Brent Marris is winemaking royalty. And it turns out, he may be descended from actual royalty too. His ancestor, William de Marisco was rumoured to be the illegitimate son of Henry I – not that that guaranteed the family good relations with royalty. The de Mariscos spent much of the 12th and 13th centuries living a life of piracy on the Isle of Lundy. Brent created the King Series to celebrate his family’s history, and their tempestuous relationship with the monarchy. This is a classic Waihopai Sauvignon Blanc – an important Marlborough sub-region where the wines are particularly vibrant. Think guava, gooseberry and a hint of blackcurrant leaf which sets this apart from the standard. Of course, it’s also mouth-wateringly fresh – a dream with barbecued fish with mango salsa.

name: Château Livran 2014, Médoc
description: Château Livran once belonged to both Edward I and Pope Clement V. It was also a hub for the French Resistance. Today, it's run by Edwige Lurton Michon and Olivier Michon. Edwige is the daughter of famed Bordeaux expert Lucien Lurton. She's followed in his footsteps to continue Château Livran’s impressive history. This is a supple red with elegant tannins. Expect sophisticated notes of blackcurrant, mulberry and hints of spice. Partner with roast chicken.

name: Cave de Lugny 'Reserve' Mâcon-Chardonnay 2019
description: The small village of Mâcon-Chardonnay isn’t coincidentally named. It claims to be the birthplace of Chardonnay and made a name for itself in the nineteenth century for producing high-quality Chardonnay wines. Cave de Lugny are a true specialist – they’re the biggest white Burgundy producer in the world, but they don’t rest on their laurels. They’re determined to prove that classic Burgundy doesn’t have to be boring or expensive. This wine is a case in point. Despite its history, as a lesser-known region it’s great value for money. It’s rested on its lees – spent yeast particles – which intensifies the flavours and gives the texture a creaminess which is really moreish. It’s got lovely ripe fruit flavours. Peach. Apricot. Apple. Citrussy freshness. It’s fantastic with soft, creamy cheese.

name: Krasno Sauvignon Blanc Ribolla 2019, Brda
description: If you choose right, you can find a real bargain in Slovenia. We can't think of a better example than Krasno's delightful white wines. This is made just over the border from Fruli, in Italy, where similar wines demand considerably higher prices. It's fresh and elegant, with plenty of green-apple flavours, floral aromas and a fruity finish. Try it with white fish.

name: Bread & Butter Chardonnay 2019, California
description: This really does what it says on the tin. It’s a lush Chardonnay which tastes of – you guessed it – bread and butter. But it’s a lot more than just a creamy and comforting hug of a wine. The way it’s made makes it really exciting. The winemaker combined grapes from two very different regions. Monterey is known for its big Chardonnays, thanks to the generous length of the growing season. On the other hand, Carneros is far cooler, so the flavours are more understated. The briochey notes that make us think of Champagne. Bring the two together and you get this – a marriage of styles which is full but fresh, rich but refreshing. It’s a very comforting, warm style of Chardonnay. It’s packed with flavour and is fantastic with food – anything with brown butter sauce is heaven. We can’t lie – it’s not a subtle wine. But that’s why we love it, and why you will too.

name: Edna Valley Pinot Noir 2018, Central Coast
description: Halfway between LA and San Francisco you’ll find Edna Valley AVA, a stone’s throw from the Pacific. It’s not as well-known as regions like Napa and Sonoma – making it an enclave of not just quality, but value too. When Jack Niven first arrived in the valley in the 1970s, his ambition to plant vines in what was then dairy pasture was laughed out of the room. But he preserved, and pioneered not just vineyards, but the certification of the region as an American Viticultural Area. Edna Valley Vineyard was born – and forty years later, it’s still making wines which are true to the cool, coastal climate. This Pinot Noir is a case in point. Light and silky, it’s a wonderfully fresh style. It’s full of blackberry and black cherry flavours – with a touch of tart cranberry. Ideal with roast lamb.

name: Louis Latour Mâcon-Lugny 2019
description: We’ve sold this wine for thirty years – and for that whole time it’s been a bestseller. Why’s it so popular? White Burgundy is a go-to for Chardonnay drinkers, and the Mâconnais in particular offers great value. Unlike the northern Côtes, the southern part of Burgundy doesn’t have a formalised vineyard hierarchy system. So the wines tend to be a bit more wallet-friendly. Not to mention, Lugny is one of the best regarded villages in the Mâcon, and Latour one of the most famous houses. Honeyed. Peachy. Crisp. A dream with salty finger foods or a classic Sunday roast chicken. It’s proof that sometimes it pays to stick with the old favourites.

name: Pommery 'Brut Royal' Champagne
description: Looking for a luxurious gift that's sure to spoil? This Pommery Brut Royal comes ready-wrapped in a unique presentation box. Pommery has been made in Reims since 1836, and they were the first house to pioneer the brut style of Champagne. It's a style they continue to make to this day. This famous non-vintage wine has been matured for three years in their historical chalk cellars. So you can expect excellence in every sip. It shows bright, lively notes of honey, almond, brioche and citrus. It's the perfect way way to toast life's big moments

name: Graham Beck Brut, South Africa
description: You’ll be in good company when you uncork a bottle of this Brut. It’s affectionately known as the ‘President’s Choice’ because it was served at both Nelson Mandela’s inauguration and Barack Obama’s presidential win. It’s an ‘MCC’, méthode cap Classique – a style identical to the traditional method used to make Champagne, using Chardonnay and Pinot Noir too. It means it’s a real steal for fizz fans who love the taste of Champagne – but not the price. Fresh but creamy. Full of limey citrus flavours – it’s got a lovely briochey richness from its second fermentation in bottle. It’s fantastic with cheesy nibbles.

name: Managers' Choice: VIK 'A' Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Cachapoal Valley
description: In 2004, Alex and Carrie Vik had the ambition to create some of the world's best wines. After a two-year study of 4,300 hectares of Andean wilderness, they're behind some of Chile's finest Cabernet. This one was picked as the best of a delicious pool of contenders by our store managers. It's complex and elegant, with an inviting smooth texture and notes of ripe red fruit and coffee. Enjoy with slow-cooked beef

name: Rocklin Ranch Pinot Noir 2017/18, Monterey
description: This Pinot Noir was crafted at the foot of Monterey’s volcanic mountain range. Here, the Scheid family’s winery is powered with a single wind turbine. They pay strict attention to detail, as only 2% of grapes picked are judged as good enough to make this Pinot Noir. So you're in for something great. This lush wine shows notes of raspberry and cherry with a hint of toasted oak. Serve with roasted pork loin.

name: Greywacke Pinot Noir 2015 Marlborough
description: A virtual compote loaded with black plums, boysenberries and redcurrants lightly infused with cinnamon and cloves. An intensely perfumed Marlborough Pinot with fruit sweetness, floral highlights and a distinct smoky fragrance. The full-flavoured palate has dark fruit richness with great length and freshness - finishing with a hint of Middle Eastern spice.

name: Château Musar 2001, Gaston Hochar, Lebanon
description: Lebanese winemaking spans over 6,000 years. Château Musar is the country's best known winery for good reason. This is rich, vibrant and brimming with delicious spices. You'll have a tough time finding anything that comes close elsewhere.

name: Cave de Viré Viré-Clessé 2019
description: Viré-Clessé is so highly regarded that, in 1998, it was awarded its own appellation status in the renowned Mâconnais region. Cave de Viré’s expertise here has been recognised worldwide. Thanks to this area's limestone soils and flintiness, this is a mineral, elegant Chardonnay. Expect charming notes of tropical fruit, peach and honeysuckle. Perfect with roast chicken.

name: Duckhorn Vineyards Chardonnay 2018, Napa Valley
description: Duckhorn Vineyards was one of the first forty wineries established in Napa Valley. The area has a stellar reputation for a reason – its vineyards are the first place to look for grapes of outstanding quality. Napa is inland and has higher temperatures than neighbouring coastal regions. These conditions make Chardonnay particularly rich and fruity. Think white peaches, blossom and creamy lemon flavours. This is a complex, textured white with. the subtle sweetness of French oak. It’s a sumptuous wine that will elevate a simple roast chicken to something really special.

name: Pol Roger 'Sir Winston Churchill' Champagne
description: The clue's in the name. Pol Roger created this in tribute to Winston Churchill in the style of the 1928 vintage, one of his personal favourites. It's made exclusively from Grand Cru Chardonnay and Pinot Noir vineyards that were under vine in his lifetime. This is robust, full-bodied and rich, with a capacity to age for decades. It's so delicious, the family behind it keep the recipe top-secret. A fitting tribute to one of Britain's most prominent figures. Drink with oysters.

name: Ch St Michelle Cabernet Sauvignon 2016
description: This Cabernet from Washington's oldest winery is packed with ripe blackcurrant and blackberry flavours. Notes of cedar and vanilla follow on to the silky smooth finish that lingers on the palate with the blackcurrant and plum. The most perfect suitor: Prosciutto wrapped stuffed dates with blue cheese. A must.

name: Yalumba 'The Signature' Cabernet Sauvignon-Shiraz 2015, Barossa
description: You may have tried Yalumba's entry-level work before. This is its Saturday-night wine. Almost equal parts Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz, it?s a rich, full-bodied cocktail of mocha, plum, cherry and black pepper that boasts plenty of complexity. Wine Enthusiast gave it 93 points, calling it "Classic Barossa".

name: Vavasour 'Papa' Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Marlborough
description: Awatere Valley’s stony soils nourish grapes that develop distinct elegance, perfume and texture over a lengthy ripening period. But this cool-climate’s brilliance was unknown until it was discovered by Vavasour. They've picked every grape behind this from one carefully selected spot to make a truly unique, precise expression of the land. Expect a mineral-lined palate of citrus, fresh herbs, blackcurrant leaf and white peach. It’ll partner beautifully with soft, creamy cheeses.

name: Gabb Family Cabernet Sauvignon
description: A core of rich blackcurrants and black plums, with a savoury overlay of meat extract, white pepper and mixed spice. The velvety texture is the result of maturation - the wine is at the perfect drinking age and has fine textured tannins.

name: Louis Pommery English Brut, Hampshire
description: After a space race between all of Champagne's biggest names to get here first, Pommery is the first house to launch English fizz. Teaming up with Hampshire-based Hattingley Valley, owned by the chairman of Wines of Great Britain, it's applied the same traditional method that it uses back home to craft a crisp, arrow-precise bouquet of waxy lemon, Granny Smith, freshly baked bread and honeycomb.

name: Laurent-Perrier Rosé NV Champagne
description: Unusually made by the saignée method, with plenty of stylish strawberry fruit, full of vivacity and easy-drinking charm. A wonderfully refreshing apéritif, it also works well with a variety of dishes.

name: Laurent-Perrier Rosé NV Champagne Magnum
description: This is a classic pink fizz made with a method rarely applied in Champagne. The result's plenty of stylish strawberry fruit, vivacity and easy-drinking charm. It's a wonderfully refreshing apéritif. But its elegant structure means that it also works well with a variety of dishes, particularly shellfish.

name: Bollinger Champagne Magnum
description: At the Bollinger Champagne house, only the finest will do. Their Champagne is made from first-run juice, and the majority of vineyards have Premier and Grand Cru status. This results in a full-bodied Champagne of great class. It's pale-gold in colour and the mousse is light and persistent. The ideal accompaniment to smoked salmon canapes.

name: Lanson 'Black Label' Champagne
description: With over 260 years of winemaking history, Lanson is one of the oldest Champagne houses. Their Black Label was famously given as a tribute to the Royal Court of England in 1937. They've been supplying it with world-class wine ever since. The Lanson Black Label has a fine stream of persistent bubbles and a beautiful straw colour. The nose is vibrant with hints of toast and flowers leading to a palate of citrus and ripe fruit. The perfect Champage to elevate the flavours of fish and chips.

name: Laurent-Perrier 'Grand Siècle' Champagne
description: Laurent-Perrier's deluxe cuvée, Grand Siècle, is a blend of a number of the best vintages. It has a beautifully elegant, fresh, creamy style. Gloriously fine bubbles and a slight nuttiness complete this sophisticated Champagne. Serve with canapé at a special occasion.

name: Heidsieck & Co. Monopole 2011 'Gold Top' Champagne
description: Any loyal Majestic customer knows that ‘Gold Top’ is shorthand for luxury-quality bubbles – at a fraction of the price of the top Champagne cuvées. Heidsieck age this wine for up to a decade before they release it. Time equals money – so this lengthy ageing is usually something only the luxury cuvées can afford. But Heidsieck are known for combining amazing quality fizz with amazing value. This long ageing means the toasty, creamy, rich flavours we love so much are dialled right up. Lemony, perfumed, with a really lush texture – the flavours will linger with you for ages. It’s real proof that good things come to those who wait. There’s nothing better to dress up fish and chips.

name: Veuve Clicquot Rosé NV Champagne
description: When Madame Clicquot took the reins of Veuve Clicquot, in 1815, the Champagne house was never the same again. Her legacy of excellence continues to this day. This is the newest addition to the Veuve Clicquot range. This medium bodied rosé is reminiscent of the power and finesse of the non-vintage label, with soft red-fruit notes. Match with smoked salmon or strawberries and cream for optimum enjoyment.

name: Cloudy Bay Pelorus Sparkling Brut
description: You'll know Cloudy Bay for their award-winning Sauvignon. But their sparkling wines are just as deserving of the spotlight. Pelorus is a Chardonnay-dominant blend made only in the best vintages. It's aged on its lees for a generous two years. This gives a nutty, elegant complexity to crisp apple flavours. Try it well-chilled with crab pasta.

name: Jackson Estate 'Stich' Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: It’s Marlborough Sauvignon – with a difference. Jackson Estate’s ‘Stitch’ Sauvignon Blanc is a bit of a cult favourite. It’s named after the founder John ‘Stich’ Stichbury, whose family have deep roots in Marlborough. But despite their long history, Jackson Estate have always preferred to keep production low key. They’re all about sustainably made wines, made with minimal winemaking intervention. Take this Sauvignon. It’s released six months later than other Kiwi Sauvignons. That’s because it spends extra time ageing on the lees – giving a richness and roundness to the texture. It complements the really restrained flavours, making for a more French, old-school style Sauvignon rather than the typical Marlborough tropical fruit-bomb. Gooseberry; zingy rhubarb – perfect with goat’s cheese.

name: Definition Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc is the country’s favourite style of wine, so if we were going to introduce one to our Definition range we knew we had to bring in the big guns. There was only one man for the job – Brent Maris, the winemaker behind our bestselling Kiwi Sauvignon, The Ned. We worked with Brent to cherry pick the very best grapes from specific parcels of vines. We wanted to go big on flavour and make it so refreshing it’d make your mouth water just thinking about it. The result? It’s the very best of Marlborough in a bottle. It’s a heady mix of fresh-cut grass, elderflower and passionfruit, with textbook freshness and cirtussy flavours. It’ll sing with any tangy, dairy-based dishes. Or just by itself.

name: Duckhorn Cabernet Sauvignon 2017, California
description: Duckhorn have quickly become one of America's star winemaking names. And it's all thanks to this iconic Napa Cabernet. They've been making it since 1978 from the very best fruit of the valley. Their secret trick is blending the Cabernet Sauvignon with a dash of Merlot and miniscule amounts of Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc. This means it's deliciously approachable in its youth but also matures excellently with age. Expect a luscious, intense wine, with notes of boysenberry and blackcurrant, and a balancing savoury streak. Try it with a beef burger.

name: Coney Pizzicato Pinot Noir 2017 Martinborough
description: Grown on the Martinborough terrace north of Wellington, by winemakers Debbie Christensen and Emma Easthorpe. The chewy tannins and complex fruit aromas of Martinborough wines shine through in this particularly expressive Pinot Noir. Ripe cherry, black fruits and smoky, savoury overtones with plum and pomegranate fruits combined with spicy, toasty, complex flavours complemented by integrated chewy tannins and a long, savoury finish. Good Kiwi Pinot can be enjoyed in its youth, or over five years. An ideal match with venison, game and pasta.

name: Mt Difficulty 'Roaring Meg' Pinot Noir 2019, Central Otago
description: Roaring Meg came about almost by accident. In 2003, there was a bumper vintage. There was so much leftover fruit that the team at Mt. Difficulty saw an opportunity to try out a different style of Pinot Noir. Their Pinot vineyards in Bannockburn in Central Otago are the oldest in the area so the quality of even the excess fruit was really high. It lent itself to a Pinot Noir which was first and foremost fruity – one which was made to drink young. Roaring Meg was born. And it was such a success, they’ve been making it ever since. This is a real favourite of ours – packed with red fruity flavours. Raspberry. Red cherry. A hint of spice. Perfect with glazed ham or rustic duck cassoulet, it’s a really savoury, grown-up wine. You’ll love it.

name: Saint Clair Pioneer Block Chardonnay 2018 Marlborough
description: St. Clair have gone for maximum impact and expression with this Chardonnay, fermenting 100% of the grapes in a mixture of new and old French oak, before ageing the wine on for 9 months, with regular stirring to draw full character from the lees. The New Zealand trademarks of fresh and snappy peach and tropical fruits are finished with a wonderfully soft, generous texture and a biscuity edge. Rich, with plentiful amounts of pure, tropical fruit, this wine is fantastic with grilled asparagus or fish with parsley sauce.

name: Majestic Loves Chardonnay 2019
description: A real gem, our Majestic Loves Chardonnay is produced by decades-old vines in the Robertson area of South Africa. Expect plenty of fresh citrus, lime and tropical flavours. A superb choice with poultry.

name: Gabb Family Chardonnay 2020
description: Situated in the most Southerly part of Stellenbosch the cooler climate of the vineyards produces a premium wine with rich and complex flavours of Vanilla and butterscoth balanced by a fresh citrus finish.

name: Errazuriz Sauvignon Blanc 2020 Casablanca Valley
description: Don Maximiano Errázuriz was a driven man. In 1870, he spent two days straight on horseback, travelling 100km north of Santiago on a quest to find a terroir he felt did justice to Chile’s wild spirit. In the shadow of the mountains at Acoucagua Valley, he found it. Maximiano was the first to introduce French grapes to the valley, founding a family winery which is still flourishing nearly 150 years later. This lip-smacking Sauvignon Blanc is a great example of his foresight. It’s grown just a few kilometres inland of the Pacific, and the sea has a big imapct on flavour. Freshness is the watchword here. It’s crisp. It’s zingy. But it’s also ripe with those delicious gooseberry and passion fruit flavours we love in a Sauvignon. It’s incredible with ceviche – or tangy, dairy-based dishes.

name: Santa Rita 'Medalla Real' Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Maipo Valley
description: Santa Rita was founded in 1880, by Mr. Domingo Fernández Concha. Each lot is individually vinified at its peak of ripeness and aged in French oak barrels. Deep, ripe ruby red in colour with a nose of blackcurrant, menthol and toasty vanilla notes. Rich, ripe almost sweet tannins and a long length. Enjoy with spicy red meats, venison and goose.

name: Vieux Remparts Lussac St Emilion Magnum 2016
description: Lussac is one of five villages to the northeast of St-Emilion with the right to add that name to their own. Lussac makes excellent 'satellite' wines, slightly more rustic than its big brother, but rounded and with great charm.

name: Château Tour Haut Caussan 2012
description: Rated by Robert Parker as producing wines of 5th growth quality in the best year this is powerful with ripe blackcurrant fruit flavours followed by notes of vanilla and cedar.

name: Domaine Jean-Max Roger ‘Cuvée Genese’ Sancerre 2019
description: Jean-Max Roger began with just four hectares of vines. In the 50 years since, he's consistently produced sensational Sauvignon Blanc, and his winery is now one of Sancerre's finest. Today, he and his two sons pick each grape by hand on sustainably-run vineyards. This wine is then only released when the Rogers deem it ready. And the attention to detail shines in every sip. This is a remarkably fresh and complex Sancerre. Pair its notes of citrus and white flowers and long, flavoursome finish with white fish.

name: Domaine du Vaux St Georges Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Touraine
description: Domaine du Vaux St Georges offers a fantastic terroir of flinty sands, close to the Cher river. The influence of which, gives fantastic crispness to their grapes, creating some of the best that Touraine has to offer. A pale yellow juice boasts aromas of citrus, elderflower and a note of cedar. True to its exquisite background, the finish is long and balanced. A true summer's refresher Fantastic as an aperitif, or enjoy with a wide variety of fish.

name: Duckhorn Vineyards Chardonnay 2018, Napa Valley
description: Duckhorn Vineyards was one of the first forty wineries established in Napa Valley. The area has a stellar reputation for a reason – its vineyards are the first place to look for grapes of outstanding quality. Napa is inland and has higher temperatures than neighbouring coastal regions. These conditions make Chardonnay particularly rich and fruity. Think white peaches, blossom and creamy lemon flavours. This is a complex, textured white with. the subtle sweetness of French oak. It’s a sumptuous wine that will elevate a simple roast chicken to something really special.

name: Pol Roger 'Sir Winston Churchill' Champagne
description: The clue's in the name. Pol Roger created this in tribute to Winston Churchill in the style of the 1928 vintage, one of his personal favourites. It's made exclusively from Grand Cru Chardonnay and Pinot Noir vineyards that were under vine in his lifetime. This is robust, full-bodied and rich, with a capacity to age for decades. It's so delicious, the family behind it keep the recipe top-secret. A fitting tribute to one of Britain's most prominent figures. Drink with oysters.

name: Making Tracks Cabernet Sauvignon 2017
description: Cabernet loves the sun but often in its famous Bordeaux home, it doesn't get enough. This can produce green, austere wines. No such problem in sun-licked Australia. Here Cabernet is pampered and produces big, flavourful reds that cost a fraction of the price of its French cousins. Making Tracks is a tribute to this Aussie brilliance. It's a spicy cocktail of rich blackcurrant, dark cherry, vanilla and oak.

name: Shaw & Smith Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Adelaide Hills
description: Shaw & Smith have made this Sauvignon Blanc for 30 years. Made from hand-picked grapes, aged on texture-imparting lees, this is no ordinary Sauvignon Blanc. Expect delicious notes of pink grapefruit, a bright, fresh palate and a luxuriously long finish. Serve with smoked salmon bagels. James Halliday, Australia's top wine critic, gave the 2019 vintage 95 points.

name: Ch St Michelle Cabernet Sauvignon 2016
description: This Cabernet from Washington's oldest winery is packed with ripe blackcurrant and blackberry flavours. Notes of cedar and vanilla follow on to the silky smooth finish that lingers on the palate with the blackcurrant and plum. The most perfect suitor: Prosciutto wrapped stuffed dates with blue cheese. A must.

name: Yalumba 'The Signature' Cabernet Sauvignon-Shiraz 2015, Barossa
description: You may have tried Yalumba's entry-level work before. This is its Saturday-night wine. Almost equal parts Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz, it?s a rich, full-bodied cocktail of mocha, plum, cherry and black pepper that boasts plenty of complexity. Wine Enthusiast gave it 93 points, calling it "Classic Barossa".

name: Vavasour 'Papa' Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Marlborough
description: Awatere Valley’s stony soils nourish grapes that develop distinct elegance, perfume and texture over a lengthy ripening period. But this cool-climate’s brilliance was unknown until it was discovered by Vavasour. They've picked every grape behind this from one carefully selected spot to make a truly unique, precise expression of the land. Expect a mineral-lined palate of citrus, fresh herbs, blackcurrant leaf and white peach. It’ll partner beautifully with soft, creamy cheeses.

name: Gabb Family Cabernet Sauvignon
description: A core of rich blackcurrants and black plums, with a savoury overlay of meat extract, white pepper and mixed spice. The velvety texture is the result of maturation - the wine is at the perfect drinking age and has fine textured tannins.

name: VIK Milla Cala 2015, Cachapoal Valley
description: In 2004, Alex and Carrie Vik set out with the ambition to make one of the world’s best wines. After a two-year scientific study, an architectural competition and 4,300 hectares of Andean wilderness, they now create some of the finest reds in Chile. A previous vintage of the Milla Cala was once featured in Decanter's Best 100 Wines in the World. This has lush flavours of strawberry, plum, vanilla and coffee. Decant for an hour before serving at 16 degrees with roast lamb or cellar for up to a decade.

name: Château Bertrand-Braneyre 2010, Haut-Médoc
description: This claret, from the sublime 2010 vintage, is made less than ten minutes' drive from 'First Growth' Châteaux Lafite and Mouton Rothschild, which both demand £400+ a bottle. Château Bertrand-Braneyre's 40-year-old vines see near-identical conditions, but its prices are minuscule in comparison. Dive into its depths of blackberry, blueberry, cinnamon and leather.

name: Thierry Delaunay 'Ceptembre' Sauvignon Blanc 2019, VdF
description: Thierry Delaunay, one of the leading wineries of the Touraine region, ages this deliciously dry Sauvignon Blanc on character-building lees. This results in a textured palate of citrus and fresh-cut grass.

name: Lanson Green Label Organic NV
description: Founded in 1760, Lanson is one of oldest and most respected houses in Champagne. This is its first ever entirely organic Champagne. Predominantly Pinot Noir, aged on lees for three years, it's pure and fresh with fine notes of citrus and berry fruits weaving through its complex mousse.

name: Eileen Hardy Chardonnay 2016, Australia
description: The Eileen Hardy Chardonnay is one of Australia's most iconic whites from one of the country's most historic cellars. Winning gold at the International Wine Challenge and 97 points from Australia's top wine critic, James Halliday, it's a toasted cocktail of ripe tropical fruit, snappy acidity and a buttery finish.

name: Larry Cherubino ‘Folklore’ Cabernet/Merlot 2018
description: The winemaking roots of the Cherubinos go back to the mid-1800s when their ancestors planted the first vines on the Frankland River. Nowadays, the family are still in the same spot. They're now one of West Australia’s most renowned producers and were named Best Value Winery in 2017 by the country's leading wine critic, James Halliday. Full bodied and velvety with notes of blackberry and bay leaf. Wonderful when paired with beef stew.

name: Simpsons Wine Estate Rosé 2019, England
description: This English rosé is made by Ruth and Charles Simpson. Until 2012, the couple only made wines at their acclaimed Southern French estate, Domaine Sainte Rose. But they returned home, driven by the UK's increasing winemaking potential. The couple now bring their French winemaking knowledge to the beautiful Kent countryside. This elegant pink is distinctly English, with grapefruit, lychee, and stone-fruit aromas. Its mouth-watering saline acidity makes it a perfect match for seafood with a squeeze of lemon.

name: Louis Pommery English Brut, Hampshire
description: After a space race between all of Champagne's biggest names to get here first, Pommery is the first house to launch English fizz. Teaming up with Hampshire-based Hattingley Valley, owned by the chairman of Wines of Great Britain, it's applied the same traditional method that it uses back home to craft a crisp, arrow-precise bouquet of waxy lemon, Granny Smith, freshly baked bread and honeycomb.

name: Chablis 'Vieilles Vignes' 2019 Domaine Michaut Frères
description: Whilst Chablis is always a treat, this old vine expression is seriously luxurious. Made by Domaine Michaut Frères which has been in family hands since the 1950s, it'smade from vines 40 years old at the very youngest. This lower yielding vines lead to a concentration of aromas and exceptional roundness in the mouth. Expect a very fresh, lively nose of flint, green apple, lemon and a hint of mushroom. These aromas are supported on the palate by mouth watering flavours of lime-flower, ripe lemon and acacia.

name: Definition Mâcon-Villages 2019
description: Mâcon-Villages wines are a step up in quality from much made elsewhere in Burgundy. A warmer climate and lower rainfall are perfect for the slow ripening of Chardonnay grapes. So you can expect ripe aromas of peach and apricot with the delicate minerality in the Definition.

name: Dominus 2016, Napa Valley
description: This is your rare chance to taste a 100 Parker Point wine. What's more, Lisa Perotti-Brown, who reviewed it, said "If I could give more than 100 for this one, I would". Made by Christian Moueix, one of the world's most respected, in-demand winemakers, this is a Napa Valley Bordeaux blend at its very best. Expect lashings of red cherries, almond, pomegranate and tobacco and silky tannins. Jeb Dunnuch and James Suckling both gave it 98 points.

name: Stag's Leap 'Hands of Time' Red Blend 2017, Napa Valley
description: Stag’s Leap shocked the world and beat the finest Bordeaux in the legendary Judgment of Paris in 1976. Ever since, theirs have become some of the most collected wines in the world. This Bordeaux blend really shows why they demanded the respect of those wine critics 34 years ago. Expect notes of dark fruit and chocolate truffle. Try this with pork tacos.

name: La Belle Angèle Pinot Noir 2019, VdF
description: La Belle Angèle Pinot Noir is as delicate as the artists' muse that it's named after. This light red wine shows intriguing aromas of morello cherry and spice, all balanced by a refreshing acidity. Pour a generous glass to accompany a charcuterie board.

name: Château de Santenay Saint-Aubin 2017
description: Although it's tucked between the two prestigious ‘Montrachet’ villages of Chassagne and Puligny, you can still find great value in Saint-Aubin. And it has its own fantastic reputation for fine, steely Chardonnay. This is made on a small 4-hectare vineyard there. It has notes of apricot, citrus and vanilla. Serve this lightly chilled with sea bass.

name: Mercurey 2017 Louis Violland, Burgundy
description: The Côte Chalonnaise was once referred to as the 'Région de Mercurey', which shows how important this famous village is. Aged for 8 months in oak barrels, this is a classical expression of Mercurey. A refined and luscious Pinot Noir, this has bright red berry flavours, hints of earthy minerality and fine-grained tannins.

name: Framingham Pinot Noir 2018, Marlborough
description: Marlborough Pinot Noir is world famous, but Framingham has made this style its own by using a portion of whole grape bunches. This is complex and labour-intensive process which involves fermenting the grapes with their stems. However, this extra effort translates to the wines complexity and plush texture. After 10 months in oak, this Pinot has notes of cranberry, cherry, plum and oak spice and silky tannins.

name: Cape Mentelle Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon 2018, Margaret River
description: Cape Mentelle is one of the 'Founding Five' wineries of Margaret River, one of Australia's finest wine regions. Its Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon is fresh and juicy, with creamy flavours of lemon and fresh herbs and a textured complexity, thanks to four months of lees ageing.

name: House of Arras 'Brut Elite Cuvée', Tasmania
description: House of Arras began in Tasmania as a project to create a sparkling wine on par with the finest Champagne. 25 years later, it's Australia's most-awarded sparkling-wine house. Their 'Brut Elite Cuvée' is a blend of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and Pinot Meunier. It's complex, structured and boasts a textured character, thanks to oak fermentation and lees ageing. Expect a zesty, persistent spritz and flavours of toast, almond, stone fruit and spiced apple. Wine Advocate's Lisa Perrotti-Brown gave it 91 Parker Points.

name: Laborie Blanc de Blancs 2014, South Africa
description: In 1691, the Taillefert family moved from France to South Africa and planted their first vines. Today, this estate produces exceptional fizz in a similar method to Champagne, Méthode Cap Classique. This vintage 100% Chardonnay wine was aged for 36 months. This means it’s gloriously complex and indulgent. Try its creamy notes of lime, hazelnut, toasted bread and cream with mussels and chips.

name: "Haut Cuvee" Pouilly Fuisse Rijckaert - F. Rouve, Burgundy
description: Florent Rouve is a winemaker on the up, and this is your chance to taste his expertise before prices inevitably rise. This is a full, rich and rounded white that shows exactly why Pouilly-Fuissé's the most prestigious region in the Mâconnais. Expect aromas of apricot, acacia flowers, citrus and honey, a long finish and an excellent minerality. Try this with a roast dinner.

name: Rupert & Rothschild 'Baroness Nadine' Chardonnay 2018, South Africa
description: This is a collaboration of two of the wine world's big family names, Rupert and Rothschild. It's a creamy, delicate Chardonnay, with flavours of lemon blossom, kiwi, grapefruit and nougat. Serve lightly chilled with salmon.

name: Creation Pinot Noir 2019, Walker Bay
description: You may expect that South Africa is too warm for the demanding Pinot Noir grape. But Jean-Claude & Carolyn Martin recognised the potential of the lofty Hemel-en-Aarde Ridge and planted vines 350 metres above sea level. Here they make the Creation Pinot Noir, which is a bright, polished wine with notes of red berries, vanilla and wood spice.

name: Nautilus Chardonnay 2019, Marlborough
description: At Nautilus, everything is done with sustainability in mind. The winery is part of the Sustainable Winegrowers New Zealand programme and has been owned by the same dedicated family for 25 years. In this Chardonnay, expect classic notes of nectarine and almond, with a creamy finish to come as it matures. Match this luxurious white with an equally creamy pasta.

name: Trapi del Bueno ‘Hand Made’ Chardonnay 2019, Osorno Valley
description: Before the millennium, planting vines in Chile’s volcanic Osorno Valley, in Patagonia, was unthinkable. But since then, daring winemakers have quickly made it one of Chile’s most exciting regions. Trapi del Bueno are a proud member of Chile’s pioneering Independent Winemakers Movement. You can really taste the fruits of their courage here. These grapes are so delicious, they have to ward off feasting pigs and pumas to create this bright, fresh Chardonnay. Try its hints of lemon and white peach with crab.

name: Sacred Hill 'Wine Thief' Chardonnay 2018, Hawkes Bay
description: This is a creamy, long, one-off wine made on one of Hawke's Bay's oldest Chardonnay vineyards. These grapes are usually reserved for Sacred Hill's most expensive Chardonnay. It's complex and deep, with notes of nectarine, mango, vanilla, cedar and brioche. Serve this lightly chilled with salmon.

name: Grant Burge 'Corryton Park' Cabernet 2018, Australia
description: Proving that Barossa Cabernet can be just as impressive as the Shiraz, this is all the cassis, spice and licorice that you'd want from an intense, velvety Cabernet Sauvignon. It's got the Grant Burge stamp of quality, meaning it tastes great now, and will also continue to age wonderfully if you want to store it for a few years.

name: Edgebaston Chardonnay 2019, Stellenbosch
description: With the belief that Stellenbosch is South Africa's finest winemaking region, the Finlayson family craft wines for the discerning collector and enthusiast alike. This is the 13th vintage of their Chardonnay, and you'll find a complex array of flavours. Expect notes of lime, sourdough and cinnamon. Sublime with shellfish.

name: Domaine Berthenet 'Les Bonneveaux' Montagny Premier Cru 2017
description: Domaine Berthenet want only the best. So they've picked Francois Berthenet, Guide Hachette's Winemaker of the Year 2020, as their winemaker. Needless to say, you can expect big things from this wine. It has complex and rich flavours of ripe fruits, pear, peach and hazelnuts. Enjoy lightly chilled with a strong cheese, like Gruyere or Gouda.

name: Bouchard Père & Fils Volnay Premier Cru 2017, Les Caillerets
description: Bouchard Père & Fils is one of the oldest estates in Burgundy. It's been going strong for nearly three centuries. The exceptional winemaking continues with Frédéric Weber. His care and patience has upheld the estate’s reputation for excellence for almost 20 years. And it really shows in this red Burgundy. It's made from Premier Cru vineyards in the famous 'Les Caillerets' area of Volnay. It's rich, complex and silky, with flavours of cherry, toasted oak and spice that's go exceptionally well with duck. Drink at room temperature now or age for another 7-10 years.

name: Castello Fonterutoli 'Philip' Cabernet 2016, Tuscany
description: The Mazzei family have made wine in Tuscany since 1435. Today, the estate boasts one of the most impressive cellars of the region. Winemaker Francesco Mazzei is a 24th generation family member. And the family brilliance really shines in this Cabernet Sauvignon. It's supple and silky, with flavours of black fruit, plum, dark chocolate, spice and cinnamon. Decant for an hour before serving at 14-16 degrees with grilled meat.

name: Château La Garde Pessac-Léognan Blanc 2019, Graves
description: Château La Garde has exceptional gravel soils. The winemaking team here claim that this is one of the finest areas for Bordeaux Blanc. Taste this and decide for yourself. This is a fresh, fruity and complex wine, with notes of citrus fruit, a mineral, chalky character and a long finish. At its best lightly chilled with asparagus risotto.

name: Remi Seguin Gevrey-Chambertin Premier Cru 2017
description: This is a complex, harmonious Pinot Noir made from premier cru vineyards in Gevrey-Chambertin. It's full-bodied and structured, with flavours of ripe black fruit, prune and cinnamon, refeshing acidity and ripe tannins. Serve at 16-17 degrees with lamb. Drink now or cellar for 5-10 years.

name: Joseph Drouhin Chassagne-Montrachet 2018
description: Chassagne-Montrachet is where you'll find some of the world's finest Chardonnay. This really lives up to the area's reputation. It's a generous and elegant wine, with notes of brioche, candied lemon, hazelnut and spice. Serve it lightly chilled with creamy chicken.

name: Château Caronne Ste-Gemme 2014 Magnum Haut-Médoc
description: Château Caronne Ste-Gemme is outstanding value for money and a Majestic classic. And now there's twice as much in the bottle. This is a rich, ripe and fruity claret, with notes of black berries, a smooth mouthfeel and a long, fresh finish. This magnum is ideal to wow your guests at a dinner party.

name: Radford Dale 'Freedom' Pinot Noir 2018/19, Elgin
description: Alex Dale left Burgundy for winemaking liberty just weeks after Nelson Mandela was elected president of South Africa - and that's when the 'Freedom' Pinot Noir began. This is fresh, perfumed and complex, with notes of strawberry, bramble fruit, oak and spice. Serve at room temperature with a hearty stew.

name: Olivier Leflaive 'Sous le Château' 2018, Saint-Romain
description: The winemaking team at Olivier Leflaive have forged great relationships with small, lesser-known grape growers. This means they get first dibs on some of the finest grapes in Burgundy. And it really shows in the estate's fantastic reputation. This is from Saint-Romain, the small, high-altitude appellation known for its mineral whites. Expect intense flavours of peach, citrus and spice. Serve at 12 degrees with pâté.

name: Olivier Leflaive Montagny 1er Cru 2018, Burgundy
description: The winemaking team at Olivier Leflaive have forged great relationships with small, lesser-known grape growers. This means they get first dibs on some of the finest grapes in Burgundy. And this is made from only premier cru vineyards. While young, this is fresh and appealing, with hints of citrus, pineapple, spice and hazelnut. It will develop a savoury complexity over the next five years. Best served at 12 degrees with hard cheeses.

name: Balfour Saignée 2018, Kent
description: The dynamic team at Balfour have applied the traditional Saignée technique - more commonly seen in Champagne and rarely found in rosé - to craft this unique wine. This involves extracting juice from a period of grape skin contact, rather than blending. The result? A structured and fragrant wine with herbaceous character and notes of thyme, fennel and rose. Enjoy with creamy chicken dishes or soft cheeses.

name: Hambledon Classic Cuvée
description: Hambledon is the UK's oldest commercial vineyard, and its head winemaker, Hervé Jestin, was cellarmaster at the Duval Leroy Champagne house for over 20 years. The Classic Cuvée, a blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir & Pinot Meunier, once famously beat Pol Roger, Veuve Clicquot and Taittinger in a blind tasting. It has notes of fresh bread, greengage, red plums and dessert apples and a precise acidity.

name: Cloudy Bay Chardonnay 2018/19 Marlborough
description: Cloudy Bay put New Zealand on the wine world map with its Sauvignon Blanc - and its Chardonnay is just as remarkable. Now on its 31st vintage, this savoury-yet-oaked white gently reveals layers of orchard fruit, peach, sweet spices and hazelnut.

name: Silver Ghost Sauvignon Blanc 2020 Central Valley
description: Just because it’s inexpensive, doesn’t mean it’s not great quality. This wine is actually made for us by one of the most prestigious winemaking families in Chile, the Arestis. They know how to get the very best peachy, citrussy flavours out of their grapes, while still keeping prices wallet-friendly. The Central Valley, where this wine is from, is cool but has loads of sunshine – so this is more like a ripe white Bordeaux than a full, tropically fruity Kiwi Sauvignon. The fact is, Chilean wines are just incredible value – a wine of this quality from another country could cost far more. In our book, that makes it the ideal party wine. It’s got a real citrussy zing and great tropical flavours. It’s incredibly refreshing, so your guests will be happy to drink it alone, or with finger foods. It’s hard working bottle which won’t disappoint.

name: Chablis Premier Cru 2019 Domaine L. Chatelain, Fourchaume
description: Fourchaume is one of the most acclaimed spots in Chablis. Exposed to the rising sun, vines here see bucketloads of grape plumping hours. Here, you'll find Chablis that's powerful and elegant, standing toe to toe with the finest Grand Crus. Domaine L. Chatelain's is a prime example. On the nose, it emits delicate hints of almond, butter and licorice. The palate is round and buttery with flavours of citrus, marmalade and toasted almond.

name: Aurore de Dauzac 2014
description: Château Dauzac takes its second wine very seriously. Grapes are carefully picked by hand from 30-year-old vines. You'll taste the resulting freshness in an elegant bouquet of red fruits and fine tannins. And it's almost half the price of its big brother!

name: Chateau Batailley 2014, Pauillac
description: Château Batailley is one of the oldest estates in Médoc. It was recognised in the prestigious 1855 classification of Bordeaux. Over the past few decades, there has been an even greater emphasis on quality of fruit – and it really shows in this bottle. This is an intense, typical Pauillac, with fresh notes of black fruit, smooth tannins and a long finish. Exceptional served at room temperature with lamb.

name: La Réserve de Léoville-Barton 2017 St-Julien
description: From the gravelly dunes of Léoville-Barton's vineyards, leading down to the bank of the Gironde, La Réserve is the second wine of this famous property. As an earlier drinking version of the grand vin, this is real value for money. With a classic 'left bank' nose of smoky blackberry fruit and pencil lead, the palate is rich and rounded with assertive tannins, ample fruit and well integrated oak. Serve with shoulder of lamb braised in wine. Drinking well at the moment, but will keep for another 5 or so years.

name: Nicolas Potel Bourgogne Pinot Noir 2019
description: As this bottle is labelled as ‘Bourgogne Pinot Noir’, it's a real bargain. Winemaker Brigitte Putzu is a Burgundy native. She has more than two decades of experience in creating fantastic red Burgundy in Nuits Saint Georges. And she's used her expertise to blend Pinot Noir grapes from across the region to create this. It's completely unoaked and this means it's all about the red-fruit flavour. We recommend trying it with roast pork.

name: Puligny-Montrachet 2017 Alain Chavy, Burgundy
description: Wine Advocate's Neal Martin said ''Alain Chavy has gone about making fine white Burgundy in recent years''. On the fringe of the Puligny village, in Burgundy's finest commune, it's aged this creamy white in its coldest underground cellar. This encourages slow ripening of flavour, and you'll taste the fruits of patience in a vibrant palate of apple, lemon pastry and toasty oak.

name: Cave des Vignerons de Buxy 'Les Millières' Montagny 1er Cru 2019
description: Deep in the Côte Chalonnaise, Montagny is an appellation with a difference. It dedicates itself entirely to the production of white wine – all Chardonnay. Two thirds of the vineyards have the prestigious Premier Cru classification – an extremely high proportion of high-quality vineyards. ‘Les Milliers’ is one of these Premier Crus, made by Caves de Buxy. They’re a name to note, known as an excellent, quality-conscious producer – and because they’re a cooperative with access to grapes from a variety of top-notch growers, it’s excellent value for money. It’s fermented and matured in stainless steel tanks to preserve the really pure, ripe fruit flavours. Peach. Pear. Honey. It’s a really delicious glassful, with a nicely rich texture that’s still fresh. Perfect with cold ham.

name: Cave de Lugny 'Les Pierres Blanches' Mâcon-Villages Blanc 2019
description: This is proof that ‘classic’ doesn’t have to mean boring or expensive. Cave de Lugny are a true specialist – they’re the biggest white Burgundy producer in the world, but they don’t rest on their laurels. Their mission is to make standout white Burgundy which is approachable, affordable – and delicious. Extraordinarily for the price, it’s made using grapes from thirty-year-old vines. Their age intensifies the flavours in the grapes – and they dial up this fruitiness by leaving it unoaked. It’s rested on its lees – spent yeast particles – which gives the texture a creaminess, and makes it even more aromatic. It’s fruity, juicy and refreshing – think citrus, green melon and a touch of honey, with a really lip-smacking finish. Alongside a chicken salad, it’ll please everyone.

name: Pouilly-Fuissé 2019 Domaine Cordier
description: From the Domaine Cordier estate with vines grown in Fuissé village and Vergisson, yields of 40 hectolitres per hectare and 15% new oak this is a modern, sumptuous interpretation of the full-bodied style of Pouilly Fuissé. Very forward with ripe tropical peach and a rich (but not over-powering) use of toasty oak make this a wonderfully full style of Fuissé. Length on the finish is long and harmonious. Slightly more upfront oak and fruit than some of Cordier's other wines, a creamy dish like stroganoff would complement a wine like this.

name: Chablis Elodie Duprés 2019
description: A series of difficult harvests have made it a real challenge to find Chablis which is everyday-affordable. Luckily, our buyers have the contacts and the know-how to bring you Chablis which is both great quality and great value. And this really is everything you could possibly want from a Chablis. White peaches. Lemon zest. Green apple. Taking a sip is like taking a bite of a Granny Smith. It’s intensely refreshing – so crisp and citrussy that you can’t help going back for a second glass. It’s the very best of unoaked Chardonnay, a real French classic. Nicely light and fresh, this would make a fantastic party wine. It’s also delicious with crispy, cheesy nibbles like gougères. That’s your canapés sorted.

name: Viré-Clessé Vieilles Vignes 2019 Christophe Cordier
description: Cordier's Viré-Clessé is made from 40-year-old vines from village of Viré, with grapes bought in from a mixture of winegrowers, some of which practise biodynamic viticulture. Using 300lt barrels, 10% of which are new, the lees are also stirred for added body. Elegant yet opulent, this wine combines fresh fruit on the palate with a minerally core, with added dimension provide by a slight toastiness. Langoustines, lobster and prawn dishes will match well with this wine, as will most fowl recipes.

name: Pouilly-Fumé 2018 Jean Vincent
description: Father-and-son team Jean and Vincent Chatelain are the 11th and 12th generations of an illustrious winemaking family, based in the heart of Pouilly-sur-Loire. Each parcel of their 30ha estate is vinified individually to maintain the character of each plot. An intensely aromatic wine, with a nose that centres on gooseberry fruit, enhanced by a smoky, flinty character. This mineral theme continues all the way to the long, steely finish. A mouthwatering, dry aperitif wine, also ideal with fresh shellfish dishes.

name: Le Volte dell' Ornellaia 2018
description: Ornellaia is an estate in demand. Their first wine is one of the most sought-after in Italy. ‘Le Volte’ is your chance to taste their expertise at a lower price, and it favours the adventurous. Here, Sangiovese is combined with Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. It's an exciting twist on the Super Tuscan blend. Aged for over 10 months in oak, it has great structure, fresh notes of red fruits and a balancing acidity that are sublime with venison.

name: Pouilly-Fumé 2018 de Ladoucette
description: Father-and-son team Jean and Vincent Chatelain are the 11th and 12th generations of an illustrious Loire wine making family. In fact, the family has been making Loire wines since 1630! This unrivalled level of expertise is translated into this beautiful Sauvignon Blanc that's intense, mineral and concentrated with white-fruit, gooseberry and flint flavours.

name: Pouilly-Fumé 'Les Griottes' 2019 Jean-Pierre Bailly
description: Grapes from up to 40 year old vines planted on the argilo limestone and kimmeridgian clay, more commonly associated with the Chablis region, imbues the wine with Pouilly-Fumé's classic richness, smokiness and complexity. Rich and smoky on the nose, with subtle limes, white florals and honeysuckle atop a palate of fresh, clean and vibrant acidity, citrus fruit flint, minerality and green peppers. A long and layered finish. Enjoy over the next three years with asparagus in hollandaise, Caesar salad or shellfish.

name: La Belle Angele Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: You’d be forgiven for overlooking Vins de Pays. In French wine, all the focus is given to AOCs – controlled areas or ‘appellations’ where the type, style and quality of wine is rigorously controlled. It means AOC wines command the highest prices – and Vins de Pays or ‘IGP’ wines are often the leftovers. But that’s not true everywhere. Certain areas like Gascony and the Aude have built their reputation on the exceptional quality of their Vins de Pays – and this wine is a blend of Sauvignon Blanc from both areas. It’s a demonstration of everything that’s great about French Sauvignon. Crisp and refreshing, bursting with flavours of citrus, apple and gooseberry. And because it’s Vins de Pays, it’s a real steal. Delicious with any tangy, dairy-based dishes.

name: Domaine de la Metairie DAlon Chardonnay Le Village 2016
description: This is a fabulous Chardonnay from a small property that makes its wine with as much care as top Burgundies. Barrel fermentation results in complex flavours of lemon, freshly baked pastry and hazelnut.

name: Rustenberg 'Five Solders' Chardonnay 2017, Stellenbosch
description: This complex, long, creamy Chardonnay is made by Murray Barlow. He's the only person to win Diners Club's Young Winemaker of the Year award twice. And we expect that there's plenty more awards to come for him. This has flavours of citrus, melon, peach and oak. Serve lightly chilled and pair with buttery chicken.

name: Veuve Clicquot Brut, Champagne
description: 1800s France wasn’t very female-friendly. Women weren’t even allowed to have bank accounts – making even more extraordinary that twenty-something Madame Clicquot not only kept her late husband’s business, but turned it into the must-have Champagne of the Russian court. The key to her success? Riddling. She invented A-frame racks to hold bottles at an angle. Rotated slowly for eight weeks, they let the sediment settle in the neck of the bottle, meaning the Champagne was clearer, higher quality, and less likely to explode in transit. It was revolutionary. Today, the Clicquot name is still synonymous with excellence. This Brut NV is aged for twice the time legally required, giving it a really biscuity, toasty flavour. Rich. Ripe. Citrussy. It’s fresh but decadent. Perfect with canapés.

name: Devaux 'Augusta' Brut, Champagne
description: The Augusta is made by the same house that bought you Oeil de Perdrix. This Pinot Noir-dominated Champagne is complex and powerful, with flavours of brioche, yellow plum and apricot. It's a full-flavoured fizz that really is excellent value for money. Drink with fish and chips.

name: Veuve Clicquot Champagne Magnum
description: In 19th century France, women weren’t even allowed to have bank accounts. So it's extraordinary that twenty-something Madame Clicquot turned her late husband’s business into the must-have Champagne of the Russian court. It really shows in every drop of this magnum. It's a superb marriage of freshness and power, with rich fruit flavours and a mouthfilling mousse that lingers delightfully. Pair with mature cheeses.

name: Veuve Clicquot Demi-Sec Champagne
description: Demi-Sec is a style of Champagne that's a bit sweeter than usual. And Veuve Clicquot haven't neglected any of the flavour. First produced for the Russian court in the 19th century, this Champagne shows rich, candied fruits, toast and baked bread. It has a creamy mousse, a decadent palate and ends in a deliciously fresh finish. It's a perfect dessert wine, so serve it with peach pudding.

name: Taittinger Comtes de Champagne 2007 Champagne
description: Produced exclusively from top quality Chardonnay grapes grown in 'Grand Cru' rated vineyards. One of the greatest Blanc de Blancs champagnes, Comtes was first produced in 1952. A Champagne with the potential to age for decades and reward the more patient hedonist.

name: Heidsieck and Co. Monopole 'Bronze Top' Brut Champagne
description: Heidsieck & Co. Monopole is one of Champagne's oldest houses and has long been a favourite of the famous and royal. It was a particular favourite of the Russian Tsar Nicholas II, who used to have two whole trains filled with the Champagne delivered to him each year. The Bronze Top continues this heritage of excellence. Expect a fine stream of bubbles, with citrus and brioche flavours. Divine with oysters.

name: Louis Roederer 2013 Champagne
description: Louis Roederer's 2013 vintage was one of the best on record. After a cool, late spring, the summer months truly dazzled, filling grapes with delicious juice. It was a no-brainer for them to make their signature vintage Champagne that year. It's a blend of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, showing all of the elegant notes of citrus, flowers, pears and spice.

name: Nicolas Feuillatte 2010 Champagne
description: Nicolas Feuillatte was a man about town. He made a fortune in instant coffee and was a fixture of the New York jet set – celebrity connections which made his Champagne instantly popular. Today, Nicolas Feuillatte is far and away the most popular Champagne label in France – and the third most popular globally. That Jackie Kennedy and Lauren Bacall used to drink it in Nicolas’ plush Fifth Avenue apartment did nothing to hurt its popularity. Nor that it’s delicious and incredibly well-made. And it’s one of kind in Champagne – the only co-operative in the region, a collective of nearly 5000 growers. This vintage 2010 is a real cut above. It’s incredibly rich and briochey, ripely fruity with flavours of apricot, yellow fruit and raspberry. Delicious with aged comté – or fish and chips.

name: Graham Beck Brut Rosé NV
description: It's made with the same laborious method as Champagne. But because it's from South Africa, you pay a fraction of the price. Decanter called it "great value". You'll see why in its elegant creamy red-fruit aromas, rich mousse and brioche-laden finish.

name: Louis Roederer Quartet NV California
description: From Champagne house Louis Roederer's excellent Californian operation in the Anderson Valley. Reserve vintage wines are added to the Quartet, making it a multi-vintage cuvée in the traditional Roederer style. Crisp and elegant with complex pear, spice and hazelnut flavours. Fresh and lightly fruity with great finesse and depth of flavour. A brilliant apéritif, or try alongside lobster.

name: Waimea Estates Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Nelson
description: As a child, winemaker Hamish would sit on an upturned bucket in his grandpa’s garden shed, watching him record all his winemaking notes in a set of cherished, tattered notebooks. With that background, there was only one profession Hamish could pursue. He landed at Waimea, on the edge of the Tasman Sea – a place regularly credited with being New Zealand’s sunniest spot. It’s the perfect place to grow Sauvignon Blanc. Sheltered. Coastal. Sunny. There couldn’t be more ideal conditions to draw all those tropical, fruity flavours out of the grapes. The result is a vibrantly fruity Kiwi Sauvingon, crisply zingy – really mouth-watering. There’s nothing better with yoghurt-marinated chicken or seafood.

name: Villa Maria 'Private Bin' Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Villa Maria is the most awarded winery in New Zealand. They craft benchmark Sauvignons and people recognise their name the world-over. But like another world-renowned brand we could mention, Villa Maria actually started life in a garage. To be precise: in George Fistonich’s garage in Mangere, an Auckland suburb. Sir George – and his winery – have come an awfully long way since then. The Private Bin range is the only introduction you need to Kiwi Sauvignon. It’s all about the fruit. Lip-smacking, tropical, zesty fruit. The glass bursts with gooseberries, lemongrass and lime. Villa Maria say they made this as the perfect aperitif, but we think it’s rather fine with a dressed crab salad. Mix and match – do both.

name: Oyster Bay Chardonnay 2019 Marlborough
description: Oyster Bay is Marlborough's, if not New Zealand's, most iconic producer. But it's not just a star with Sauvignon Blanc. Part oak-aged, its elegant Chardonnay is brilliant value and bursting creamy lemon and peach flavours.

name: The Ned Noble Sauvignon Blanc 2018 Half Bottle
description: Botrytised grapes have been specially selected from amongst the vines of the Waihopai River vineyard to make this wine. The fermentation process takes a lengthy six months and extracts huge flavours from the shrivelled berries. Intensely sweet, yet balanced with a mixture of crisp citrus and fresh melon flavours, and as such, never cloys. Lingers long on the palate. An amazing match for gorgonzola or rich pâtés.

name: Alamos Lujan De Cuyo Cabernet 2019
description: The grapes for Alamos are grown at an altitude of between 3,000 and 5,000 feet where the temperature variation allows the grapes to grow slowly developing more flavour. Hand harvesting and sorting ensure only the best grapes are used to make the wine. There is lots of blackcurrant and bramble fruit flavours in the concentrated and silky wine. The purity of fruit is enhanced by a slight woody note on the finish. This will be great with grilled meats flavoured with herbs.

name: Goldwater Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Wairau Valley
description: Goldwater is one of the oldest wineries in New Zealand, founded back in 1978. But that’s not all that makes them pioneers. It started out on remote Waiheke Island, just off Auckland. But in the early 1990s they headed south to what is now Sauvignon heartland, Wairau Valley. Since then, Goldwater has become a byword for top-drawer, seriously aromatic Sauvignon Blanc. With Alastair Maling MW, one of the most awarded winemakers in New Zealand, at the helm, it could never be anything less. Alastair leaves the wine on its skins for a short period which helps to amp up its aromatics. Think passionfruit, gooseberry and elderflower. It’s a real riot of intense tropical flavours. But it’s also intensely fresh – a deliciously crisp glassful, absolutely made for grilled fish with spicy mango salsa.

name: Cloudy Bay Pinot Noir 2018 Marlborough
description: Cloudy Bay crafts some of New Zealand's finest wine. The cool-climate Marlborough is almost too generous to the demanding Pinot Noir grape. Here, the trailblazing winery has created this silk-smooth 92 Parker Point 2017 vintage that's loaded with raspberry, strawberry and cola. A deliciously sweet hint of spice lingers on the finish.

name: Escarpment Pinot Noir 2017, Martinborough
description: Larry McKenna has been celebrated in the New Zealand Wine Hall of Fame and is a Fellow of New Zealand Wine Growers. He's a specialist in Pinot Noir and is even known affectionately as Larry McPinot by fans of his fantastic reds. Taste what all the fuss is about in this perfumed, delicate wine, with elegantly textured tannins and supple red and black fruits. It will cellar for up to 10 years but also go well now with crispy pork belly.

name: Catena Alta Chardonnay 2018, Mendoza
description: Catena Zapata won Drinks International Magazine's 'World's Most Admired Wine Brand 2020'. Their expertise is in high-altitude winemaking. And this is made on vineyards towering up to 4,757 metres above sea level. Chardonnay grapes benefit from a long ripening period at this height. So this is understandably delicious. It has notes of pear, peach, citrus and jasmine. Serve this lightly chilled with sushi.

name: Ten Minutes By Tractor Estate Chardonnay 2015 Mornington Peninsula
description: Mornington Peninsula is a sub-region of Victoria which, in recent years, has been identified as arguably the best area in Australia for cultivating the classic Burgundian varieties. From the estate's Main Ridge vineyard, this Chardonnay is whole-bunch pressed and fermented with wild yeasts in French oak barriques.

name: Au Bon Climat Pinot Noir 'Isabelle' 2016 California
description: Rather than making this wine from a single vineyard's grapes as one might expect from Au Bon Climat, Isabelle is created by blending selected barrels from several sites, including Bien Nacido, Sanford & Benedict, Talley Rincon and Mount Carmel. A superbly structured, deep, complex and involving Pinot, and a great benchmarker of the vintage. An expressive floral nose leads into a palate of ripe dark cherries, plums, baking spices and french oak with a lengthy fruit driven finish. There is real power, precision and elegance here. Delicious and layered enough to enjoy on its own, this would also be a fine pairing for lighter meat dishes or game.

name: Klein Constantia Sauvignon Blanc 2019 Constantia
description: Klein Constantia dates back to 1685. It without a doubt produces some of South Africa's finest wines. It has a rich history and was even referenced in novels by Charles Darwin and Jane Austen. In the cool-climate foothills of the Constantiaberg mountain, Sauvignon Blanc grapes thrive. You can really taste the benefits in this complex, textured wine. Expect notes of citrus and stone fruits that are best with grilled seafood.

name: Boekenhoutskloof Cabernet Sauvignon 2017, Stellenbosch
description: Boekenhoutskloof and winemaker Marc Kent are behind one of our classic fine wines, The Chocolate Block. And they've smashed the target again with this Cabernet Sauvignon. This is a smooth, textured wine, with notes of black fruit, graphite, sweet spice and cocoa. Decant for an hour and serve at room temperature with meatballs.

name: Montes Single Vineyard Chardonnay 2018
description: The grapes use to produce this wine come from the cool climate Casablanca Valley which produces low yields of exceptionally healthy grapes with an abundance of fruit flavours. Malolactic fermentation adds and oak ageing adds buttery complexity. Fresh citrus flavours are augmented by a hint of asparagus typical of the Casablanca Valley are layered over toast, butter and vanilla notes. This will be a fine accompaniment for a roast chicken.

name: Majestic Loves Sauvignon 2020, Chile
description: If you like your Sauvignon fruitier than the French stuff and crisper than Kiwi, opt for Chile. This is light, refreshing and alive with zesty notes of green apple and citrus.

name: Montes Single Vineyard Chardonnay 2018
description: The grapes use to produce this wine come from the cool climate Casablanca Valley which produces low yields of exceptionally healthy grapes with an abundance of fruit flavours. Malolactic fermentation adds and oak ageing adds buttery complexity. Fresh citrus flavours are augmented by a hint of asparagus typical of the Casablanca Valley are layered over toast, butter and vanilla notes. This will be a fine accompaniment for a roast chicken.

name: Majestic Loves Sauvignon 2020, Chile
description: If you like your Sauvignon fruitier than the French stuff and crisper than Kiwi, opt for Chile. This is light, refreshing and alive with zesty notes of green apple and citrus.

name: Domaine Rolet Arbois Chardonnay 2016, Jura
description: Arbois is the most important region in Jura. In the foothills of the Alps, the harsh winters may put off many winemakers, but not Domaine Rolet. This estate handpicks its grapes from 30-year-old vines and ages this Chardonnay in oak. Expect notes of rich citrus, white flowers and a hint of oak influence in the supple texture and delicate nuttiness.

name: Cuvée Pierre Brun 2018 Domaine des Tourelles, Lebanon
description: When it comes to Lebanese wines it's not only Château Musar that's worth collecting. Founded in 1868, Tourelles is Lebanon's oldest winery. It's now run by Faouzi Issa, who earned his stripes working in Côte Rôtie and at iconic Château Margaux. This experience is reaping rewards. A blend of Cabernet, Syrah, Cinsault and Carignan, this has fine aromatic morello cherry and spice aromas, a dark fruit core with layered earthy spice, and balanced tannins.

name: Devil's Creek 'Gold Reserve' Pinot Noir 2019, Central Otago
description: The Devil's Creek River used to be known for its gold. The precious metal has long dried up, but you can still discover delicious Pinot Noir in Central Otago. It's known as the home of New Zealand's finest Pinot Noir, and for good reason. This is bright, lively and bursting with fruit. The elegant tannins make it a great pairing for pork.

name: Puligny-Montrachet 1er Cru 'Les Champs Gains' 2017 Domaine Alain Chavy, Burgundy
description: ''The 2014 Puligny Montrachet 1er Cru Champ Gains comes from two parcels on shallow 20-centimeter stony soil. It is tighter on the nose than the Puligny-Charmes when I tasted it from barrel and I suspect it will require longer bottle age. The palate is elegant on the entry, that leitmotif of bitter lemon coming through, good density with a brisk finish that will hopefully develop more persistency as it matures in bottle. There is elegance here, but it will need time to show.'' Neal Martin - The Wine Advocate

name: Devil's Creek 'Gold Reserve' Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Since we introduced it to the UK, Devil’s Creek Sauvignon Blanc has become a firm customer favourite. Now, if you can believe it, we’ve gone one better. Winemaker Michele Pedron is a Sauvignon specialist. He’s worked in top Sauvignon regions around the world – from Sancerre, to Chile’s Maule Valley. With this Gold Reserve Michele’s drawing out the subtle, often over-looked differences between Marlborough’s sub-regions. This is from Wairau, where the wines are richer and fruiter. No wonder this wine is so tropical and full bodied, bursting with gooseberry, guava and elderflower flavours. Crisp and refreshing – it’s a fantastic wine for sharing over some Thai food.

name: Bread and Butter Pinot Noir 2019
description: Bread & Butter is that thing that you can count on. It’s dependable, comforting – you know what to expect. That’s exactly what you’ll get with this wine, a textbook Californian Pinot Noir from the same winery which brought you the sell-out Bread & Butter Chardonnay. Their vineyards are ideally located for classic varieties like Pinot. It’s sun-drenched, but cooled by the daily morning mists which roll in off the Pacific. It’s exactly the sort of position that pernickety Pinot thrives in. This deliciously silky wine proves our point. It’s all about red fruit. Cherries. Raspberries. Cranberries. It’s seriously juicy and seriously delicious, with a nice fullness which is classic California. Oak ageing gives it a touch of cedary spice, and woody herb notes. Perfect with roast mushrooms.

name: Eight Thousand Lakes Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Washington State
description: The vines behind this revitalising Sauvignon Blanc are protected from rain by the foothills of the Cascade mountain range. This means a long ripening season - and, as a result, plenty of flavour. This is a remarkably fresh wine, with notes of citrus, white peach and nettle. Partner with baked camembert.

name: Q Cabernet Sauvignon, Alexander Valley
description: Fed up of astronomical Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon prices? Head to Alexander Valley. It's one of the most important regions for the grape in America. Aged in French oak for 12 months, packed with intense hints of cassis, summer-pudding fruit, cedar and roasted demerara sugar, Q Cabernet is a prime example of this star-studded spot.

name: Yalumba Cigar Cabernet Sauvignon 2016, Coonawarra
description: Yalumba The Cigar Coonawarra Cabernet Australia has a full nose with savoury blackcurrant fruit and on the palate there are layers of plum and cherry fruit and a lengthy savoury textured finish. The palate surprises as it is not the brute the nose suggests. It is a medium bodied wine with deep fruit flavours of dark cherries and blackberries. The tannins are silky smooth and act in harmony with the fruit for a lingering finish.

name: Yellow Tail Chardonnay 2020
description: Expect a lush palate of peach and citrus delivered through a creamy mouthfeel that's polished with a dusting of vanilla and spice. It's the perfect partner for a comforting roast chicken dinner.

name: Three Vintners Cabernet Sauvignon 2018
description: Three Vintners is a celebration of three generations of family winemakers, boasting the best of the secret that's been passed from grandfather to father to son. It's luscious, bold and chock-full of black fruits, mocha and sweet spice.

name: Te Mata Estate 'Cape Crest' Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Hawke's Bay
description: This Sauvignon Blanc, Semillion and Sauvingon Gris blend is left in contact with its skins, fermented in oak barrels and stirred on its lees for 9 months. All this effort creates a Graves-style Sauvignon Blanc that's as textured and rich as they come while maintaining a refreshing finish. Expect complex hints of goosberry, grapefruit, oak and mango from this celebrated white.

name: Emma Marris Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Emma Marris is the daughter of Brent Marris, the man behind our best-selling Sauvignon Blanc, The Ned. But she's carving her own path as a winemaker. After spending her life surrounded by wine, she applies university-trained, ground breaking techniques to this, a Marlborough Sauvignon with a difference. Expect bold, inviting notes of green apple, papaya, lime and lemongrass. Enjoy with a green salad.

name: Rustenberg Stellenbosch Cabernet Sauvignon 2019
description: Murray Barlow is one of South Africa's most exciting young winemakers and has won Diners Club's Young Winemaker of the Year title an unrivalled twice. He's shown why once again in this well-structured Cabernet. Expect dark berries, tobacco and a lengthy finish.

name: Casillero del Diablo Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Chile
description: More than 100 years ago, a winemaker was losing barrels of wine. He couldn’t catch the thieves – so instead, he put about a story that the Devil lived in his cellars. The story quickly became legend – many people claimed to have seen the Devil with their own eyes – and the winemaker was satisfied. No thieves dared to enter the ‘Casillero del Diablo’, so they never touched his wines again. But the story hasn’t deterred British drinkers – this Cabernet is a national favourite. It’s quintessentially Chilean, big, bold and fruity. It’s packed with flavours of cherries and plums, with a hint of vanilla spice. Silky textured, it’s a fantastic option with roasted red meats.

name: Stonier Pinot Noir 2018, Mornington Peninsula
description: Mornington Peninsula has long been Australia's best spot for top-shelf Pinot Noir - and it's tough to secure the best for less than £25. Not now! Taste the cool-climate excellence in structured notes of red fruits and spice and a luscious finish.

name: Domaine de la Pinte Crémant du Jura
description: An hour's drive from Burgundy is Jura, a picturesque region with a continental climate. Here, Domaine de la Pinte hand-picks Chardonnay and Savagnin (not to be confused with Sauvignon) grapes from organic vines to make this balanced, complex Crémant. Adding no sugar at the disgorgement stage means the fruit reigns supreme.

name: Te Mata Estate 'Awatea' Cabernets/Merlot 2017, Hawke's Bay
description: First made in 1982, the Awatea is still the benchmark for great-value Hawke's Bay Cabernet/Merlot blends. Its intense hints of red and black fruit, plum and espresso, all chased by a heady finish and framed by fine tannins, scored 92 Wine Enthusiast points.

name: Bouvet Saphir Saumur 2018, Loire Valley
description: Love vintage Champagne, but not the price tag? Then you need to try Bouvet’s Saphir vintage crémant. Crémant is the savvy drinker’s first choice alternative to Champagne. It’s made using the same ‘traditional method’, the lengthy and expensive method that makes Champagne so pricey. Crémant de Loire is usually just Chenin Blanc. But this Saphir vintage includes a portion of Chardonnay which gives it more of that biscuity, briochey character. This makes it a must-try alternative to Champagne. They even age it for eighteen months which dials up those savoury, bready flavours. Peachy, honeyed and nutty, this is really classy fizz – and an absolute steal for the quality. Delicious with white fish in a buttery sauce.

name: Definition Sancerre Blanc 2019, Loire Valley
description: To capture the definitive elements of Sancerre, we went to the best-selling name in the region. As you'd expect from 25-year-old vines in Jurassic limestone soils, the Definition Sancerre is dry and complex with flavours of citrus, green apple and white flowers and a smooth minerality.

name: Domaine Bernard Michaut 'Beauroy' Chablis Premier Cru 2019
description: Beauroy is a Premier Cru that lies on the upper slopes of Chablis. This is a prime area for growing exceptional Chardonnay, which benefits from warming sunlight and exceptional soil quality. And it tastes even better when it's aged in oak. Expect golden hints of almond, vanilla and a long finish. If you can wait, this will taste even more complex in a decade. If you can't, drink a glass with moules frites.

name: Montagny 1er Cru 'Vignes Derrières' 2019 Domaine Pierres Sauvages, Burgundy
