description: Domaine Pierres Sauvages is a tiny, 3-hectare, family owned vineyard, and this is boutique white Burgundy at its best. Aged in oak for up to 8 months, this is refined, creamy and smooth Chardonnay with flavours of peach, apple, citrus and sweet spice. Pair it with poultry, cheese or seafood.

name: Bourgogne Côte dOr Chardonnay 2018 Edouard Delaunay
description: This is made by Edouard Delaunay, a Burgundy estate that was the choice of The Orient Express in the 19th century and restored to its former glory in 2017. It's made from grapes mainly from grapes from Auxey-Duresses and Meursault. Expect fresh aromas of peach and lemon and a subtle hint of hazelnut, thanks to some oak ageing and lees influence.

name: Edouard Delaunay 'Septembre' Pinot Noir 2018, Burgundy
description: One half of the Abbotts & Delaunay powerhouse, Laurent Delaunay, has returned to Burgundy to make modern-day wines and resurrect his family estate. The Septembre Pinot Noir boasts all the elegance and subtlety that you'd expect from the best expressions of Pinot Noir alongside a balance and freshness of fruit. Its hints of red and black cherry are chased by a full, fresh finish.

name: Talbott 'Kali Hart' Pinot Noir 2017, Monterey
description: Talbott Vineyards put the prestigious Santa Lucia Highlands on the wine world map with its excellent cool-climate wines. This Pinot Noir is complex, bright and has notes of cherry, cola and vanilla that are lined by a balancing minerality Wine Advocate's Erin Brooks gave it 92 Parker Points.

name: Viré-Clessé 'Haute Cuvée' 2019 Rijckaert F. Rouve, Burgundy
description: Florent Rouve studied under Jean Rijckaert, one of Burgundy's star winemakers, for years. He now manages Rijckaert's entire estate. To make 'Haute Cuvée', he picks grapes by hand, only the very finest make the cut, and he presses them slowly. He then ages the wine for up to 15 months in small oak barrels with minimal intervention. The result is a crisp, fresh Chardonnay with notes of citrus fruit and vanilla balanced by a delicate minerality.

name: Tabalí Maray Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Valle Del Limarí
description: Pioneering Chilean producer Tabalí crafts this Sauvignon Blanc in the Limarí Valley which sits at one of Chile's most northerly points. The region's cool climate and sea breezes offer grapes a long growing season that results into this fruit-forward white with fresh citrus notes and crisp acidity.

name: Château la Garde 2012, Pessac Léognan
description: Château la Garde sits on one of Pessac Léognan's finest spots, and its wine is structured, concentrated and ages beautifully. This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot is aged in oak for up to 14 months. Its aromas of ripe fruit, liquorice and smoke complement its rich, silky palate. Robert Parker called this 2012 vintage "a very good offering".

name: Firepeak Chardonnay 2018, Edna Valley
description: Just 5.4 miles from the Pacific Ocean, Edna Valley is one of the coolest growing regions in California. So it's full of complex, bright Chardonnay like the Firepeak. Barrel fermented and aged on its character-building lees for nine months, it's smooth and balanced, with notes of pear, lemon, pineapple and vanilla.

name: Casillero Del Diablo Chardonnay 2019
description: The Casillero del Diablo Chardonnay comes from vineyards located just a few kilometers from the Pacific Ocean. Sea breezes moderate the temperatures in the vineyard and result in remarkably fresh wines. With floral scents and notes of pineapple, citrus and peach this is a wonderful pairing with poultry.

name: Château Tour du Haut-Moulin 2013, Haut Médoc
description: Tour du Haut Moulin is situated in Cussac Fort, between Margaux and St-Julien, in the heart of Haut-Médoc. It has been owned by the Poitou family since 1870 and is now in the 6th generation of family ownership. It has 10 hectares of 25-year-old vines, and this is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot and Petit Verdot. With mellow bramble fruit, notes of cedar spice and firm tannins, it's a benchmark Left Bank red.

name: Definition Chablis 2019
description: The brief was to find a wine that went amazingly with smoked salmon, as any great Chablis should. That meant a creamy, lively, savoury, rich wine - not too many boxes to tick then. Our Chablis, a classic example of this famous wine. Find crisp citrus fruits, a hint of white flowers and a touch of oatmeal. The Majestic Wine Definition range captures the quintessential qualities of the world's greatest wine styles, with a little help from some of the world's greatest winemakers

name: BenMarco Plata Chardonnay 2020, Uco Valley
description: This is made under the watchful eye of Susana Balbo. She was the first woman to graduate with a winemaking degree in Argentina and was president of Wines of Argentina three times. So, as you'd imagine, it's delicious. It's fresh and aromatic, with notes of citrus, vanilla and white flowers. Excellent with Thai foods.

name: Laurent-Perrier Rosé Champagne & 2x Champagne Glasses
description: The elegant 16th-century bottle shape may give it away. But this is one of the finest pink Champagnes going. It's made purely from the demanding Pinot Noir grape and has divine notes of raspberry, redcurrant and strawberry.

name: Château Begadanet 2015, Haut-Médoc
description: Château Begadanet is a single family-owned vineyard in the village of Begadan, which is classified as a Cru Bourgeois area. The family makes this Merlot-dominated blend from vines with an average age of 35-years-old. With an excellent structure, this is a classical style of claret with notes of briar fruit and cedar.

name: Louis Roederer 'Cristal' Champagne 2012
description: Cristal Champagne was first designed upon request of Tsar Alexander II. Fearful of an assassination attempt, he requested that his Champagne was served in clear bottle. And an icon was born. This is made from low-yielding Pinot Noir and Chardonnay vines and aged for six years on texture-building lees. It's complex, lively and powerful, with a concentrated, long finish and great balance. Serve with buttery chicken. William Kelley gave it a 97+ Parker Point score.

name: J Vineyards Russian River Valley Chardonnay 2018
description: This sun-drenched area in Sonoma County is associated with elegant and aromatic Chardonnay's. In this fruit-forward wine from J Vineyards, expect notes of apple, ginger and blosson. Try with pork loin.

name: Joseph Drouhin Montagny Premier Cru 2018
description: This exceptional white Burgundy is made on Premier Cru Montagny vineyards by the fourth generation of the Drouhin family. It's rounded and poised with notes of golden apple, citrus and almond. Pair this with white fish or soft, creamy cheeses.

name: Taittinger Comtes de Champagne 2008
description: Only made in Taittinger's best vintages, the 2008 vintage of this prestigious Taittinger collection is elegant and irresistible. Budding collectors, take note. This is also an excellent cellaring wine. In this remarkable champagne, you'll find notes of honeysuckle and nuts. Enjoy on its own or with dessert.

name: Balfour Les Sixes 2014, Kent
description: This innovative sparkling English wine hails from the picturesque rolling hills of Hush Heath in Kent. It's made from Champagne grape varieties including Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and lesser-known Arbane. It's spent five years ageing on lees to develop its signature creaminess and structure. Expect an indulgent palate of shortbread, citrus and almond aromas. A superb apéritif.

name: Château Caronne Ste-Gemme 2014 Haut-Médoc
description: When the neighbours’ wines go for £50+ a bottle you know you’re onto a good thing. Caronne St-Gemme is only 500 yards from St Julian, next door to Château Gruaud Larose and Château Lascombes – and itself produces classed-growth quality fruit. That makes it not just one of the most reliable Cru Bourgeois – it’s also one of the best value. It’s classic Haut-Medoc, predominantly Cabernet Sauvignon which means it has a firm structure and lots of tannin – so it’s great with protein, and can age fantastically. It’s packed with really rich, dark fruit flavours. Plum. Blackberry. Classic Cabernet cassis. Delicious with roast red meat.

name: Castelnau de Suduiraut 2013 Sauternes Half Bottle
description: This is the second wine of Château Suduiraut, a premier Cru Classé Sauternes whose vines border those of Château d'Yquem. Crafted after stringent grape selection, this is a well-made and elegant wine. With seductive aromas of fragrant orange blossom, spices and fudge. The smooth initial taste develops into soft sweetness on the palate backed by the delicate aromas that are found on the nose. Savour these great value half bottles as a partner to traditional smooth duck liver terrine or alternatively just enjoy it on its own.

name: Caves des Vignerons de Buxy 'Vieilles Vignes' Montagny 2018
description: Montagny is an appellation in the Côte Chalonnaise. It’s a real enclave of quality, specialising in Chardonnay. In fact, it’s the only grape that’s grown in the whole region – so Montagny-based Cave de Buxy really know their Chardonnay inside-out. Montagny’s limestone soils mean the wines are fresh, with a fuller body than other Chalonnaise wines. The fruit flavours are really pronounced – a factor amped up by the fact that it’s ‘Vielles Vignes’. It’s made using grapes from thirty-year-old vines. Because they’re old, they only yield small amounts of grapes – so all the flavour is exceptionally concentrated. Think ripe apple, citrus and white blossom. The ripeness is balanced by serious crispness, and it’s left unoaked so the fruit flavours can really shine through. Perfect with chicken.

name: Mâcon-Fuissé Vieilles Vignes 2019 Christophe Cordier
description: Taken from vineyards in Macon-Vergisson, La Roche or Saint-Sera (from declassified Pouilly-Fuisse fruit), this wine is a great-value alternative to some of the more expensive Cordier wines, made from the 'Christophe Cordier' negociant arm. A medley of ripe apple and zesty lemon fruit characters creates great depth and intensity, with some body coming from careful use of oak. Slight mineral in the form of slate is detectable on the palate. Excellent as an apéritif or with any kind of salad or white meat, this wine is best enjoyed over the next couple of years.

name: Definition Chardonnay 2018, Limoux
description: This was one of the first wines we added to our Definition range. It’s a deeply luxurious oaked Chardonnay from Limoux – a region that’s a real insider secret for all but the most ardent Chardonnay lovers. It’s in the Langudoc, but it’s higher and cooler than the rest of the region – which means the wines are more concentrated, more aromatic and (we think) more delicious. Chardonnay thrives here. So, it was a real no-brainer to source a wine we called the ‘definition’ of Chardonnay from here. Pineapple. Peaches. Vanilla. It’s a wonderfully ripe, deliciously aromatic wine with a lovely creamy, full texture. A citrussy zing which balances the fullness of the wine. It’s delicious with anything cream or butter based, like Béarnaise sauce.

name: Wolf Blass Yellow Label Chardonnay 2020, South Australia
description: Yellow Label is the original series from Wolf Blass, one of Australia's most awarded wineries. Founded in the Barossa Valley, grapes are now carefully sourced from particularly ripe parcels of fruit across South Australia and matured in oak. A rich, honeyed, vanilla-flavoured Chardonnay, showing smooth, tropical fruit flavours on the creamy palate. Try it with Chinese-style fish dishes or more traditional roast chicken.

name: Laurent-Perrier La Cuvée, Champagne
description: Marie-Louise de Nonancourt knew what it took to run a Champagne house with the potential to become a household name. She steered the house through the Second World War until the return of her son, Bernard, from fighting with the French Resistance. No hero worship here, she insisted he do every job his future employees would have to. As a result, Bernard de Nonancourt was the only head of a Champagne house to have worked as a vines labourer, cellar and office worker and salesman. It gave him a complete grounding in the business which he fermented into the household name Laurent Perrier is today. The signature fresh, light, citrussy style is his invention. It’s appley and peachy. Of course, it’s a deliciously indulgent aperitif – especially because it’s excellent with canapés.

name: Moët & Chandon Grand Vintage 2012 Champagne
description: Vintage Champagne is only made in the very best years and the 2012 crop was exceptional. Moët remains one of the finest exponents of vintage Champagne and has made the most of this blockbuster year. Awarded 92-points by Wine Advocate, its 'Grand Vintage' is a sophisticated medley of expressive yellow orchard fruit, toasted nuts, fresh baked bread and honeycomb. A truly decadent flute filler.

name: "R" de Ruinart NV Champagne
description: Founded in 1729, Ruinart was the first house to export Champagne. They are one of the oldest Champagne houses and still continue to set the bar for classic and sought-after fizz. The 'R' is so elegant and subtle, it's considered the jewel in Ruinart's crown. It's golden in colour, with a consistent and fine mousse of. Expect notes of pear, apple, apricot and spice. Pair with poached scallops.

name: Krug Grande Cuvée NV Champagne
description: In 1843, Joseph Krug had an aim: to craft the very best Champagne he could offer. It's gone from strength to strength ever since. It's a blend of ten vintages, made from only the best grapes, aged for up to seven years fine oak casks. Expect complex and elegant layers of honey, nuts and a balancing acidity. Make fried chicken fine dining with this opulent Champagne.

name: Lanson Gold Label 2009 Champagne
description: With a blend of 51% Pinot Noir and 49% Chardonnay this wine has a nose of dried fig, apricot and pear mixed with biscuit undertones. The palate shows fullness and a lively finish.

name: Moët & Chandon Rosé NV Champagne
description: Moët et Chandon has the largest estate in Champagne, including cellars connected by a vast network of 28km of underground tunnels. With pink copper hues and a lively expressive aroma this is an elegant zesty rosé wine with notes of wild strawberry.

name: Devil's Creek Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Once upon a time, Devil’s Creek was a mecca for prospectors. They flocked to the lush Marlborough valleys, panning for gold in the river. Today, it’s just liquid gold that’s left. Delicious, fruity, moreish gold – the Sauvignon Blanc which has made Marlborough world-famous. We’re lucky enough to have the UK exclusive on this – and all the wines from Devil’s Creek. With such excellent Sauvignon as this, we think it’s quite the coup. Gooseberry. Peach. Citrus. It’s classic Marlborough Sauvignon – a bold, tropical, intensely refreshing glassful. A wine this zingy wants nothing more complicated that a goat’s cheese salad. Try to say no to a second glass.

name: Zuccardi Chardonnay 2014
description: This wine is made by Sebastian Zuccardi who is 2016 Argentinean Winemaker of the year at the foot of the Andes on an esate that benefits from wide temperature range and a cool dry climate. This is a classic cooler climate Chardonnay with fresh flavours of pears and peach which will develop notes of spice, flowers and toast over time. This is the perfect match for Fish Pie

name: Escarpment Te Rehua 2016 Pinot Noir
description: The Te Rehua site is extremely sheltered with deep alluvial gravels that produce classic New World Pinot Noir. Ageing in 30% new French oak for 18 months adds complexity to the elegant black cherry and plum fruit flavours.

name: Acacia Road Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: South Africa is a fabulous place to look for innovative and great value wines. This Sauvignon is lively and fresh with an abundance of zingy grapefuit and apple flavours softened by hints of ripe melon. Grapefruit and green apple shine from the glass with a lovely honeydew melon to soften the finish. A marvellous addition to a lovely fish dish or mixed vegetable platter.

name: Ataraxia Chardonnay 2018 Hemel-En-Aarde
description: Founded in 2004 by husband-and-wife team Kevin and Hanli Grant, Ataraxia are already producing some of the best small-volume Chardonnay that South Africa has to offer, utilising the mild Atlantic climate of the Hemel-en-Aarde to create a wine of purity and fine structure. Matured for 10 months in barrique, the wine has a creamy texture and gentle hazelnut complexity that pervades the citrus and stone fruit core.

name: Errazuriz Wild Ferment Chardonnay 2019 Casablanca Valley
description: With grapes taken from 'La Escultura Estate,' where cool sea breezes help preserve the freshness in the grapes. Naturally occurring yeast in the grape skins are used instead of cultivated yeasts, along with subtle use of 10% French oak. This wine is unbelievably well balanced, with subtle tropical stone fruit in the form of apricot and peach mingling with subtle oak characters and a rich creaminess. Enjoy this wine over the next two years to allow further integration of oak and fruit, and enjoy with roast chicken and seafood dishes.

name: Errazuriz Single Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc 2019 Casablanca Valley
description: The single vineyard blend is sourced from a specific block at La Escultura Estate, which was planted in 1992. This site is benefiting from sandy-loam soil and cool sea breezes. Freshly cut grass aromas complement those of green apple and pineapple, crisp acidity lends freshness to the intense and invigorating palate. The finish is dry and refreshing. Choose this wine if you are thinking of serving baked trout or salmon.

name: Errazuriz Estate Series Pinot Noir 2019, Valle de Aconcagua
description: Don Maximiano Errázuriz founded Viña Errázuriz in 1870 in the Valle de Aconcagua, 100 kms north of the capital city, Santiago. He realised that the cool, rainy winters and hot, dry summers combined with Pacific breezes to make it an ideal area to grow grapes. An attractive colour with an aromatic nose of cherries, floral notes and a touch of mint. The fresh palate displays fruit that is well-balanced by smoky nuances from the oak ageing. A smooth, silky mouthfeel make this an excellent partner to roast pork or fillet of salmon.

name: Six Brothers Chardonnay, Adelaide Hills
description: This Chardonnay is a tribute to the story of the Vandenberg family – which includes surviving a shipwreck, six brothers and their father returning from the Second World War and captaining the Hawthorn football club. So as you'd imagine, it's a big, bold Chardonnay. It's made from grapes in the cool-climate Adelaide Hills region and aged in oak for nine months. As a result, you'll taste rich notes of stone fruits, crème brûlée and cream. Ritchie Vandenberg recommends trying it with butter-poached scallops.

name: Montagny 1er Cru 2018 Louis Latour
description: You'll be shocked to know that this single-vineyard Chardonnay is totally unoaked. Why? Well, the deep roundness of the supple peach, pear and hazelnut flavours, all tickled by a whisper of butter, are so decadent you'd swear it had seen some!

name: Making Tracks Chardonnay 2018, Padthaway
description: Padthaway's cool climate gifts grapes a luxurious ripening period. So, naturally, it's storming its way onto the scene as a new-age Chardonnay hub. It's renowned for its clean, pure fruit flavour with depths of complexity. You'll taste it in Making Tracks Chardonnay - it's refined, crisp and full of zingy notes of tropical fruit and melon.

name: Nicolas Courtin Rosé
description: Although Nicolas Gueusquin began in 1993, he's already an up-and-coming talent in Champagne. So we’re very lucky to his label exclusively for Majestic customers. In this delightful rosé, discover fresh strawberry flavours and notes of cream and brioche on the finish. Perfect as an apéritif or with sashimi.

name: Selborne Classic Cuvée Brut, England
description: English fizz is making a real emergence on the sparkling-wine world map. In fact, some of Champagne's top houses are buying vineyard plots on the other side of the Channel. Ours epitomises why home-grown fizz is enjoying its moment in the spotlight. It's an elegant, creamy sparkling wine with all the fruits of a Hampshire summertime. That's why we named it in tribute the naturalist who inspired Charles Darwin.

name: Hang Loose Cabernet Sauvignon 2015
description: A classic American Cabernet, this wine emits punchy notes of blackcurrant, a slick spoonful of vanilla and dusting of regal spices. It's the gluggable red to bring buckets of Cali' sun to your sofa through winter.

name: Baba Marta Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Bulgaria
description: Domaine Boyar has been making wine since Bulgaria's communist regime fell. Its Sauvignon Blanc is fresh, bright and emits punchy notes of citrus, green apple and freshly cut grass.

name: Single Vision Chenin Blanc 2019
description: This Chenin Blanc is made by two of South Africa's most adored winemakers with a 'single vision' to create great quality and even better value. Aged on richness-gifting lees for 4 months, it's a textured bouquet of pineapple, cream and passion fruit.

name: Hokum Chardonnay 2018, Western Cape
description: A great example of new world Chardonnay, a delicate touch of oak gives this wine a lovely full bodied feel and it really reminds me of lemon meringue pie!

name: The Long Coast Cabernet Sauvignon 2019
description: Replete with luscious blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit flavours this delicious Cabernet finishes with a moreish hint of vanilla and white pepper.

name: Mr. Glover Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: This is the solo effort of Ben Glover, the man who made Mud House the colossal name it is today. After a long time hunting for a new world of Sauvignon Blanc, he's finally proud enough to put his own name on it. Expect punchy notes of melon, grapefruit, citrus and guava.

name: Sancerre Rouge "Les Bonnes Bouches" 2016, Henri Bourgeois
description: Sancerre Rouge may be lesser known than its white counterpart, but it's some finest Pinot Noir produced in France. This is made by one of the region's most respected names and 11th generation winemakers, Henri Bourgeois. It's made from 40-year-old vines and aged in oak for up to eight months. The result is a refined Pinot with notes of morello cherry, violet and oak spice. This is sure to please adventurous red Burgundy fans. Serve with a hearty meat casserole.

name: Ayala 'Brut Majeur' Extra Age Champagne
description: One of the best kept secrets in Champagne, Ayala has been under the ownership of the Bollinger family since 2005. And it's been brought back to brilliance by winemaker Caroline Latrive, one of few female Chef de Caves at the helm of a Grande Marque Champagne House. The Brut Majeur Extra Age was awarded an IWC Gold medal, recommended in Decanter as the ‘Must Try Fizz’ and previously only available in top restaurants. Until now. The clue's in the name – this Champagne is aged for an additional 12 months in Ayala's cellars. This means it's rich and complex, with toasty notes of citrus and soft bubbles. Decanter's Amy Wislocki gave it 94 points.

name: Château Tourteran Haut-Médoc 2011, Bordeaux
description: Château Tourteran can be found in Haut-Médoc, near to Pauillac and a host of world-renowned Grand Cru classé estates. It's mastered the balance between ample body and fine acidity. Its bundles of red and black fruit, fresh tobacco notes and cedar aromas are released by smooth tannins.

name: Te Mata Estate Cabernet/Merlot 2018, Hawke's Bay
description: Te Mata is the oldest winery in New Zealand. They’ve set a standard that continues to this day. They combine Old World techniques with New World flavour, so you can expect nothing less than excellence. Fresh, rich and fruit-forward. Its hints of black fruit, toast and graphite will develop over the next five years. Try this with braised lamb.

name: Marisco 'The King's Wrath' Pinot Noir 2017, Marlborough
description: Brent Marris is the man behind our best-selling white wine. He's also the distant relative of one of King Henry the First's rumoured illegitimate children. His 'King's Wrath' Pinot Noir boasts oak-driven hints of red fruit, silk-smooth tannins and a bright, fresh acidity. It's a worthy tribute to a rightful legacy of royalty.

name: Wakefield 'Jaraman' Chardonnay 2018, Australia
description: Wakefield's Jaraman wines are a fusion of two exceptional parcels of grapes from some of the finest spots in Australia. Those behind this Chardonnay are sourced from Clare Valley and Margaret River, two of the country's very best cool-climate spots. Oak-aged on its texture-imparting lees, this is a precise cocktail of melon, cream and cedar. Partner with butternut squash ravioli.

name: Domaine Fouassier 'Sur le Fort' Sancerre 2018
description: The Fouassier family are part of the fabric of Sancerre. It’s a tenth generation domaine – one of the oldest in the region, and their wines usually sell for top dollar. Brothers Benoit and Paul have two goals: making the best wines possible, and leaving their vineyards in the best possible condition for the next generation. It means their organic Sancerre wines are of incredible quality – and incredibly well made. Think tart green apple. The pear and lemon notes are trademark Fouassier, and it’s so crisp you’ll find it slipping down all too easily. Full, fruity – it’s incredibly intense so the flavours really last. That fruitiness combined with Thai-syle spice is a marriage made in heaven.

name: Bruno Paillard 'Premiere Cuvée' Brut Champagne
description: Bruno Paillard sold his vintage Jaguar to fund his dream of making fizz. Almost 40 years later, he and daughter Alice are behind one of the most prestigious family-run houses in Champagne. Aged for three years on texture-imparting lees, the Première Cuvée is fizz as complex as they come. Decanter gave it 92 points.

name: Fincher & Co. Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Awatere Valley
description: This is made by Ben Glover, previously winemaker of Wither Hills and Mud House, and a Master of Wine, Liam Steevenson. It's from the Awatere Valley, which is cooler than the rest of Marlborough. As a result, the wine is less overtly tropical. It's a New Zealand Sauvignon with Loire sensibilities. Try its elegant peach, lemon sherbet and mineral hints with sea bass and asparagus.

name: Nicolas Courtin Brut 2013, Champagne
description: Most Champagne is a careful blend of vintages to ensure a uniform house style. However, vintage Champagne is only made from a single excellent vintage and must be aged for double the amount of time as normal Champagnes. As you will taste in the Courtin, this combination creates a luxurious fizz. With extended bottle age it is a ripe, brioche-toned, toasty vintage Champagne with excellent weight of citrus fruit.

name: Drappier 'Signature' Blanc de Blancs Brut, Champagne
description: A pioneer of hands off winemaking, and the first carbon neutral Champagne house, Drappier is a byword for innovation. Made from Chardonnay and a dash of rarely used Pinot Blanc – this Blanc de Blancs is a prime example of its ingenuity. With 5% of the wine aged in barrel and lees ageing of 36 months with little sulphites, it's both thoroughly modern and delicious.

name: Marsannay 2018 Louis Latour, Burgundy
description: With rich iron soils, Marsannay is where you'll find powerful, robust Pinot Noir at a fraction of the price of those next door in Gevrey-Chambertin. Louis Latour. Arguably the biggest name in Burgundy, ages its expression for up to a year in stainless steel, crafting a muscular palate of red fruit and leather and perfectly integrated tannins.

name: Babich Organic Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Babich own one of the first vineyards to ever be planted in New Zealand. They were also the first winery to be certified organic in the country. As a result, this is a real benchmark organic Sauvignon Blanc. It's fresh and ripe, with notes of stone fruit, lime and apple and a mouth-watering finish. Try it with a light salad or white fish.

name: Puligny-Montrachet 2018 Doudet Naudin
description: Founded in 1849, Doudet Naudin is one of the most respected wineries in Burgundy. Made from old vines, from hand-picked grapes, and aged in vanilla-imparting oak for a year, its Puligny-Montrachet is a creamy cocktail of almond, honeysuckle and exotic fruits.

name: Jomain Frères Bourgogne Blanc 2018
description: The Jomain family is known for its delicious Puligny-Montrachet. But it also makes great value white Burgundy with vines owned by a family friend. Once the Chardonnay grapes are picked by hand and pampered by the same fine treatment, the wine is aged in both steel and oak for a total of 12 months. The result is soft, dry and emits smooth hints of lychee and pear that are lined by a refreshing acidity.

name: Scheid Family Wines 'Rocklin Ranch' Cabernet Sauvignon 2017/18, Monterey
description: Rocklin Ranch sits in the volcanic foothills of Monterey and is powered by a single wind turbine. They're just as dedicated to fantastic wine as they are the environment. And it really shows in this bold, structured Cabernet Sauvignon. It has notes of ripe plum, blackberry, blueberry, baking spices and toasty vanilla. Try it with a beef burger.

name: Balnaves Cabernet-Merlot 2014, Coonawarra
description: In 2019, the Balnaves winery was rated a perfect 5 Red Stars for the 17th year in a row by Australia's leading wine critic, James Halliday. In the cool-climate Coonawarra region, it creates outstanding, balanced Cabernet Sauvignon. This is blended with Merlot to craft a plush, rounded palate with notes of blackberry, elderberry and toasty oak and fine-grain tannins.

name: Le Voyage d'Odette Sauvignon Blanc 2019, France
description: Le Voyage d'Odette is an expressive, fruity Sauvignon Blanc with exotic aromas, hints of citrus and white flowers and a fresh finish.

name: Chablis 1er Cru Vaillons 2019 Séguinot-Bordet
description: An elegant, steely and incredibly pure Premier Cru Chablis from Jean-Francois Bordet. The Vaillons Premier Cru vineyards face south east, and as a result the wines are famous for their great richness. Expect mineral notes together with plenty of fine citrus and white stone fruit flavours. There is a yeasty complexity and a creaminess to the texture, but the finish remains classically racey and fresh.

name: Meursault 'Les Clous' 2018 Domaine Prunier
description: For this luxurious Chardonnay winemaker Vincent Prunier picks every grape by hand from one of Meursault's most famous vineyards - known as a 'lieu-dit'. Once they're pressed, he vinifies it in stainless steel and ages it in oak for up to a year and a half. The result is a smooth, creamy marriage of vanilla and stone fruit with an all-important balancing acidity.

name: Château Cantenac-Brown 2008, Margaux
description: Although Château Cantenac-Brown has changed hands many times since it was built in 1806, winemaker Jose Sanfins has been a constant of the last decade. His respect for its historic land shines through in his wines, all grapes and hand-picked and sorted with a great degree of care. His wines are made with a real potential to age, making them a favourite among keen collectors.

name: Domaine Maire 'Heritage' Chardonnay 2018, Côtes du Jura
description: For wine lovers in the know, the Jura is the region to go to for great-value Burgundy style wines. Just like Burgundy, winemakers in the Jura work with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Domaine Maire have hit a real sweet spot. The high altitude of their vineyards bring an amazing freshness to their Chardonnay. Add a dash of Savignin (Jura's signature grape) and you've got a really unique wine. It's fine and elegant, with notes of white flowers, citrus and cedar. Enjoy this with an equally rich cheese omelette.

name: Saint Véran 'Haute Cuvée' 2018 Rijckaert F. Rouve, Burgundy
description: Jean Rijckaert is renowned for creating fantastic white Burgundy. Under his guidance, Florent Rouve, who began making wine in Jura at just 16 years old, has created this exceptional Saint-Véran. Aged for up to 15 months in small oak barrels, it's rich and round with gentle oak influence and a fine, succulent texture.

name: Saint Véran 'Reserve' 2018, Collovray & Terrier
description: This 'Reserve' wine from Collovray & Terrier is delicious proof that Saint Véran remains one of the best value communes in Burgundy. Aged for six months on lees, in a combination of oak and steel tanks, this golden Chardonnay has a beautiful structure and richness. Expect notes of ripe citrus, white flowers and a delicate minerality.

name: Yealands Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Yealands was the first winery in the world to hold carbon-zero certification as soon as it began. With Natalie Christensen, who was one of the World's Most Influential Women in Wine in 2018, as head winemaker, it's won four trophies and 27 gold medals with this Sauvignon Blanc in just eight years. Expect lively notes of gooseberry, blackcurrant leaf and fresh herbs. Try this with a light salad.

name: Côtes de Beaune Villages 2017 Bouchard Aîné & Fils, Burgundy
description: Bouchard Aîné & Fils has produced Pinot Noir in the heart of Burgundy since 1750. It ages this Pinot Noir in oak for 12 months, crafting ripe notes of red and black fruits and a delicate hint of woody spice. It's elegant, complex and makes the perfect partner for red meat or poultry.

name: Domaine Chofflet-Valdenaire Givry 2018
description: It's said that Henry IV's favourite wine was Givry. Today, this spot of Burgundy produces fresh, vibrant Pinot Noir at great value. This is the best one our buyer Nadia found. It has flavours of blackcurrants, raspberries and cherries that are great with white meats or game.

name: Firepeak Pinot Noir 2018, Edna Valley
description: If you’re a fan of Pinot Noir, the Edna Valley is a region to know. You’d be forgiven for thinking that California would be too hot for the delicate grape. But some of America’s best Pinot Noirs come from this coastal region. The Firepeak is a case in point. The wine is complex yet balanced, showing notes of cherry, blackberry and cola. Enjoy with roast chicken.

name: Erath Pinot Noir 2017, Oregon
description: Dick Erath planted his first Oregon vineyards in 1969, founding what has become one of the region's finest properties. In terms of climate and terrain, much of Oregon's vineyard area is said to bear a striking resemblance to Burgundy. Showcasing the refined, cool-climate style of Oregon, this is a bright, fresh Pinot with a supremely poised and fine-textured profile, enhanced by maturation in 25%-new French oak. Erath recommends pairing this with peppery oriental dishes.

name: Boekenhoutskloof 'Porcupine Ridge' Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon 2020, South Africa
description: Boekenhoutskloof is one of South Africa’s most prominent wineries. Their Porcupine Ridge wines showcase a more unconventional, fun approach to winemaking. The results are delicious and this Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon blend is a striking example of their talent. It’s a style widely celebrated in Bordeaux but rarely seen in South Africa. Generous sunlight has encouraged intense flavours, and a touch of oak has dialled up its character. Expect luscious notes of melon, stone fruit and gooseberry. Partner with roasted pork loin.

name: Awatere River Chardonnay 2017, Marlborough
description: Louis Vavasour's parents were the first to plant vines in Awatere Valley. In the present day, Louis continues to reap the rewards of their courage. And it really shows in this Burgundy-style Chardonnay. It has notes of melon, citrus and peach with hints of hazelnut and cashew. After a few years of development in the bottle, it's primed to partner your rich roasts this winter.

name: Barista Chardonnay 2019, Western Cape
description: South African Chardonnay is a bit of an unsung gem. The country’s best known for its blockbuster reds – like Barista Pinotage. And now the winemaker behind that customer favourite has made this ripe, fruity, juicy Chardonnay. Bertus Fourie is nicknamed ‘Starbucks’ because he’s famous for bringing out the distinctive coffee-like flavours of his trademark Pinotage. He’s done the same with this white. It’s all about citrus. Zippy, zesty, creamy citrus – with just a touch of peach and vanilla. It’s part-fermented and matured in old oak barrels, giving the texture a roundness which is a great foil to the crispness of the fruit. It’s delicious with grilled chicken.

name: Paolo Leo 'Renèe Bianco Organic' 2019, Puglia
description: The family behind Paolo Leo winery have been making wine since the 18th century. So they know a thing or two about getting the best out of the Puglian soil. Their passion for Italy is reflected in the fact they choose to farm organically. We think the fruit really stands out as a result. In certainly shows in this blend of Chardonnay, Fiano and Sauvignon Blanc, with its rich notes of tropical fruit and citrus acidity. Pair with delicate white fish.

name: Creation Chardonnay 2020, Walker Bay
description: In 2002, Jean-Claude and Carolyn Martin struck up the courage to start the Creation winery on the picturesque Hemel-en-Aarde ridge. Back then, this lofty spot had never seen a vine before. Today, they create exceptional Chardonnay sustainably. And you'll get the high-altitude benefits in every sip. If you like Bread and Butter Chardonnay, you're in for a treat. This is a Burgundy-style Chardonnay with South African sensibilities. Expect flavours of peach, pear and vanilla and a refreshing minerality. Jean-Claude and Carolyn suggest pairing it with creamy pasta.

name: Ernst Loosen Pinot Noir 2018, Pfalz
description: Ernst Loosen's family have made fantastic Riesling on the Mosel slopes for over 200 years. But to get the best from Pinot Noir, he headed to Pfalz. Pfalz Pinot Noir has quickly become one of Germany's flagship wine styles - and this really shows why. It has bright notes of red fruit, balancing acidity and smooth tannins. Excellent with roast chicken.

name: Louis de Cherencey Chablis 2018
description: After a few tough harvests, it's been tough to get Chablis at a good price recently. Luckily, Burgundy buyer Nadia headed to the region on the back of the stunning 2018 vintage and discovered this gem. It's crisp and fine, with notes of lemon and lime. Excellent with seafood.

name: Montes ‘Alpha’ Cabernet 2018, Colchagua Valley
description: Montes was founded in 1987 by winemaker Aurelio Montes and Douglas Murray. Their vision was to produce Chilean wines of premium quality. They've continued to raise the bar for the country ever since. And the name is now one of the most celebrated in South American wine. This is rich and layered Cabernet, with notes of ripe black fruit, sweet tobacco and spice. Serve at 17-19 degrees with beef ribs or cellar for up to a decade.

name: Domaine Faiveley Puligny Montrachet 1er Cru
description: Domaine Faiveley's signature style is Puligny-Montrachet that's approachable in its youth, with just a hint of oak influence. This is made on the premier cru climat of Champ Gain. It has notes of lemongrass, pear, toast and flint. Serve at 10-13 degrees with roast chicken.

name: Château de Santenay 'Terroirs et Château' Chardonnay 2018/19, Santenay
description: Château de Santenay have been making Burgundy for over 700 years. Terroirs et Château is the fashionable winery that leans against the Château. And its winemaking team have not disappointed with this complex Chardonnay. It has flavours of stone fruits, honey, vanilla and citrus. Serve this at 10-13 degrees with Camembert.

name: Domaine Jean Chauvenet Nuits-Saint-Georges 2017
description: The Chauvenet family have been making wine in Burgundy since 1680. For their outstanding Pinot Noir, they balance their heritage with constant innovation. Expect complex notes of red berries. Pair with rich tomato pasta.

name: Girardin Père et Fils Puligny-Montrachet Vieilles Vignes 2017
description: This is Burgundy at its best. Labelled 'Vieilles Vignes', this wine comes from old vines that are more than 65 years old. In the cellar, there is minimal intervention to bring out the best of this great vintage. Expect mineral notes with a well-balanced acidity. Wonderful with creamy chicken.

name: Laurent-Perrier 'La Cuvée' Champagne & 2x Champagne Glasses
description: The winemaking team at Laurent-Perrier took a big gamble when they decided to recreate their non-vintage - which was, at the time, one of the best-selling Champagnes in the world. When you taste 'La Cuvée', you'll be glad they did. It has elegant hints of citrus, peach and toast.

name: Domaine Lucien Lardy Beaujolais-Villages Blanc 2019
description: Just 5% of the wine made in Beaujolais is white. The region's only a short drive from the heart of Burgundy. But it has a marginally warmer climate. So what’s made here is a richer style of Chardonnay. Lucien Lardy makes this smooth, delicate and citrussy wine in a fiercely Independent, low-intervention way in some of Beaujolais’ top vineyards. Pair this with chicken or grilled fish.

name: Te Mata 'Coleraine' 2018, Hawke's Bay
description: Te Mata make Coleraine from the finest grapes on their oldest vineyards. It's a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot and Cabernet Franc that's medium-bodied with an excellent length. Expect notes of ripe blackberries, mocha, cedar and sweet spice that go excellently with beef.

name: Château La Rame 2017 Ste-Croix-du-Mont Half Bottle
description: Château La Rame is one of the oldest properties in the Sainte Croix du Mont appellation, where the influence of the river encourages botrytis or noble rot. A blend of 75% Sémillon and 25% Sauvignon Blanc. Aged in one-third new oak barrels for two years. A golden hued wine exudes aromas of ripe stone fruits, candied summer fruits and honey. Luxurious sweetness and spicy vanillin characters layer and coat the palate before a clean and fresh finish. Sweet wines can age for as long as you can resist them, a perfect match for Crème Brûlée or blue cheese.

name: Bourgogne Pinot Noir 'Les Pierres Rouges' 2018 Louis Jadot
description: Maison Louis Jadot has been making wine in Burgundy since before 1859. With an unwavering dedication to quality, winemaker Jacques Lardière aims for excellence with all his wines. Displaying subtle hints of red fruit and violets on the nose, with a well rounded palate of cherry and raspberry fruit with a rounded texture. Gently tannic with good length. Drinking superbly on its own, also try this with small game.

name: Rully Rouge "En Rosey" Maison Chanzy 2018
description: Maison Chanzy own top quality vineyard sites all over Burgundy including Grand Crus. Rully is a lesser known village in the Côte Chalonnaise that produces Pinot Noir that can rival the quality, if not the price tag, of its more famous rivals to the north. This wine is stuffed full of bright red fruit flavours, think cherries and redcurrants, with a fine firm tannic structure and balancing acidity that makes it a great foil for all manner of food. This will be an excellent match for roasted poultry or game birds.

name: Domaine Jomain Bourgogne Blanc 2018
description: The Jomain family are best known for creating superb Puligny-Montrachet. And this is your chance to taste their expertise at a lower price. Once the grapes are hand-picked and pressed, this wine is aged partially in oak. These grapes may not be from the usual family estate, but they receive the same care and attention as the family's finest. The result's a soft, dry Chardonnay with notes of lychee and pear. Best with cold cuts.

name: Louis Jadot 'Les Roches Blanches' Mâcon-Villages 2020
description: There’s something to be said for a wine you can rely on. Louis Jadot is one of the biggest names in Burgundy – a name you can depend on for consistently well made, consistently delicious wines. Their Mâcons are enduring Majestic favourites for a reason. They offer the quality guarantee of Jadot at a wallet-friendly price. Winemaker Frédéric Barnier built a reputation for elegant white wines working at £40+ a bottle Château de Chassagne Montrachet. You can taste his skill here too. For this, he’s used parcels of grapes from the very best sites in the Mâcon. It’s wonderfully fruity – think apples, pears and a subtle, pastry-like richness. Frédéric leaves the wine unoaked so the fruit flavours can really shine through. As for food pairing? Think cream, butter (or both) and you can’t go wrong.

name: Domaine Louis Moreau Petit Chablis 2019
description: Chablis may be part of Burgundy – but you won’t find any Pinot Noir vines here. It’s all about Chardonnay. Specifically: crisp, citrussy, refreshing Chardonnay. Chablis is a lot further north than the rest of Burgundy so has a cooler climate. This, combined with the tradition of leaving the majority of wines unoaked means that the style of these lean, light white Burgundys are very different from their rich, fruity southern cousins. It takes a real expert to hit the balance of freshness and flavour. Luckily, Domaine Louis Moreau have been doing it for six generations. This Petit Chablis is absolutely textbook. It’s crisp. It’s citrussy. It’s wonderfully fresh. It’s all about that classic appley, lemony Chardonnay fruit – and it’s a fantastic palate-cleanser. Try it with sashimi.

name: Bourgogne Côte Chalonnaise 2019 La Buxynoise
description: The Côte Chalonnaise in the southern part of the Côte d'Or is responsible for the majority of winemaking in Burgundy, where cooperatives of growers produce some of the most consistently good quality wines such as this Chardonnay, representing great value. With just 3% of the blend fermented in new oak, this is a fresh and crisp Chardonnay with a focus on golden apple and citrus flavours, with very subtle texture and toastiness. The perfect white wine to accompany cod or other white fish cooked 'au gratin'.

name: Saint-Aubin Blanc 1er Cru 2019 Domaine Gérard Thomas et Filles
description: Bordering the acclaimed vineyards of Puligny-Montrachet, Gérard Thomas produces a St Aubin Premier Cru that challenges its pricier neighbour. He barrel ferments this wine to give it a softer and smoother complexity. With a nose of ripe citrus, fresh peach, subtle sweet spice and hints of minerals, this is followed by gentle hazelnut aromas, lemons and limes, hints of vanilla and butter on the palate. This fantastically well balanced and complex wine would be a great accompaniment to pan fried scallops, fresh crab or lobster dishes.

name: Maison Chanzy 'Rully Blanc En Rosey' 2018, Burgundy
description: Maison Chanzy own top quality vineyard sites all over Burgundy including Grand Crus. This wine may be from a less illustrious village than Chassagne or Puligny but it is made with the same care and attention resulting in a fabulously decadent and delicious wine. Pale gold with pronounced aromas of greengage and pear. The palate is richer with ripe apple, lemon and a slight note of toast yet retains a delightful freshness in a long persistent finish. Perfect with chicken or meatier fish.

name: Saint-Véran 2018 Les Vignerons des Grandes Vignes
description: Saint-Véran is in the southern part of Burgundy overlapping the Mâconnais and Beaujolais districts. This wine is produced by an outstanding co-operative. This is a lively wine showing delicious aromas of peach and acacia blossom. Fantastic when young, it has the potential to develop a more honeyed, complex palate with age. This delicate and elegant wine would be a great match with seared scallops or mushroom ravioli.

name: Le Difese 2013 Tenuta San Guido 2018
description: "This 70-30 blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Sangiovese would indeed pair perfectly with game dishes such as fettuccine in rabbit sauce or wild boar sausage thanks to that extra touch of acidity that cuts through any fattiness. Dried cherry, blackberry, leather and tobacco enhance the wine's medium build and silky texture. There is a distant touch of overripe fruit at the very beginning. Le Difese is aged for 12 months in both French and American oak. Anticipated maturity: 2013-2018". 89 points. Monica Larner, The Wine Advocate.

name: Chateau De Crezancy 2019
description: This is a beautiful expression of crisp citrus and peach flavours heightened by floral notes and balanced by a fine mineral finish.

name: Domaine Roger Neveu 'Cóte des Embouffants' Sancerre 2019
description: The Neveu family’s roots in the Loire run deep. Neveus have been recorded in the village of Verdigny since the twelfth century. That’s right in the heart of Sancerre – where their name is now a byword for quality. And like the Neveus, the vineyard this wine comes from is steeped in history. Côtes des Embouffants was singled out by Abbot Poupart in his 1777 history of Sancerre. He said that 'the Bouffants hillside is one of the best I know in our Sancerre area’ – and nearly 250 years later, his assessment still holds true. The chalky soils are perfect for vibrant, crisp Sauvignon, and the flavours really leap out of the glass. It’s intensely refreshing with citrus and lime acidity, and a hard-to-put-your-finger-on complexity from six months of lees ageing. Ideal with a classic goat’s cheese tart.

name: Chapel Down Rosé 2019, England
description: Chapel Down, official supper to 10 Downing Street and The Cambridge and Oxford Boat Race, has encapsulated English summertime perfectly by plucking grapes from Kent and crafting this balanced rosé. Its soft red fruits meet floral notes and a refreshing finish. This is the ideal barbecue wine.

name: Nicolas Feuillatte NV Champagne 150cl Magnum
description: A light and fruity Champagne with fine discreet bubbles and subtle overtones of apples and pears. Has undergone four years cellar ageing.

name: Drappier Premier Cru Champagne
description: It’s a household name in France – but Drappier is still a relatively well-kept secret on this side of the Channel. The house is 200 years old, with some of the oldest and most extensive cellars in Europe. They’re the only ones to have survived unscathed through the world wars and the fires which swept the region soon after. But they don’t rest on their history. They have a really forward-thinking approach based on sustainability, making carbon-neutral fizz. It’s from Premier Cru vineyards – consistently rated in the top 90% in the region for quality. Fresh and intense – it tastes of pear and vanilla. It’s the perfectly plush glassful for fans of rich, toasty Champagne. Of course, it’s an ideal aperitif, but for a high-low option it’s fantastic with fried chicken.

name: Bollinger Special Cuvée, Champagne
description: Think Bolly – think Bond. Or, rather less prosaically, Absolutely Fabulous. Bollinger has glitzy associations, but beneath the ritz lies seriously high-quality fizz. Unusually for a Champagne, where richness and toasty flavours are usually the result of ageing, Bollinger uses oak to ferment its base wines so they have a roundness, spiciness and creamy texture. Also unusually for Champagne, 75% of the grapes used for this Brut NV are grown by them in vineyards which are nearly all Grand and Premier Cru. It means the fruit in the bottle is the best of the best – so it’s no wonder that Bollinger has a bit of a cult following. Ripe. Buttery. Full. The lush texture underpins apple and pear flavours. It’s 60% Pinot Noir, giving the wine great structure, making it ideal with sushi or sashimi.

name: Louis Roederer Brut Premier Champagne
description: From the maker of Cristal, a deliciously rich Champagne, with an overt fruitiness and nutty, yeasty notes. A long, complex and rewarding finish is a hallmark of the house style.

name: Lanson 'Le Rosé' Brut Champagne
description: Lanson made one of the first ever rosé Champagnes. 60 years later, they've perfected their recipe. The Grande Marque's signature rosé is a delightful salmon pink. It's well-rounded and fresh, with intriguing aromas of roses and red fruits. It makes an ideal partner to delicate white meat dishes, or even fresh fruit desserts.

name: Veuve Clicquot 'La Grande Dame' 2008 Champagne
description: Named after the 'Grand Lady', in tribute to Madame Clicquot, 'La Grand Dame' is the flagship and pinnacle of Veuve Clicquot's style. Elegant, refined and delicate, it's a perfect homage to the role she played in the formative years of the Champagne house. A fine accompaniment to sea bass.

name: Pol Roger 2012/13 Champagne
description: Pol Roger reserve their declarations of a vintage for years in which all the climatic, viticultural and winemaking components come together for the making of the finest Champagne.

name: Chapel Down Brut, England
description: Chapel Down is the UK’s largest winemaker – and one of its best. Even ten years ago, English wine wasn’t something any wine lover gave much time to. Its thanks to pioneers like Chapel Down that that’s all changed and English wine has gained the recognition it deserves. Their vineyards are ideally positioned in the south-east, with HQ among the oast houses, deep in picturesque East Sussex. Here, the chalk soils and balmy climate are ideal for making crisp wines. It’s made using the laborious and expensive ‘traditional method’, standard in Champagne. But though it’s made from the same grapes – Chardonnay and Pinot Noir – these are altogether zestier bubbles. Apple. Lemongrass. Quince. It’s fresh, fruity and really moreish. Perfect to cut through the crunchy batter of fish and chips.

name: Shaw and Smith 'M3' Chardonnay 2019, Adelaide Hills
description: Shaw and Smith began in 1989 when cousins Martin Shaw and Michael Hall Smith decided to realise a dream to make wine together. Their aim was to make exciting, refined wines and they specialise in Sauvignon Blanc, cool climate Shiraz and this single vineyard Chardonnay. "This beautiful Chardonnay gets better with each vintage. It's a gorgeously subtle white wine with a core of lemony fruit and just a hint of very smart, powdery oak." Max Allen, The Weekend Australian. Lightly chill and drink over the next five years with roast pork.

name: Alamos Uco Valley Chardonnay 2019/20
description: The grapes for Alamos are grown at an altitude of between 3,000 and 5,000 feet where the temperature variation allows the grapes to grow slowly developing more flavour. Hand harvesting and sorting ensure only the best grapes are used to make the wine. The Alamos Chardonnay from the Uco Valley has a fine array of bright fruit flavours including lemon, ripe apple and peach. There are further notes of tropical fruit and a touch of toasty oak. The flavours here will work brilliantly with grilled chicken flavoured with a touch of spice.

name: St Clair 'Wairau Reserve' Sauvignon Blanc 2018 Marlborough
description: Saint Clair's range of 'reserve' Sauvignons have been made from top-quality vineyards within a specific region, in order to showcase the effects of terroir. Fermented with selected yeasts in 100% stainless steel at cool temperatures for maximum varietal impact. Displaying subtle fragrances of honeysuckle, powerful passion fruit and gooseberry flavours on the nose, which develop into blackcurrant leaf, lime and ripe nettles on the palate. Full, rich and firm. An ideal partner for sophisticated seafood dishes such as salmon or seared scallops.

name: Oyster Bay Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Oyster Bay has been an award-winning gold-standard Marlborough Sauvignon since its very first vintage, and it’s only got better and better. It was even formerly named the ‘Best Sauvignon Blanc in the World’ – and they have the name recognition to prove it. It’s really no surprise then that their Sauvignon is textbook Marlborough. Big. Fruity. Tropical. To say it’s mouth-wateringly refreshing is an understatement – the smell of gooseberries and lime will hit you the minute you unscrew the bottle. It’s really versatile – delicious as a glassful on sofa, for parties or with a flaky goat’s cheese tart.

name: Saint Clair Pioneer Block Pinot Noir 2019, Marlborough
description: Saint Clair Pioneer Block wines are made from small parcels of outstanding fruit identified by our winemaker. Fruit for this wine was sourced from Saint Clair's own vineyard at Doctor's Creek, and blended with a small amount from the Southern Valleys to add complexity. Bright ruby in colour, aromas of raspberry and plum are complemented by a touch of spice. A luscious and soft palate with flavours of redcurrant, cherries and an added warmth from ageing in oak barrels. This is perfectly matched with tuna or beef fillet with mushrooms and will cellar for the next two to five years.

name: Dog Point Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: James Healy and Ivan Sutherland are Sauvignon Blanc experts with over 50 years of winemaking experience between them. After spending a decade at Cloudy Bay, they began making this Sauvignon from Sutherland's vineyards, which were planted in the '70s and '80s. This vine age makes for a unique complexity in their wine. As a result, it's long been the darling of critical praise. It has plush notes of passionfruit and citrus, and a saline acidity. Try it with fresh seafood.

name: Escarpment 'Kiwa' Pinot Noir 2016, Martinborough
description: Pinot Noir superstar Larry McKenna's 'Kiwa' Pinot Noir is a perfumed, spicy red that emits attractive notes of red cherry and raspberry. Its snappy tannins and succulent acidity balance this powerful Pinot perfectly. James Suckling gave this vintage 96 points.

name: Catena Chardonnay 2019, Mendoza
description: A blend of grapes from three high altitude vineyard sites, La Pirámide, Domingo and Adrianna, creating a wine of unique character that has balance, concentration and a strong varietal identity. Showing notes of mango, banana and apple, with a hint of sweet spice and wet stone minerality. Full yet clean on the palate, with pear and fig fruit flavours rounded off with fresh, crisp acidity. Excellent with roast chicken or gammon dishes.

name: Vasse Felix 'Filius' Chardonnay 2019, Margaret River
description: Although Vasse Felix almost single-handedly put Margaret River on the world map, only 7% of the region's wines leaves Australia. Luckily, we've just secured 250 cases of its 'Filius' Chardonnay. Complex hints of honey, pineapple, lemon sherbert and a hint of ginger lead a citrus-lined acidity.

name: Puligny-Montrachet 2017 Domaine Alain Chavy
description: Many regard Puligny-Montrachet as the finest expression of Chardonnay. Not just the best of Burgundy - but the best in the world. Domaine Alain Chavy is an expert in the art, plucking grapes from 42-year-old vines and ageing the wine for a total of 18 months in oak AND steel in 18-feet-deep cellars. This resulting 2015 vintage benefits in its delightful bouquet of apple blossom, gooseberry, slate and cream.

name: Château Citran 'Moulin de Citran' 2010, Haut-Médoc
description: Château Citran has stood strong since the 14th century. The Moulin de Citran is its second wine. This means that it's made from slightly younger vines than the Grand Vin, but with just as much care. An excellent freshness, balancing acidity, ripe fruit flavour and a great potential to improve with age. It has perfumed notes of raspberry, black cherry and strawberry, a great texture and a long finish. Try this with pork chops.

name: Selborne Rosé Brut
description: Selborne captures all the subtle flavours that make English sparkling wine so unique. On the palate, it is wonderfully crisp with delicate summer berry fruits balanced by a hint of lemon zest and a deliciously creamy finish.

name: Castaway Island Chardonnay 2018, Gisborne
description: Years ago, New Zealand was a treacherous place to reach by sea. It's been responsible for countless shipwrecks. Premium Chardonnay is the Kiwi castaway. Overshadowed by Sauvignon Blanc, it's not given the focus it deserves on the wine-world stage. So it's still under the radar. And you can taste this world-beating expertise at a snip. You're in for a punchy cocktail of tropical fruit and melon, all balanced by zesty acidity. It shows the best of what's unique to Gisborne. You'll truly get lost in the abundance of fruit flavours in this. And you'll just find it here.

name: Miss Lucy Sauvignon Pinot Gris
description: Crisp and fresh citrus flavours of grapefruit are to the fore in the elegant and fresh wine. A perfect match for seafood the fruitiness gives way to a fine mineral finish.

name: Kutch Pinot Noir 2016, Sonoma Coast
description: Jamie Kutch fled to California to make wine just 14 years ago. But he's already swimming in praise. This 2016 vintage alone won a huge 93 Wine Enthusiast points. Its concentrated flavours of black cherry, vanilla and herbs is balanced by a cooling freshness.

name: Bird in Hand 'Two in the Bush' Chardonnay 2019/20, Adelaide Hills
description: Bird in Hand's winemaker, Kym Milne MW, was the second Australian to ever become a Master of Wine. He was also named Australian Winemaker of the Year two years in a row by Winestate magazine. This is made from Bird in Hand's cool-climate vineyards and is a chance to taste their expertise at a lower price. This is a textured Chardonnay, with notes of stone fruits, nougat, hazelnut and sweet spice. Try it with roast chicken.

name: Cono Sur Bicicleta Pinot Noir 2019, Chile
description: Cono Sur has been named IWSC ‘Chilean Winery of the Year’ two years in a row. It’s rare for a winery to win it in consecutive years – and the British public have given it the seal of approval too. It’s the UK’s most popular Pinot Noir. And it’s as sustainable as it is delicious. Everything Cono Sur do is designed to be kind to the planet. They use geese as pest control and bicycles to get around the vineyards – and the vines reward them with fruit which is extra-delicious. It’s light. It’s fresh. It’s vibrantly fruity. It’s a really easy-drinking Pinot Noir, full of juicy cherry, blackberry and plum flavours. Delicious with charcuterie or fish dishes.

name: Domaine des Patureaux Pouilly Fume 2019
description: Domaine Patureaux is boutique winemaking at its best. On this small, family-owned estate, the father-and-son team has created a powerful, refreshing Pouilly-Fumé with bold aromas of pear, peach and apricot, a long, rich finish and a delicious smokiness.

name: Doctor's 9.5% Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: The Doctor (PhD, not GP) behind this Sauvignon uses pioneering vineyard techniques to craft the best hallmarks of Marlborough at just 9.5% alcohol. With a zesty palate of gooseberry, nettle and fresh grass, it's the perfect picnic wine.

name: Framingham 'Nobody's Hero' Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Framingham does things differently. This Marlborough estate holds an outdoor rock concert every harvest. So, as you can imagine, their approach to winemaking is a little unconventional. We love the result. To preserve complexity, each parcel of fruit behind this bold Sauvignon is picked from several sites on Framingham's estate and vinified separately. The result is zesty Sauvignon full of rich guava, apple-skin and pineapple flavours.

name: Devil's Creek 'Barrel Select' Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: This isn’t your standard Kiwi Sauvignon Blanc. Winemaker Michele Pedron knows his crisp, refreshing whites inside out. He’s worked all over the world specialising in exactly the sort of mouth-watering whites New Zealand is known for. He also worked in Sancerre – inspiring him to try a barrel aged Sauvignon. It’s a style more commonly found in France, but his gamble more than paid off. Barrel ageing gives the wine more weight and a roundness of texture. It’s a moreish contrast to the textbook Kiwi Sauvignon tropical, gooseberry flavours, adding a sweetly spicy edge. Delicious. The subtle smokiness makes this an interesting food wine – it’s incredible with usually tricky to pair smoked fish.

name: Larry Cherubino ‘Folklore’ Chardonnay 2019
description: In the mid-1800s, the ancestors of the Cherubinos planted the first vines on the Frankland River. The winemaking family are in the same spot to this day. They're now one of West Australia’s most renowned producers and were named Best Value Winery in 2017 by the country's leading wine critic, James Halliday. Bright and creamy, expect citrus and white peach notes. A divine accompaniment with seafood.

name: Luis Felipe Edwards Elegido Sauvignon Gran Reserva, Leyda Valley
description: Aromas of white flowers and gentle honeyed notes emerge from the bouquet, inviting you to enjoy its zippy citrus palate. The finish lingers beautifully after each sip - delivering mouthwatering freshness and sophistication at once.

name: De Bortoli Tumbarumba Chardonnay 2018
description: Although it planted its first vines less than 40 years ago, Tumbarumba is one of the most promising regions in Australia. This single-vineyard Chardonnay is made from one of the two oldest vineyards there. It's cool-climate brilliance wins buckets of gold medals on home soil with its complex palate of stone fruits, cream, brioche and a flinty acidity.

name: Rocklin Ranch Chardonnay 2018, Monterey
description: Rocklin Ranch has sustainability at its heart. Situated in the volcanic foothills of Monterey, their winery is powered by a single wind turbine. They take their name from the ancient river rocks deposited around their vineyards. Monterey’s reputation for distinctive white wines is certainly reflected in this first-class bottle. Classic Chardonnay lovers, this one’s for you. Bright and elegant, this has notes of red apple, pear and lemon. Pair with a grilled chicken caesar salad.

name: Jordan Chardonnay 2013 Stellenbosch
description: Grown on the cooler south and east-facing slopes of Stellenbosch, this Chardonnay is fermented in Burgundy-style French oak barriques, around 50% new oak. After 9 months' maturation, it is blended with a small amount of unoaked wine for added citrus character. A rich and powerful wine, brimming with glossy, honeyed apple and lemon fruit, with hints of hazelnut. The deft use of oak ageing has added aromas of buttered toast, and a roundness to the palate. Best enjoyed with roast poultry.

name: Château Bel-Orme Tronquoy de Lalande 2006 Magnum, Haut-Médoc
description: The family behind the wine also owns Château Rauzan-Gassies - a winery recognised as a 'Second Growth' in the legendary 1855 Bordeaux classification. Aged in oak for 12 months and developing in the magnum bottle for 12 years - this is now a garnet-red wine with notes of red fruit, spice and cedar. Helpful hint: a wine of this maturity needs a bit of extra care. Drive the corkscrew in a little more than usual and ease the cork out gently. Decant it for at least an hour before serving.

name: Château Senejac 2016, Haut-Médoc
description: Château Sénéjac is a prime location for perfectly ripened Cabernet Sauvignon grapes. The esteemed Cordier family add dashes of Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot to perfect the blend before ageing it in oak for a year. This results in expressive notes of black cherries and blackberries, hints of vanilla and well integrated tannins. James Suckling gave this 2016 vintage a 92-93 point score.

name: Awatere River Single Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Awatere Valley
description: This Sauvignon is 100% sustainably produced, vegan friendly and most importantly delicious. Crafted from a single Awatere Valley vineyard, it has greater length and elegance than your average Marlborough Sauvignon. Enjoy concentrated gooseberry and lime zesty flavours along with a stone-like minerality

name: Indomita 'Gran Reserva' Pinot Noir 2019/20, Casblanca Valley
description: Chile's Casbalanca Valley has the perfect climate for ripening the infamously tricky Pinot Noir grape. A long, warm ripening season fills the fruit with delicious juice. You can taste the benefits in this luscious wine's flavours of strawberry, coffee and vanilla. Pair a glass of this with thinly-sliced Jamón ibérico.

name: La Fleur Godard 2016, Côtes de Bordeaux
description: Côtes de Bordeaux is made up of five lesser-known Bordeaux regions. These labels don't claim huge sums at auction houses. But if you're looking for a wine for the dinner table, not the library, you're in for terrific value. This is made by a member of the celebrated Thienpont family. It's fresh, fruity and wonderfully smooth. Expect notes of ripe red fruit, aromas of spice and a long, smooth finish.

name: Saint Clair 'Pioneer Block' Cabernet Merlot 2019, Hawkes Bay
description: Under the watchful eye of winemaker Hamish Clark, Saint Clair make it their mission to create internationally renowned wines from unlikely small parcels. For this wine, both the Cabernet Sauvignon and the Merlot grapes were sourced from a single vineyard in Hawkes Bay. Discover notes of plums, blackcurrant and blackberries. Enjoy with slow-cooked beef.

name: Collemassari Grattamacco Rosso 2019, Bolgheri
description: The Grattamacco estate was founded in Bolgheri in 1977. It was part of the 'Super Tuscan' movement that experimented with blending non-indigenous grapes with Sangiovese. Today, Super Tuscans are some of the most in-demand wines in the world. They've been continuously improving the Rosso over more than 38 years – and their expertise really shows. It's a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Sangiovese that's remarkably drinkable in its youth. It’s a dense, powerful wine with ripe tannins, aromas of dark fruits and chocolate and an excellent structure. Pair it with a hearty Tuscan stew.

name: Escarpment 'Kupe' Pinot Noir 2018, Martinborough
description: Escarpment was born from winemaker Larry McKenna's passion for the Martinborough wine region. After years of extensive research, he set up his own winery. He believes that the area is ideal for growing Pinot Noir. In his rendition of this delicate grape, expect fresh flavours and soft tannins. Wonderful with roast chicken.

name: Jim Barry ‘First Eleven’ Cabernet Sauvignon 2015, Coonawarra
description: The late Jim Barry is a godfather of Australian wines. His estate is now in the hands of his grandson, Tom, and was named Winery of the Year 2020 by Australia’s leading wine critic, James Halliday. Cabernet thrives in Coonawarra’s cool climate. So the Barry family planted vines in the Penola Cricket Ground here in 1999. The 'First Eleven' is only made in exceptional vintages, as a tribute to Coonawarra’s cricketing heroes. Expect fantastic notes of black fruit, tobacco and dried herbs. Sublime with braised beef.

name: Yalumba 'The Menzies' Cabernet Sauvignon 2015, Coonawarra
description: In 1961, Sir Robert Menzies said that Yalumba's Galway Vintage was the best Australian red he'd ever tasted. So they decided to make one even better and name it after this former Australian Prime Minister. They've done the name proud. This is structured and complex, with intense notes of red berries and a hint of oak influence. It's truly Coonawarra Cabernet at its best. Drink with tender beef short ribs.

name: Lismore Chardonnay 2018, Cape South Coast
description: Samantha O'Keefe is one of South Africa's most exciting winemakers. Sadly, her estate burned down in 2019. With the help of the South African wine industry, she's making a comeback. This was the final vintage made before the fire. And as her cellar is also no more, what we have are some of the last cases left. This is a Burgundy-style Chardonnay, with notes of citrus, brioche, honey and walnut. Taste this with creamy pasta.

name: Domaine Faiveley Nuit St Georges 1er Cru
description: Nuits-Saint-Georges is world-renowned for its complex Pinot Noir. This is made on premier cru vineyards in the appellation by a domaine that's made it since 1825. Expect aromas of spice, wood and toast that give way to flavours of blackberry. It's fantastic served at 14-16 degrees with turkey. Drink now or develop for a decade.

name: Alphonse Mellot 'Les Demoiselles' Organic Sancerre Rouge 2018
description: The Mellots have a rich 507-year-history in Sancerre. One member was even appointed as Louis XIV’s official wine advisor. Today, Alphonse Sr and Alphonse Jnr, the 18th and 19th generations make this red biodynamically and organically. It has hints of raspberries, cherries, vanilla and cinnamon. A great alternative to red Burgundy that's fantastic with duck. Serve at room temperature.

name: J Vineyards Russian River Valley Pinot Noir 2017
description: Winemaker Nicole Hitchcock's aim with J Vineyards is to nurture cool-climate varieties in the sun-drenched Russian River Valley. Here, the delicate Pinot Noir grape is handled with great care to produce an outstanding wine. Expect notes of blueberry, raspberry, dark cherry and mushroom. Exquisite with turkey and cranberry sauce.

name: Rémi Seguin Morey-Saint-Denis 2018
description: What Morey-Saint-Denis lacks in size, it more than makes up for in character. This village is known for its complex red wines. And this intriguing, perfumed red Burgundy is among the best we've found. Expect notes of raspberry and kirsch, with a hint of aniseed. A delicious alternative to Gevry-Chambertin at great value. Try it with pork.

name: Moët Grand Vintage Rose 2012
description: This is only made during exceptional years. And 2012 marks Moet & Chandon's 43rd Grand Vintage Rosé. The 2012 vintage was marked excellent Pinot Meunier, which stands out in notes of blood orange, cherry and grapefruit. Extraordinary on its own or with dessert.

name: Cloudy Bay Sauvignon Blanc Magnum 2020, Marlborough
description: Cloudy Bay is widely credited with introducing New Zealand wines to the world with their signature Sauvignon Blanc. The 2020 vintage saw near-perfect vineyard conditions, producing a bright and focused wine. Expect to find notes of ripe grapefruit, lime and passionfruit. Wonderful with salmon and asparagus.

name: Veuve Clicquot Extra Brut Extra Old Champagne
description: Made from fruit souced entirely from Grand Cru and Premier Cru vineyards, this is an exceptionally high-quality Champagne, which has undergone extended cellarage. Supremely refined and complex, with notes of flowers, dried fruit and toasted brioche.

name: Château Branaire-Ducru 2014, St Julien
description: If you’re looking to discover St Julien, look no further than Château Branaire-Ducru. They’re one of the more affordable estates in this otherwise pricey region. But that doesn’t mean they hold back on flavour. Expect a combination of ripe cherries and raspberries, as well as aromas of lavender and tobacco. Budding collectors take note: this will age excellently for 12 years. Enjoy at room temperature with an equally smoky truffle pasta.

name: Greywacke Chardonnay 2016, Marlborough
description: After 25 years as head winemaker at Cloudy Bay, Kevin Judd founded Greywacke with his wife, Kimberly. And together they create fantastic Chardonnay. It has ripe notes of pineapple, grapefruit, red apple, hazelnut and spice. Try this with fish cakes.

name: Stag's Leap 'Hands of Time' Chardonnay 2018, Napa Valley
description: Stag’s Leap shocked the world and beat the finest Bordeaux in the legendary Judgment of Paris in 1976. Ever since, theirs have become some of the most collected wines in the world. The Hands of Time is your chance to taste what all the fuss is about at a smaller sum. This is a medium-bodied, soft Chardonnay with hints of pear, pineapple, orange blossom, and a touch of oak on the finish. It’s exceptional with buttery pasta.

name: Cloudy Bay Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Cloudy Bay's huge success is largely down to groundbreaking Sauvignon Blanc. This is one of New Zealand's most celebrated wines and a real benchmark for quality. And remarkably, it will develop with age over the next five years. You can say the same for very few Sauvignon Blancs. Expect a complex and layered Sauvignon, with notes of ripe lemon, lime, passionfruit, peach and freshly cut grass. Serve this chilled with salmon.

name: Santa Rita 120 Sauvignon Blanc 2020 Central Valley
description: Santa Rita's 120 range places the emphasis firmly on lively, up-front fruit flavours, and great value. Maximum fruit character is obtained by hand-harvesting and cold fermentation of the Sauvignon grapes, and the addition of 2% Semillon helps keep the blend well-rounded. Crisp and refreshing, this wine offers an abundance of grapefruit and lemon fruit flavours, delivered in a softer, more tropical fruit texture. A great party wine, endlessly gluggable on its own, or alongside seafood.

name: Silver Ghost Cabernet Sauvignon 2020, Central Valley
description: Quality and cost aren’t necessarily linked. This wine is made for us by one of the most prestigious winemaking families in Chile, the Arestis. Their winery is 100% female owned, and they have and instinctive understanding of how to get the very best out of their grapes, while still keeping prices wallet-friendly. Chilean wine is always incredible value – but it’s the Arestis’ skill which makes this particular wine such great quality at such a great price. It’s from the Central Valley which is cool but has loads of sunshine – perfect conditions for fresh, juicy Cabernet Sauvignon like this. It’s really black-fruity – bursting with blackberry and plum flavours. With a lovely silky texture, it’s seriously drinkable red which makes it the ideal party wine. It’s great alone – or with burgers.

name: Ségla 2012/13, Margaux
description: Ségla is the second wine of the famous Margaux second growth Château Rauzan-Ségla. More accessible than the first wine, this is does not receive as much new oak, so tannins are riper and smoother making the Ségla ripe and ready for drinking. It is a wine with excellent concentration and poise. Showing fragrant notes of ripe fruits, violets and cedar with beautiful length and delicate tannins. With great structure and balance and a long finish A great example of good value Margaux drinking well now, this wine will also soften further and improve over the next 7-8 years

name: Nicolas Potel Bourgogne Chardonnay 2018
description: There's nothing that gets your tastebuds tingling like an insider-secret bargain. The bottle is simply labelled as 'Bourgogne Chardonnay', but it's a blend of seriously high-quality fruit from mature vines in prime vineyards in the Mâcon, Côte Chalonnaise and the Côte d'Or. Unusually, it's not oaked at all. This means its incredible cirtussy freshness is front and centre. It's a really delicious wine. Apple. Blossom. Marzipan. A long, almondy, slightly creamy finish. It tastes far more expensive than it is, which is always a winner in our book. It's guaranteed to transform a humble chicken salad from simple to spectacular.

name: Haut Poitou Sauvignon Blanc 2019 Pierre Sauvion
description: Covering an area to the north of Poitiers, in the southeastern part of the Loire, Haut Poitou has been a well known winemaking territory for centuries, but was created an AOC as recently as 2011 as a result of its wines' notable levels of quality. A crisp, dry and well structured white reminiscent of the classic Sauvignons of the central Loire Valley. Full of incisive citrus fruit character, lifted herbaceous aromatics and cleansing acidity. Drink with fresh seafood.

name: Château de Pennautier 2017 Cabardès
description: This release from Château de Pennautier has been crafted by talented winemaker, Bertrand Seube, formerly of the world-famous Château Mouton-Rothschild! So, you can be assured you're in exceptionally talented, experienced hands with this release of Château de Pennautier.
