This ripe Cabardès has a charmingly rustic palate of mulberry, raspberry and soothing spices. It's lengthy finish makes it a stellar companion for richly-flavoured food.

name: L'Etoile de Begude Chardonnay 2019 Limoux
description: Domaine Begude is a small hilltop domaine very close to the town of Limoux, owned and managed by British couple James and Catherine Kinglake. The area is known for its relatively cool climate and hence its ability to produce crisp and aromatic Chardonnay, with characteristics to rival fine Burgundies, but at significantly more palatable prices. Enjoy with butter roasted chicken

name: Marquis de Pennautier Chardonnay Viognier 2019 PGI Pays d'Oc
description: From the estate of the historic 17th century Château de Pennautier in Cabardès, this is a bright and fruity Chardonnay-Viognier blend. Expect notes of apple and citrus from the Chardonnay, and hints of apricot from the Viognier. Try with meatier fish and Mediterranean-inspired dished such as ratatouille.

name: Laurent Perrier La Cuvee Magnum Champagne
description: Laurent Perrier La Cuvee NV Champagne Magnum 150cl is Laurent-Perrier's signature champagne, so called as it is crafted using the purest grape juice obtained during the gentle first pressing. La Cuvée defines the House style and serves as an ideal introduction to our wines with its characteristic lightness, freshness and elegance.

name: Moët & Chandon Brut Impérial NV Champagne
description: Moët is the ultimate celebratory fizz. Not only are they credited with inventing that louche party staple, the Champagne pyramid; the celebratory ‘spray’ of Champagne familiar from sports podiums the world over is also down to Moët. At Le Mans in 1967 the winner was handed a jeroboam of their Brut NV – which he promptly used to drench the crowd. From that moment, Moët & Chandon has been inextricably linked with celebration – with explosions of joy. With moments which deserve a truly great Champagne. But not only does their famous label put you in a party mood – it’s also a fantastic wine. It’s really crisp and refreshing, but rich and ripe, full of stone fruit flavours too. Think peach and lemon zest. With briochey, biscuity aromas, it’s as decadent as it is moreish. Ideal with canapés.

name: Devaux 'Oeil de Perdrix' Rosé Champagne
description: What do church organs and high-powered motorbikes have in common? They’re winemaker Michel Parisot’s hobbies – his downtime from making sublime Champagnes. It’s a fitting portrait of a winemaker whose trademark approach to this most traditional of fizzes is decidedly unconventional. Michel is all about maximum flavour. He picks the Pinot Noir grapes as late as possible for as much aromatic flavour as possible. Michel then uses only the first press of the grapes – guaranteeing freshness. He also ages the wines for a minimum of four years – far longer than the requisite thirty months. The result is a rosé Champagne which punches well above its price point. It has moreish flavours of redcurrant and raspberries. And the creamy texture is perfect with puddings, especially fruit-based ones.

name: Veuve Clicquot 2012 Champagne
description: Veuve Clicquot vintage Champagne is seriously rare. They only make it in exceptional years, so you can be sure that it's something special. Each release is eagerly awaited by Champagne fans in the know - and it doesn't disappoint. This is clear, pure and intense with a complex nose of brioch and biscuit. Its balancing salinity makes it an ideal pairing for oysters.

name: Taittinger Brut Réserve NV Champagne
description: The first time Pierre Taittinger visited Champagne was as an injured officer of the French army during WWI. Château de la Marquetterie was being used as a French command post at the time. Young Pierre promised himself, that if he ever had the chance, he would buy the estate for his family. And in 1932 he did just that. Luckily, the sale included some of the original vineyards which had been planted by Benedictine monks in the 1730s. So, wine merchant Pierre was able to launch a Champagne label under his family name. Chardonnay’s 60% of the blend, meaning it’s full of honeysuckle, brioche and pear flavours. Bright. Fresh. It’s a classic NV – and not just because it was Bond’s favourite in Fleming’s original novels. Try it with something equally decadent – cheese gougères fresh from the oven.

name: Pol Roger Réserve Champagne
description: Pol Roger is famous as Churchill’s favourite Champagne – they’ve even named a cuvée after him – and it’s been the fizz of choice for royal weddings. In the 1850s, the savvy founder noted that British tastes favoured a drier style – Brut – and he prioritised perfecting it. It was against the fashion of the times, but it was suited to Pol’s incredibly deep cellars. Thirty-three meters below street level, they’re three degrees cooler than other Champagne cellars – a tiny variation which makes all the difference. It slows the fermentation right down, the longer process intensifing flavours and creating the extra-fine bubbles which make this such a lush glassful. It’s full of pear, mango and honeysuckle flavours – but rich and biscuity. Sublime with dressed crab.

name: Ruinart Rosé NV Champagne
description: Ruinart are Champagne's oldest house. Their Rosé was first released in 1962, and it's gone from strength to strength since. It's initially made as a Blanc de Blancs Champagne, before a dash of Pinot Noir is added to the mix. This gives it a beautiful depth and a sublime golden pink colour. Expect a subtle nose of cherry and freshly-picked red berries, leading to a delightfully smooth finish. Decanter's James Button gave it 90 points.

name: Nicolas Feuillatte Brut Champagne
description: If you order a glass of Brut NV in a wine bar in Paris, more likely than not it’ll be a glass of Nicolas Feuillatte. It’s far and away the most popular Champagne label in France – and the third most popular in the world. It was founded by instant-coffee magnate and jet setter Nicolas Feuillatte. Jackie Kennedy and Lauren Bacall once flitted in and out of Nicolas’ Fifth Avenue apartment, glasses of fizz in hand. But it’s not just famous because of celebrity connections. Nicolas Feuillatte is one of a kind – the only co-operative in Champagne. It’s been known for it's delicious, incredibly well-made Champagnes since the 1980s. This Brut NV is full of fruit – white peaches, apples and raspberries. Ripe and creamy, it’s still got a great citrussy zingy. It’s perfect with fish and chips.

name: Incanta Pinot Noir 2020, Romania
description: Love Pinot Noir but don’t love the price tag? Romania is a where insiders look for great value, great tasting Pinot. It’s in the swing of a post-Soviet winemaking renaissance – and Englishman Philip Cox, and his Romanian wife, Elvira, are ready to sing its praises to the rooftops. Grapes have been grown on the site of their vineyard since 1447 – but it’s only in the past three decades that Philip and Elvira have united the lattice of tiny plots owned by family growers under a label big enough to export. This Pinot is deliciously fresh and juicy. Full of black cherry, raspberry and cinnamon. It’s a wonderfully fruity, light and approachable Pinot Noir. And it’s incredible value at a price traditional Pinot Noir regions like Burgundy could never offer. Try slightly chilled with charcuterie.

name: Balfour 1503 Brut, England
description: This superb English sparkling comes from one of the country’s leading producers. Owen and Fergus Elias have been winemakers at Balfour for the last 10 years. So they know the best local vineyards for crafting traditional method sparkling that rivals top Champagne houses. For the 1503 Brut, they picked Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from the beautiful Hush Heath Estate in Kent. The resulting wine shows crisp notes of apple and citrus fruit, pepper and thyme. Complex, toasty and the perfect aperitif to any meal.

name: Nyetimber Classic Cuvée, England
description: When Cherie Spriggs’ parents gave her a bottle of Nyetimber, they sparked a passion for the wine she would one day come to make. She and her winemaker husband joined Nyetimber in 2007 – and since then, the wines have gone from strength to strength, winning buckets of awards – and accolades for Cherie herself. She was the non-Champagne winemaker to ever be named ‘Sparkling Winemaker of the Year’. It’s not hard to see why. ‘Nitimbreha’ was first recorded on the site of the winery in the 1086 Domesday book. Though the first vines weren’t planted there for nearly 900 years, Cherie evokes a real sense of bucolic England in her wines. It’s ripe with orchard fruit flavours – like the golden apples picked nearby. Honeyed, with fresh pastry-like aromas, it’s delicious with grilled trout.

name: Saint Clair 'Pioneer Block 25' Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Wairau Valley
description: Saint Clair have won more awards for their Sauvignon Blancs than any other producer in Marlborough. In fact, they’ve been winning awards since their very first vintage. No wonder when they’re pioneers in the region. They’ve been growing grapes there since the 1970s, so they really know the region inside out. They decided to put this knowledge to good use, creating single vineyard – ‘block’ – wines which are expressive of their particular patch. This Sauvignon is one of them. Because it’s so selective, they only make it in the very best vintages. It’s worth the wait. It really bursts with that classic Marlborough fruit – think passionfruit, gooseberry and freshly cut grass. Bright. Fruity. Intensely refreshing. Perfect with Thai food.

name: Mud House Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Mud House has quickly become one of the biggest names in Marlborough. They’re known for their zingy ‘savvy’ – deliciously fresh wine which is absolutely textbook Marlborough Sauvignon. No wonder. Marlborough is everything to Mud House’s founder. They fell in love with the region twenty years ago, and after preparing the land for vines they built their home using the local earth. It’s the ‘mud house’ we all know and love. When we introduced it a few years ago, Mud House quickly became a firm customer favourite. It’s incredibly drinkable – the perfect wine to have just-in-case in the fridge door. Intensely refreshing, it’s overflowing with exotic, tropical fruit aromas. Think passionfruit, guava, grapefruit, melon and citrus. Fresh. Fruity. What more could you ask for?

name: Villa Maria 'Cellar Selection' Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: Like another world-renowned brand we could mention, Villa Maria was founded in a garage. George Fistonich’s garage in an Auckland suburb, to be precise. He's now ‘Sir George’, and his backyard experiment is the most awarded winery in New Zealand. Of course, it’s mainly known for its benchmark Sauvignons – and the Cellar Selection is particularly special. Think gooseberry and passionfruit with the zing of grapefruit. The grapes are even harvested in the cool of the night to make sure as many aromas as possible are preserved. If you love Kiwi Sauvignon this is a no-brainer upgrade – it’s got all the flavour dials turned way up.The Cellar Selection wines were created expressly for food. Kiwis love Asian-inspired foods, so this Sauvignon is extra aromatic which works really well with spice.

name: Villa Maria Cellar Selection Pinot Noir 2018, Marlborough
description: This wine is made by blending grapes of selected vineyards in the Awatere and Wairau Valleys of Marlborough. The vineyards are managed to the highest standard to ensure quality. It is medium bodied with beautifully poised red fruit and very well integrated oak flavours. The crisp acidity and fine grained tannins provide a delightfully clean long finish of considerable finesse. Serve with rabbit, veal or duck.

name: The King's Legacy Chardonnay 2018, Marlborough
description: This Chardonnay is sourced from two vineyards, one a mature plot in the centre of the Wairau Valley, the other a plot of younger vines in Brancott Valley. The grapes were fermented in large French oak casks using predominantly wild yeasts and allowed 10 months' on lees. An easily enjoyable Chardonnay that combines fresh apple, nectarine and tangerine flavours with subtle creaminess, hints of toasted almonds and a gently spicy complexity. Try this with seared scallops served with black pudding.

name: Mud House Claim 431 Pinot Noir 2014 Central Otago
description: This is a single-vineyard wine from Central Otago, arguably the home of New Zealand's finest Pinot Noir. The Claim 431 vineyard is planted with specially selected Pinot Noir clones with different ripening characteristics, which lends complexity and structure to the blend. Aromas of plum and boysenberry give way to subtle aromas of oak toast and spice. The palate offers intense fruit yet an elegant structure with fine tannins and acidity. Mud House recommends serving with slow-cooked pulled chipotle beef.

name: The Ned 'Southern Valleys' Pinot Noir 2019, Marlborough
description: Marlborough Pinot Noir is a bit of an unsung gem. It’s often overshadowed by its regional sister, Sauvignon Blanc – but we think it’s fantastic if you love deeply fruity, silky reds. This is from the Southern Valleys, the prime spot for pernickety Pinot Noir which needs very specific soil and climate conditions to thrive. The grapes ripen slowly, developing really concentrated, berry-fruit flavours which are a trademark of the Marlborough style. Added to which, Brent Marris is the master of Marlborough – he knows how to get the very best out of his grapes in these famous valleys. It’s a real corker of the wine. Ripe. Juicy. Fruity. It’s full of raspberry, strawberry and red plum flavours, with a nicely savoury quality and silky texture. Perfect with slow-cooked beef ragu.

name: Catena Cabernet Sauvignon 2019 Mendoza
description: Catena's Agrelo vineyards possess ideal soil and micro-climatic conditions for growing classic varieties such as Cabernet Sauvignon, and are renowned for producing wines of great finesse and elegance. Full bodied, with rich, dark, supremely concentrated cassis-scented black fruit and a warm, spicy finish. Excellent with steaks, roast beef or, if you are feeling adventurous, haggis.

name: Two Hands 'Sexy Beast' Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, McLaren Vale
description: Two Hands is already one of the finest Shiraz producers in Australia. Turning its hand to Cabernet Sauvignon, it's crafted a red that's chock full of blackcurrant, mocha, mint and a hint of graphite. Scoring a solid 90 Parker Points with this vintage alone, it's clear the winery is master of many.

name: Kangarilla Road Chardonnay 2020, Adelaide Hills
description: Kangarilla Road is a Majestic classic. It's made by the acclaimed winemaker, Kevin O'Brien, mainly from cool-climate Adelaide Hills vineyards. It's also treated to a restrained use of oak. This means it's sleek, mineral and elegant. The best of modern-day Australian winemaking. It's dry and classy, with notes of lemon sherbert, melon, pear and nectarine. Try it with creamy chicken dishes.

name: Rustenberg 'John X Merriman' 2019, Stellenbosch
description: Murray Barlow's wines have won heaps of critical praise. He's the only winemaker to twice win Young Winemaker of the Year at South Africa’s biggest wine awards event, Diners Club. This wine commemorates John Xavier Merriman, the last prime minister of The Cape, and it's surely a fitting tribute. Expect aromas of mint, black fruits, spice and cigar box. This wine suits robust meat dishes, especially roast beef.

name: Rustenberg Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Western Cape
description: Murray Barlow's wines are some of Stellenbosch’s finest. He's rightfully won not just a cult following, but heaps of critical praise. In fact, he is the only winemaker to win Young Winemaker of the Year twice at South Africa’s biggest wine-awards event, Diners Club. He's excelled yet again with this Stellenbosch Sauvignon. It's fresh and crisp, with notes of melon, gooseberry, pineapple and passionfruit. Try it with Thai food.

name: Capaia One 2018, New Philadelphia
description: The grapes were picked at optimum ripeness before undergoing natural fermentation in large French Oak fermenters. Made from selected blocks from Capaia terroir and only using the free running juice for this full-bodied red wine, it shows complexity and structure with rich flavours of ripe fruit and firm tannins.This powerful blend with almost four equal parts of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Shiraz and Petit Verdot puts up a show, characterised by dark, black fruit, savoury and spicy notes all well integrated with the oak from spending 18 months in 60% new French oak barriques.

name: Santa Rita Medalla Real Chardonnay 2020, Limari Valley
description: Sourced mainly from the Limarí Valley, the northernmost wine region in Chile, this is a delicious cool-climate Chardonnay. Expect green-apple freshness alongside a creamy palate gained from oak ageing.

name: Luis Felipe Edwards Gran Reserva Chardonnay 2020 Casablanca Valley
description: The Luis Felipe Edwards winery is no stranger to the praise of both Robert Parker and Tim Atkin MW. But it still makes fantastic produce at a pocket-friendly price. Punchy notes of stone fruit and ripe apple are licked by a rich slather of vanilla. This would cost gallons more in the Old World.

name: Vandenberg 'Six Brothers' Pinot Noir 2018, Tasmania
description: You might think that Australia's too warm for the demanding Pinot Noir grape. But it has a long, luxurious ripening season on the cool-climate island of Tasmania. Taste the benefit in these notes of mulberry, cranberry, raspberry, dried herbs and toasty oak. This is at its best with duck breast.

name: El Aviador Blanco 2019, La Mancha
description: From La Mancha, this is is a crisp and expressive blend of Chardonnay, Verdejo and Viura. Enjoy bright notes of apple, peach and tropical fruit alongside a hint of minerality.

name: Ara 'Sightlines' Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Marlborough
description: From the award-winning Ara winery this is a benchmark example of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc. Expect vibrant notes of white peach, lemon and thyme alongside a fresh acidity, and crisp finish.

name: Escarpment Chardonnay 2018, Martinborough
description: Larry Mckenna became one of New Zealand's top winemakers with his Pinot Noir. But its also Burgundy-inspired Chardonnay that he hits dizzying heights with. His Escarpment Chardonnay is aged on lees and fermented Burgundy style to add a complex creaminess to its palate of white stone fruits.

name: Château Marsac Seguineau 2015, Margaux
description: From the stellar 2015 vintage and just hitting its stride this is rich and indulgent with bright fruit flavours of cherry and blackcurrant enhanced by notes of cedar and vanilla

name: Château Noble Meynard 2010, Bordeaux Supérieur
description: A Bordeaux from the perfect 5/5 2010 vintage (maybe even vintage of the century!) at this low price? Your eyes don't deceive you! From 20-year-old vines in the heart of the Gironde, the world's fine-wine heart, comes deep notes of black fruit, a scattering of sweet spices, polished tannins and a lengthy finish.

name: Chateau de Chassagne Montrachet 2018
description: To many, Burgundy's Chassagne-Montrachet is home to the world's greatest white wines. Take a sip of this, and you'll see why. It's delicate and fruity, with elegant hints of peach, white flowers, almond and cedar, and a very long finish. Outstanding Chardonnay that's best with king prawns.

name: Stone Arka Chardonnay 2019
description: With its industry dating back to 400 BC, Bulgaria was once one of the biggest wine-producing countries in the world. With its long, warm summers similar to those in Burgundy, it's the pocket-friendly spot for a fan of refined Chardonnay. Stone Arka entwines classic white Burgundy characteristics of ripe stone fruit with a delicate nuttiness.

name: Emperors Prize Pinot Noir 2019
description: From one of the best vintages in recent memory this Yarra Valley Pinot Noir is packed with red cherry flavours and floral notes that linger and finish with a touch of spice. Drink this by itself and savour every drop; or alongside roast duck with plum sauce.

name: Vasse Felix Filius Cabernet Merlot 2018, Margaret River
description: The ocean-breeze-cooled Margaret River is Australia's finest wine region. Here, Virginia Willcock, one of the most awarded winemakers in Australia, makes this delicious Cabernet-Merlot blend. Expect excellently woven hints of plum, mocha and cedar.

name: Yalumba Y Series Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: The grapes behind Yalumba's Y Series Sauvignon Blanc are picked in the cool of night to bring every hint of grapefruit, Granny Smith apple and white blossom to your glass. It's also stirred on its complexity-building lees. So you're in for a structured sip.

name: Dog Point Pinot Noir, 2016 Marlborough
description: Dog Point is a joint venture between James Healy and Ivan Sutherland, respectively former winemaker and vineyard manager at Cloudy Bay, started in 2003. The wines come from selected parcels of old vines, and are made with a minimalist approach, including fermentation by natural yeasts. This Pinot Noir spends 18 months in a mixture of new and old French oak, for a classically elegant, sweet and savoury structure.

name: Orin Swift Palermo 2017
description: Unashamedly powerful and fruity this is resplendent with cherry and raspberry flavours followed buy notes of sweet spice and star anise.

name: Definition Pouilly Fumé 2019, Loire
description: Pouilly Fumé is one of the Loire Valley's most revered whites and rivalled only by Sancerre when it comes to Sauvignon Blanc. Fumé is French for smoke, and it is in Pouilly Fumé Sauvignon that you find the iconic 'gun smoke' aroma. The Definition captures the charm of this special region with fragrant notes of citrus and white peach, a creamy mouthfeel, and a delicate smoky tint.

name: Smith & Wright Chardonnay 2019
description: Smith & Wright is the creation of a duo with a passion for enjoyable, fruit-forward wine that's best enjoyed with company. Expect juicy notes of peach and lemon blossom, a creamy slather of vanilla and a crisp, refreshing finish.

name: Ayala 'Le Blanc de Blancs' Champagne 2013
description: Ayala, a Champagne house now owned by Bollinger, produces 'Le Blanc de Blancs' in limited quantities only in the best vintages. Winemaker Caroline Latrive, who has rich experience in a wide variety of the leading Champagne houses, picks Chardonnay grapes only from Grand Cru vineyards to craft it. Expect a luxurious, rich palate of peach and passionfruit, a dry minerality and a silk-smooth finish.

name: Wakefield 'Jaraman' Cabernet Sauvignon 2017, Australia
description: Wakefield are Cabernet specialists. And it really shows here. Made from Cabernet grapes from Coonawarra and Clare Valley and aged in French oak, their 'Jaraman' Cabernet Sauvignon is a big, heady concoction of plum, eucalyptus, oak and herbs. It's complex, elegant and fantastic with beef ribs.

name: Mud House Rosé 2020, Marlborough
description: You may know Mud House best for their Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc. They're no one-trick ponies, as this rosé really rewards the adventurous drinker. It’s made of 95% Sauvignon Blanc and 5% Pinot Noir. It’s lively and tropical, with aromas of fresh red berries, and a long finish. If you like Kiwi Sauvignon, this is the perfect pink. Try it with chicken salad.

name: Pommery Rosé Brut NV
description: Madame Pommery, the inventor of dry (brut) Champagne, was known as 'Lady of the Roses'. This certainly doesn't let her legacy down. It's an arrow-precise palate of stone fruit and strawberry that's backed by a delicious minerality and a fine mousse.

name: Aloxe-Corton 1er Cru 'Les Chaillots' 2016 Louis Latour
description: Aloxe-Corton produces some of Burgundy's finest Pinot Noir. It's also the home of the famous Latour family, and this wine demonstrates how good both can be. The name ''Les Chaillots'' refers to the pebbly soil in the vineyard, as ''caillou'' means pebble in French. Fermented in large open top oak vats and aged for 10 to 12 months in French oak barrels, this complex Pinot has an intense aromas of black cherry and undergrowth. On the palate spices mix with black cherry and blackcurrant fruit, liquorice and nutmeg.

name: Pouilly Fuissé 2019 Domaine Trouillet
description: Domaine Trouillet is a small boutique estate in the Maconnais. Now in the hands of the third and fourth generation of the family, its specialism is Pouilly Fuissé. Trouillet's vineyards are on average 40-50 years old and the lower yields from this venerable vines produces complex, full-flavoured whites. With aromas of almond, hazelnut and citrus, supported by a peach, brioche and honey notes, this is Pouilly Fuissé at its best.

name: Morey-Saint-Denis 'Très Vieilles Vignes' 2015 Henri Pion, Burgundy
description: Nestled next to Gevrey-Chambertin, Morey-Saint-Denis is where you'll find the best value red Burgundy. Here, the Henri Pion winery hand-picks Pinot Noir grapes from a single plot of 80-year-old vines. Once they're pressed, the wine is aged in French oak for 14 months. This results in a complex, deep Pinot Noir, full of black fruit and vanilla, with a luxuriously long finish.

name: Talbott 'Kali Hart' Chardonnay 2016, Santa Lucia Highlands
description: Forget everything you think you know about American Chardonnay. Talbott Vineyards put the now famous Santa Lucia Highlands on the winemaking map. The cool climate on their vineyards brings out the Chardonnay grape’s subtle and elegant aromas. Made in a Burdgundian style, this wine is the most fruit-forward in Talbott Vineyards’ portfolio. This is bright and refreshing, with notes of white stone fruit, tropical fruit and vanilla. Sublime with white fish.

name: Alphonse Mellot 'Les Demoiselles' Organic Sancerre 2018
description: The Mellots have a rich 507-year-history in Sancerre. One member was even appointed as Louis XIV’s official wine advisor. Today, Alphonse Sr and Alphonse Jnr, the 18th and 19th generations, together craft biodynamic, organic Sancerre with a distinctly creamy character. This is deep and rich, with flavours of pink grapefruit, gunflint and oak. Serve at ten degrees with shellfish.

name: Chorey-les-Beaune 'Vieilles Vignes' 2019 Domaine Machard de Gramont
description: Domaine Marchard de Gramont picks every Pinot Noir grape by hand before it ages this light, smooth Pinot Noir in its 11th-century cellar for up to 14 months in both French and American oak. Expect notes of red and black fruits, elegant tannins and a delicious aftertaste from this great-value red Burgundy.

name: Rimapere Sauvignon Blanc 2019 Baron Edmond de Rothschild, Marlborough,
description: The Rothschild family are pre-eminent in the world of wine and have vineyards across the globe. Baron Benjamin, son and heir to Baron Edmund de Rothschild, purchased a prime vineyard in the cool Rapaura district of Marlborough to make this Sauvignon. Working with Terry Peabody of Craggy Range, this is an elegant and mineral style with a lemon peel and grapefruit character and crisp acidity.

name: "Sur le Marne" Chardonnay Rijckaert - F. Rouve, Cotes de Jura
description: Florent Rouve is a winemaker on the up, and this is your chance to taste his expertise before prices inevitably rise. He's made this close to the Swiss border, in the foothills of the Alps, in Côtes du Jura, and aged it in small oak barrels. The result is fresh, buttery and mineral, with citrus and floral aromas. It's indulgent enough to stand up to creamy chicken dishes.

name: Hunawihr 'Cuvée Prestige' Pinot Noir 2018, Alsace
description: There's more to Alsace than active holidays and Gothic cathedrals. In the village of Hunawihr, you'll discover the vineyards behind this excellent value Pinot Noir. It has aromas of cherry and raspberry, silky tannins and a clean finish. Pair it with red meat or, if you're feeling brave, a Chinese takeaway.

name: Hambledon Vineyards Classic Cuvée Rosé, Hampshire
description: Hambledon is the UK's oldest commercial vineyard, and its head winemaker, Hervé Jestin, was cellarmaster at the Duval Leroy Champagne house for over 20 years. The Classic Cuvée Rosé, a blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, has fresh notes of strawberries and toast and fine bubbles. Interestingly, it was served at one of The Queen's state dinners at Buckingham Palace.

name: David Finlayson Cabernet Sauvignon 2019, Stellenbosch
description: David Finlayson earned his stripes at Château Margaux, one of the very best estates in Bordeaux. He's taken his Cabernet expertise home to create this fresh, expressive red. It has plenty of black fruits, aromas of coffee and vanilla and a herbaceous backbone.

name: Framingham 'Nobody's Hero' Pinot Noir 2019, Marlborough
description: This Marlborough winery takes pride in doing things a little differently. Not only do they hold an outdoor rock concert every year, their approach winemaking is also unconventional. They now produce some of the best value Pinot Noir in New Zealand. This is lush and rounded with notes of vibrant red fruits, oak and chocolate. Pair this with roast chicken.

name: Howard Park ‘Miamup’ Chardonnay 2019, Margaret River
description: Howard Park began as a vision and a shed in 1986. The Burch family then spent 30 years discovering the best of the Margaret River region. Today they’re one of the most celebrated producers in Australia. And in 2009, they were invited into Australia's First Families of Wine, joining 11 of the biggest winemaking names in the country. This is a delightfully complex Chardonnay, with notes of stone fruits, grapefruit, honeysuckle and hazelnut. Pair with roast chicken now or cellar for a few more years.

name: Emiliana 'Elemental' Organic Gran Reserva Cabernet 2018, Maipo Valley
description: Emiliana were voted Winery of the Year 2016 by Wines of Chile. They're leading the way with delicious organic wines at great value. This Maipo Valley Cabernet is a case in point. It's velvet-smooth and intensely flavoured, with flavours of blackcurrant, strawberry and chocolate.

name: Luis Felipe Edwards Gran Reserva Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Leyda Valley
description: Chile's Leyda Valley is a region on the up. Its cooler, Pacific climate is ideal for Sauvignon Blanc grapes. Luis Felipe Edwards' vineyards are some of the closest to the sea in the valley. And the man making this wine on them, Nicolas Bizzarri, earned his stripes at the prestigious Robert Mondavi winery in California. This is a juicy, tropical Sauvignon, with notes of pineapple, passionfruit and lemon. Simply excellent with fish.

name: Indomita 'Duette' Pinot Noir 2019, Casablanca Valley
description: The delicate Pinot Noir grape is pampered by Pacific breezes on Chile's cool-climate Casablanca Valley. So you can expect a textured, delicate sip from the Duette. It has flavours of red berries, dried cherry, gunflint and tobacco. Excellent with duck.

name: Château Fontesteau 2013, Haut Médoc
description: Bordeaux takes the crown for the most convoluted labelling system in France. For starters, it’s still based on an 1885 classification where châteaux were classed as different ‘growths’. It’s enough to give anyone a headache – and that’s before you’ve even opened the bottle. Luckily, ‘Crus Bourgeois’ is a label which can steer you through the hundreds of Petits Châteaux like a beacon of quality. And Château Fontesteau is one of the classics. It’s old-school claret, with a robust structure and fine tannins which will last for years. Being Haut-Médoc, it’s mainly Cabernet Sauvignon, so it’s full of those classic Cabernet cassis flavours. Rich. Full. Concentrated. It’s nice and red-fruity – think cherry and strawberry. Perfect with a good cheese board.

name: Definition Pauillac 2015
description: The Definition range brings essential elements of the world's greatest wine styles to Majestic customers. Pauillac where you'll find some of Bordeaux's finest reds. So to create the Definition, we went to one of the most prestigious Châteaux in the appellation. This is medium-to-full-bodied, with ripe notes of black fruit, fine tannins and a long finish. Drink now with red meat or cellar for five years.

name: Jackson Estate 'Somerset' Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Only a few hundred cases of Jackson Estate's Somerset Sauvignon Blanc are made each year from a small, hand-harvested single vineyard in the Waihopai Valley. Full of personality, this is a distinctive style of Sauvignon Blanc with concentrated lime and gooseberry flavours, a mineral complexity and a lasting finish

name: Ropiteau Frères Chassagne-Montrachet 2018
description: In 1848, Jean Ropiteau saw opportunity. The arrival of Meursault's first railway meant that he could make his white Burgundy successful all over the world. So he bought his own winery and began making oak barrels. Today, this estate makes some of Burgundy's most respected wines. It really shows in this Chassagne-Montrachet. It's bold, rich and ripe, with flavours of stone fruit, honeysuckle and buttery almond. Serve at 12-13 degrees and pair with East Asian food.

name: Famille Brocard Chablis Grand Cru 2013, Blanchot
description: This Chablis is made by a father-and-son winemaking duo on the grand-cru climat of Blanchot. Wines made here are truly distinct, showing a lighter style with excellent minerality. This is a fruity, crisp Chablis, with flavours of citrus, white flowers and white pepper. Serve chilled with white fish.

name: Domaine Robert Sirugue 'Les Trois Terroirs' 2018, Vosne-Romanée
description: Vosne-Romanée is a region internationally renowned for its smooth and silky red Burgundy. This is made on three areas of the region specifically selected for their characteristic structure and elegance. Ripe red fruit and spices are the main notes in this excellent Pinot Noir. It will develop aromas of forest floor and leather as it ages over ten years. Divine at 14-16 degrees with roasted lamb.

name: Domaine Joly Puligny-Montrachet 2019
description: Domaine Joly have a strong historical presence in Burgundy. Their distinct style is clean and pure Puligny-Montrachet with a delicious edge. This has flavours of hazelnut and stone fruit, and a clean, pure finish. Its great structure shows potential ageing. Serve at 10-13 degrees with white fish or cellar for ten years.

name: Colle Massari 'Grattamacco 35th Anniversary' Bolgheri Superiore 2018
description: Grattamacco only began in 1977, but they're is already putting Bolgheri on the map. This Cabernet blend is their magnum opus. It shows notes of blackcurrant with a nose of Mediterranean herbs. Expect a taut and balsamic finish in this elegant wine. Decant for an hour and enjoy at room temperature with grilled fillet steak.

name: Domaine Georges Lignier et Fils Volnay Premier Cru 2017
description: Georges Lignier inherited a few vines from his father at the turn of the last century. Today, this domaine is in the hands of his grandson, George Jnr, and it continues on its upward trajectory. This is made from Premier Cru vineyards in Volnay, the village known for some of the smoothest red Burgundy going. Expect a perfume of sweet cherry and raspberry fruit with a touch of spice and smoke from oak ageing. The finish is long and complex. Sublime at 18 degrees with roast pork. Will develop over seven years.

name: Domaine Chavy-Chouet ‘Clos de Corvées de Citeau’ Meursault 2019
description: Romaric Chavy began making wine at the ripe old age of 12. He took over Domaine Chavy-Chouet at just 22 years of age. And he's been creating excellent wines ever since. This Meursault is named after the vineyard surrounded by tall walls that was once owned by Cistercian monks. Expect a bouquet of yellow stone fruit, buttered apples and hazelnut. Serving at 10-12°C. Enjoy now or develop for ten years.

name: Marisco 'The King's Desire' Pinot Noir Rosé 2020, Marlborough
description: Brent Marris is the man behind our best-selling white wine. He's also the distant relative of one of King Henry I rumoured illegitimate children. The red-fruit notes in this Provence-style, 100% Pinot Noir rosé are rich, complex and fine - it's a worthy tribute to a rightful legacy of royalty.

name: Dom Ruinart Rosé 2007
description: Ruinart was the first house to create Rosé Champagne. This was almost 300 years ago. Today, their signature is a high proportion of Chardonnay, and this brings an excellent freshness to flavours of blood orange, roses and cream. It has a complexity that'll only improve with age and will pair well now with seared scallops. A fantastic vintage that Decanter's Yohann Castaing gave 96 points.

name: Mud House Pinot Noir 2019 Central Otago
description: Central Otago is the central part of New Zealand's south island, and with its free-draining loam and gravel soils and wide diurnal temperature variation, it has become known for superb Pinot Noir. This is a single-vineyard wine from Otago's sub-region of Bendigo. An intense and powerful Pinot, full of bright red cherry and darker bramble fruit, layered with gentle notes of mocha and spice. Finely balanced tannin and acidity provide great grip and length. Enjoy with duck or lamb.

name: Louis Latour Bourgogne Pinot Noir 2019
description: Wine has been made in the Bourgogne for nearly two millennia, and Pinot Noir has been made here since the twelfth century. Pinot’s a really sensitive grape – it reflects the tiniest of variations in the soil or exposure – and it really loves the limestone soils of Burgundy. Suffice to say, over the centuries, Pinot and the Bourgogne have become so intertwined, from here it’s as close to perfect as the wine can get. Louis Latour are similarly a Burgundian institution – the label to look to for reliably high-quality wines. This Bourgogne Pinot Noir is a case in point. Bright. Fresh. Savoury. It’s full of those red cherry, raspberry flavours which are textbook Pinot. And because it’s not from a designated appellation, it’s a complete steal for the quality. With a rack of lamb, it’ll sing.

name: Santenay 'Vieilles Vignes' Rouge 2017 Girardin Père et Fils, Burgundy
description: This Pinot Noir is made from 45-year-old vines in Santenay, the village in which the founder of the winery, Vincent Girardin, was born. Aged in oak for 14 months on character-building lees, the 'Vieilles Vignes' is exceptionally well structured, with notes of strawberry and plum chased by a long, smooth finish.

name: Domaine de Ladoucette 'Comte Lafond' Sancerre 2019, Loire Valley
description: Domaines de Ladoucette are known for producing some of the world's finest Sauvignon Blanc. They made their name by creating Pouilly-Fumé but also make smaller amounts of exceptional Sancerre. This is an intense, mineral wine, with notes of grapefruit, pear, peach and gunflint. Serve it at 6-10 degrees with grilled fish.

name: Domaine L. Chatelain Chablis 2018
description: A sip of this Chablis is like taking a bite of a Granny Smith apple. It’s intensely refreshing – so crisp and citrussy that you can’t help going back for a second glass. Domaine Chatelain are known for this classic style for good reason. We love it with oysters.

name: Greywacke Sauvignon Blanc 2020, Marlborough
description: The first single vineyard Sauvignon Blanc at Cloudy Bay was called Cloudy Bay Greywacke Vineyard. It was then that winemaker Kevin Judd registered the name Greywacke – just in case he ever needed it in the future. It was sixteen years before he would. By then, Kevin – founding winemaker at Cloudy Bay – had almost single-handedly put Marlborough on the map. Greywacke is Kevin’s passion-project, his opportunity to make a style of Sauvignon which is much lighter than the typical herbaceous, tropical Marlborough style. He describes this as tasting like a sponge cake with lemon icing. Crisp. Ripe. Juicy. It’s deliciously peachy, with flavours of melon and blackcurrant. Perfect with smoked salmon.

name: Dourthe Réserve Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Bordeaux
description: Bordeaux Sauvignon is a far cry from its Marlborough counterparts. The Dourthe Réserve is a prime example of its elegant, restrained nature. It's layers of passion fruit and citrus and it's lip-smacking minerality lead a crisp, thirst-quenching finish.

name: Edna Valley Chardonnay 2018, Central Coast
description: Edna Valley Vineyard is just a stone’s throw from the Pacific – and it’s an enclave of top-quality Chardonnay. But without the name recognition of big-hitting regions like Napa and Sonoma, Edna Valley is the place to look for quality – and value. In fact, Chardonnay was the first varietal to ever be planted in the valley by Jack Niven, the man who founded both the vineyard, and the Edna Valley AVA itself. This is really classy wine, blending Edna-grown grapes with choice plots from Monterey and Santa Barbara. White Peach. Apricot. Melon. The flavours are seriously ripe, but it’s still deliciously crisp and refreshing. A touch of oak rounds out the edges and gives it a subtle spiciness which is really moreish. Perfect to dress up simple pumpkin ravioli.

name: Château de Sainte Gemme 2013, Haut-Médoc
description: Château de Sainte Gemme is a big name in Haut-Médoc. In 1981, the Bouteiller family, owners of Chateau Lanessan, bought it and set their winemaking team to work on the vines. This wine has won the silver medal at the International Wine and Spirits Competition. And it will develop excellently with age. This is a ripe and fruity style of Bordeaux, with notes of black cherries, a full body and a long, smooth finish. Try this with roast beef.

name: Balfour 'The Suitcase' Pinot Noir 2018, Kent
description: It's said that in the '60's, cuttings from some of Burgundy's most coveted vines were smuggled into the U.S where they were coined 'suitcase clones'. In homage to this history, Balfour have made this wine using a Burgundy clone grape. While they can be notoriously difficult to grow, these vines deliver small, precious yields of fine grapes, brimming with flavour. Expect aromas of cherry, strawberry and chocolate.

name: Nyetimber Sparkling Rosé, England
description: West Sussex's Nyetimber have established themselves as one of England's top sparkling wine houses. Their world class wines even rival (and often beat in blind tastings) the big names of Champagne. Their rosé is a beautiful sunset pink colour, with aromas of fresh red fruit and intriguing notes of lavender. The palate has a creamy round texture with refreshing raspberry and cherry flavours. Fantastic for all special occasions.

name: Château Recougne Blanc 2018, Bordeaux
description: Château Recougne's is our best-selling claret. We're very rarely able to secure its delicious white (' Bordeaux Blanc' is just 7% of the region's produce). However, one a year we get a small parcel. If you're quick you can enjoy this mouth watering hit of green apple, honeysuckle and pear! This wine is bone dry and elegant with a zesty edge to the flavours of grapefruit and green apple. Extra body and roundness is added by the Semillon. This wine will be perfect with soft cheeses.

name: Puligny-Montrachet 1er Cru Clos de la Mouchere 2015 Domaine Henri Boillot
description: This wonderfully complex and elegant wine is part of Henri Boillot's domaine range, from a 4-hectare clos within Puligny-Montrachet of which Boillot now has exclusive ownership. Barrel fermented but without battonage, the wine is a fresh and pure representation of this 1er Cru, offering remarkable precision and elegance of structure.

name: Mon Plaisir De La Vallee, Sauvignon Blanc, VdF 2018
description: This vibrant and fresh wine is ideal for the summer months. Packed with citrus and tropical fruit flavours it really pack a punch.

name: Domaine de la Métairie dAlon Pinot Noir 2017, Languedoc
description: Barrel-aged, seriously pampered and indistinguishable from Burgundian brilliance, this is top-shelf Pinot Noir from Abbotts & Delaunay, a Majestic favourite. Expect seductive aromas of fresh mint, violet and bramble alongside flesh red fruits on the palate and shades of cocoa and oak spice.

name: Winemaker Series Chardonnay 2018
description: Some of Majestic customer's favourite winemakers were ask to craft a unique wine. The Chardonnay has a rich textured palate of pear, white peach and bright citrus notes is tickled gently with honey aromas for a layered, complex sip.

name: Chapel Down English Rose NV England
description: This elegant sparkling wine from the excellent Chapel Down winery is made using the traditional method, from a blend of grapes that thrive in the cool climes of Kent. Slight hints of rose petals and a soft strawberry character, this crisp, medium bodied sparkling wine also has a citrus streak and fine, persistent bubbles. Smoked salmon is a fantastic match to this wine, along with crab salad.

name: Penfolds Reserve Bin A Chardonnay 2014 Adelaide Hills
description: Since its first vintage in 1994, Reserve Bin A has been sourced purely from Adelaide Hills fruit, from up to 80 vineyards and produced only when conditions are perfect. Reserve Bin A is notable for intense, elegant fruit flavours and distinctive complexity. Individual, fine-grained, French oak barrels have been selected for perfect integration, complexity, and fruit expression, producing an unmistakably rich wine.

name: Russian Jack Sauvignon Blanc 2020 Marlborough
description: European settlers arrived in New Zealand in only 1840 – and ‘swaggers’ set about clearing scrub from regions we think of today as winemaking hubs. Russian Jack was one of these gentlemen of the road. He’d appear on farms at harvest time to pick fruit, well into his old age. He became a bit of a legend – there’s even a statue in his memory. Like its namesake, this wine is a bit special. It’s made with grapes from certified sustainable vineyards, and there is no compromise on quality. Every vintage has only one bottling, with the wine left on its lees to concentrate the flavours and round out the texture. Passionfruit. Elderflower. Gooseberry. It’s seriously aromatic – and seriously mouthwatering. And with such bold flavours, it’s perfect with Thai-style fishcakes.

name: Saintsbury Chardonnay 2013, Caneros
description: A complex, balanced Burgundy-style Chardonnay from the winery that put Caneros on the wine world map. It's tropical, nutty and balanced, with notes of apple compote and pear. It's deliciously fresh when young but will age excellently for three years. Best served lightly chilled.

name: Morande M Pinot Noir 2018
description: The Belén Estate in Chile's Casablanca Valley is perfect for growing grapes that make fresh, fruit driven wines. The clay and granite soils are not too fertile and the maritime influences allows the grapes to ripen slowly retaining delicious fruit flavours. The nose of this wine is full of fresh red fruit aromas and these are represented on the palate as well but with added hints of clove, chocolate and coffee. This would be best with roast duck.

name: Z de Thienpont 2012
description: True to Left Bank Bordeaux style, this tipple's a Merlot-based blend. A bouquet of bright forest fruits shine through notes of clove and sweet spices. With a never-ending finish, pair beautifully with red meats or cured cheeses.

name: Louis Latour Volnay Les Mitans
description: Sitting between Pommard and Meursault, Volnay is the village of Burgundy known for some of the smoothest, most delicate Pinot Noir in the world. 'Les Mitans', from winemaking royalty Louis Latour, is sublimely elegant and emits roasted aromas of cherry, coconut and mocha.

name: Borgougne Chardonnay La Chablisienne
description: Founded back in 1923 and still going strong, it is said that they oversee approximately one-quarter of the region?s production, around 300 individual growers. Therefore, just like Georges Duboeuf in Beaujolais or Louis Jadot in the Cote d?Or, it is important that they maintain standards irrespective of high volumes, since their size ensures that they have an ambassadorial role for Chablis, for ordinary punters perhaps seeking to broaden their horizons."

name: Domaine Dutron Pouilly-Fuisse Les Vignes de Chateau 2016
description: You'll know once you taste its delicious minerality that this is a classic Pouilly-Fuissé. Its generous hints of acacia, yellow apple, citrus fruits and white flowers meet a curious spiciness. Wine Enthusiast's Roger Voss gave this 2016 vintage 91 points.

name: Domaine Foucher Menetou Salon 2019
description: Showing an impressive concentration of herbaceous and citrus aromas on the nose the palate displays a real intensity of gooseberry and grapefruit characters. Crisp acidity makes this a clean and refreshing wine

name: Hanna Chardonnay 2018, Sonoma County
description: In 1985 world-famous cardiac surgeon Dr. Elias Hanna founded the Hanna winery. He quickly became an iconic winemaker. And his daughter, Christine, today leads the way with this complex, elegant Chardonnay. Expect notes of tropical fruit, caramel and vanilla. Serve this at 10-13 degrees with roast chicken.

name: Yerring Estate Chardonnay
description: Rich with a cutting acidity, Yering Station'91-Decanter-point Estate Chardonnay is ideal to pair with the cheeseboard. It's a luxurious bouquet of buttercups, white flowers, stone fruit and nutty oak.

name: Penfolds 'Bin 389' Cabernet-Shiraz 2018, Australia
description: Penfolds are one of Australia's most-awarded producers. This is nicknamed 'Baby Grange' by them, as it's matured in the barrels that aged the last vintage of their top wine. It's a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz that has notes of black fruit, coconut and vanilla. Try this with a burger.

name: Casella Family 'Limited Release' Cabernet Sauvignon 2013, Wrattonbully
description: Made from exceptional parcels of grapes, the Casella family's 'Limited Release' range aims to show the ability of Australia's best wine regions. This was made in in the up-and-coming Wrattonbully region. Here, a cool climate gives grapes a luxurious ripening period, resulting in a deep, rich Cabernet Sauvignon with soft, fine tannins. This has notes of blackcurrant, vanilla and cedar. Decant for an hour and serve at room temperature with lamb or cellar for a decade.

name: Definition White Burgundy 2019
description: Uncork ‘white Burgundy’ and you’re likely drinking Bourgogne Blanc. Ripe. Zingy. It’s the gold-standard for gently oaked Chardonnay the world over. No wonder it’s one of our most popular wine styles – and a no-brainer addition to our Definition range. Burgundy can be a complex region to get your head around with all those appellations and classifications. By contrast, ‘White Burgundy’ is a catch-all term for anything which isn’t from a designated area – a great label to look for if you’re after value as well as quality. Full of citrus, ripe apples and stone fruit flavours, it’s zingy but also creamy from being aged in barrels for eight months. This is the wine your Saturday roast chicken needs. Proof that we’ll only put our names to the best of the best – that’s the Definition promise.

name: Sterling 'Vintner's Collection' Cabernet Sauvignon 2017, California
description: Oxford scholar and former Financial Times writer Peter Newton founded Sterling Vineyards in 1964. He salvaged bells from St. Dunstan's Church, East London, and restored them on his Napa Valley winery. In the present day, the winery sources the ripest grapes from the Central Coast of California to make this Cabernet Sauvignon, which spends six months in oak. It's full-bodied with notes of blackberry, caramel and mocha.

name: Tasmanian Vintners ‘Anon’ Pinot Noir 2016, Tasmania
description: The demanding Pinot Noir grape struggles in many of Australia’s hot climates. But in the red basalt soils on the cooler-climate island of Tasmania, it benefits from a luxuriously long ripening season. This area produces less than 1% of Australian wine – but when you find the right Pinot, you can really win big. This is an ethereal Pinot Noir with a savoury complexity. It has excellent notes of strawberry, plum, mocha and oak spice. Pair this with barbecue beef

name: Duluc de Branaire-Ducru 2014, Saint-Julien
description: This is the second wine of Château Branaire-Ducru. But it's no second best. It's made from grapes hand-picked from 35-year-old vines and has an excellent length. Expect notes of ripe red fruits and sweet spice, a supple palate and fine-grained tannins. Pair this with roast beef.

name: J Vineyards 'J Cuvée 20 Brut', Russian River Valley
description: The Russian River Valley is a bit of a hidden gem. Cool fogs and a maritime influence provide a long ripening season for thin-skinned grapes. J Vineyards make the most of it. Here winemaker Nicole Hitchcock is producing cool-climate sparkling wine that competes with the best of the Old World. The J Cuvée 20 Brut has an elegant mousse and a complex palate of lemon meringue, apple, and ginger snap. Decanter gave it 96 points.

name: Famille Roux Saint-Aubin Premier Cru 2018
description: Saint-Aubin sits between Puligny and Chassagne Montrachet. But prices here remain reasonable. The Roux family's St Aubin expertise has been handed down from father to son since the 19th century. And their wines are sold in the finest restaurants and hotels around the world. This is made from Premier Cru St Aubin vineyards. It has flavours of grape, toasted almond and hazelnut. Serve at 12-14 degrees with lobster.

name: Henri de Villamont 'Les Groseilles' Chambolle-Musigny Premier Cru 2014
description: From their 18th century cellars to their long-serving team, tradition is everything at Henri de Villamont. They have fantastic relationships with local grape-growers. So their wines are made from some of the Côte de Nuits’ most coveted vineyards. This is made from grapes from the tiny Chambolle-Musigny appelation. It's full-bodied and smooth, with notes of raspberries, strawberries and violets. Drink now at room temperature with strong cheeses.

name: Château Grand-Puy-Lacoste 2013, Pauillac
description: Château Grand-Puy-Lacoste is one of the oldest properties in the Médoc. But that doesn’t mean they rest on their laurels. Winemaker Phillipe Gouze is constantly innovating through a combination of traditional and modern technology. This 2013 vintage is yet another of his successes. It's linear and well-balanced, with flavours of black fruit, creamy cassis and a touch of vanilla spice. Decant for an hour before serving at room temperature. Divine with stroganoff.

name: Domaine Gérard Thomas et Filles Saint Aubin 1er Cru 2018, Sous Roche Dumay
description: Saint Aubin is the place to go for Montrachet doppelgängers at a lower price. Domaine Gérard Thomas et Filles is one of the region's finest producers. And this is made on the acclaimed Sous Roche Dumay premier cru. Expect notes of stone fruits, vanilla, hazelnut and cream. Try this with white fish.

name: Bouchard Père & Fils 'Les Lavières' Savigny-lès-Beaune Premier Cru 2014
description: Bouchard Père & Fils is one of the oldest estates in Burgundy. It's been going strong for nearly three centuries. Behind it is a passion for crafting truly exceptional wine. This continues with winemaker Frédéric Weber. His care and patience has upheld the estate’s reputation for excellence for almost 20 years. You'll taste it in this great-value red Burgundy. It has intense notes of dark fruit, a lively acidity and a great tannic structure. Serve this at room temperature with roast chicken. Drink now or age for up to seven years.

name: Gevrey-Chambertin 2018, Alexandre Gauvin
description: Gevrey-Chambertin is home to some of the world's most prestigious and expensive red wines. You'll find more grand cru vineyards here than anywhere else in Burgundy. Forget any assumption you have that Pinor Noir is soft and easy-drinking! Here, it's full-bodied, structured and develops wondrously with age. It's known as the king of Burgundy wines for very good reason!

name: Côte de Nuits-Villages 2016 Nicolas Potel
description: Côte de Nuits-Villages is essentially the northern sister appellation of the better-known Côte de Beaune-Villages in the south of Burgundy. Covering a range of respected village appellations with some excellent sites, the region produces very consistent quality. Offers the classic Pinot Noir nose of ripe red berries, with some darker hints of blackberry and liquorice. Ripe yet nicely taut on the palate, with a smooth texture lifted by a lipsmacking acidity. A great partner for poultry dishes, especially duck or turkey, but will also stand up to red meats and feathered game.

name: Red Burgundy Reserve Côte d'Or 2016 Labouré-Roi
description: A deliberately soft and juicy style of red Burgundy, blending Pinot Noir and Gamay in the traditional 'passetoutgrains' style. The grapes undergo thorough de-stemming to remove any harsh tannins, before fermentation and at least 6 months' maturation prior to bottling. Light in weight, with a juicy summer-fruit character, where bright flavours of raspberry and strawberry abound. Minimal tannins and a good dash of acidity keep the fruit feeling fresh and lively. Drink at cellar temperature with cold cuts, salads, or mild cheese.

name: Planeta Chardonnay 2018/19, Sicily
description: Planeta began with a dream to put Sicily on the world map. But how do you convince the naysayers? You speak their language – Chardonnay. This is now a Sicilian icon that's enjoyed the world over. So it's fair to say they nailed the brief. It's rich and creamy, with notes of yellow peach, honey, toast and marzipan. Fantastic lightly chilled with turkey.

name: Petaluma Chardonnay 2019, Piccadilly Valley
description: Taken from the cool Piccadilly Valley of the Adelaide Hills, Petaluma Chardonnay's creamy, turbocharged palate of cashews, butter and grapefruit won a huge 95 points and a Gold medal at the 2019 Decanter World Wine Awards. That's yet another trophy for the former 'Decanter Man of the Year' Brian Croser, who changed the wine world forever at Hardy's.

name: Big Bombora Chardonnay 2017
description: Made from grapes grown in Southern Australia, this wine benefits from warm summers to give a fantastically rich and full bodied white wine Pale straw yellow with youthful green hues. A nose of citrus fruits and ripe apples, with underlying notes of vanilla. Crisp lemon and lime on the palate, served up with a rounded and buttery feel. A great partner for chicken or even pork. With its rich flavour it works well with any dish that has a creamy sauce.

name: Jackson Estate 'Somerset' Pinot Noir 2015, Marlborough
description: Jackson Estate makes the Somerset from hand-picked grapes on a tiny vineyard that has heavy clay soils and a cool climate. The result is a Waihopai River Pinot Noir that takes on the best red Burgundy. But less than 300 cases are made only in the very best years. Expect an intense, concentrated red-fruit character, aromas of rose petal and violet and hints of spice.

name: A Sticky End Noble Sauvignon Blanc Half Bottle
description: This dessert wine from Marisco Vineyards' King's Series is made from Sauvignon Blanc grapes that have been allowed extra hang-time on the vine, giving them extra ripeness and promoting the onset of botrytis, creating intense sweetness and complex flavours. Enticing and expressive on the nose, which offers ripe peach and lychee and fig, alongside subtle nutty notes. Intensely concentrated flavours of honey and ripe nectarine with a citrus lift. Drink now or cellar for 2-3 years for more flavour development. Partner with summer fruit puddings.

name: Waimea Estate Pinot Noir 2017 Nelson
description: Grown in Nelson, to the west of Marlborough, on stony loam soils with high sunshine hours and a moderating Maritime influence. The winemaking philosophy blends Old World techniques with pioneering methods now becoming commonplace in the New World. An elegant nose of black cherry, rose petal, cranberry and subtle oak with hints of spice. A medium bodied wine with luscious dark cherry, blossom fruits, silky soft tannins, and a generous mouthfeel. An excellent partner to red meat and game dishes or mushroom flavoured sauces. A wine to be enjoyed over the next four years.

name: Black Cottage Sauvignon Blanc 2016 Marlborough
description: Winemaker David Clouston spent a number of years working at wineries in the USA, France, Spain and Chile before returning to his native Marlborough, and now lives in a black-painted wooden cottage in the Wairau Valley after which the winery is named. Bright, pale yellow, with lime green tinges. Offers aromas of gooseberries with a hint of lychee and a leafy complexity. Tropical and herbaceous characters continue on the crisply acidic palate. Drink with mildly pungent cheeses such as feta or chêvre, or with a crayfish salad.

name: Catena Alta Chardonnay 2018, Mendoza
description: Catena Zapata won Drinks International Magazine's 'World's Most Admired Wine Brand 2020'. Their expertise is in high-altitude winemaking. And this is made on vineyards towering up to 4,757 metres above sea level. Chardonnay grapes benefit from a long ripening period at this height. So this is understandably delicious. It has notes of pear, peach, citrus and jasmine. Serve this lightly chilled with sushi.

name: Morande M Chardonnay 2019
description: The Belén Estate in Chile's Casablanca Valley is perfect for growing grapes that make fresh, fruit driven wines. The clay and granite soils are not too fertile and the maritime influences allows the grapes to ripen slowly retaining delicious fruit flavours. This fine, fresh Chardonnay is all about fruit with an abundance of citrus, pear, apple and pineapple flavours. Try this with marinated meaty white fish.

name: Château Meyney 2013, Saint-Estèphe
description: Château Meyney's grand vin is made up predominantly of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, with a dash of Petit Verdot. Its notes of blackberry, raspberry, graphite and sweet spices are framed by snappy tannins, and it's just hitting its prime now.

name: Dusky Sounds Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: With flocks of exotic birds, steep-sided mountains and a deep flowing river, Dusky Sounds is a unique, enchanting spot of Marlborough. Its Sauvignon Blanc grapes are soaked in sun, producing this refreshing, crisp wine with heady hints of passion fruit, citrus and gooseberry.

name: Caves des Grand Crus Blancs Pouilly-Vinzelles 2019
description: Caves des Grand Crus are a co-operative bringing exceptional value to famously expensive Burgundy areas. And they don't compromise on quality. This rich white Burgundy is a case in point. It's expressive and complex with notes of soft peach, hazel and toast from oak ageing. Serve with chicken in a creamy sauce.

name: Chablis 1er Cru Vauligneau 2017, Domaine Michaut Freres
description: This wine is all about finesse with elegant flavours of citrus and acacia flowers wrapped in a rounded mouth feel and followed by a fine mineral finish.

name: Dom St Jean de la Cavalerie Chardonnay 2019
description: This fresh and delicate Chardonnay has abundant floral elegance and richer cedar notes which add layers of complexity to the fine apricot and peach fruit flavours This fresh and delicate Chardonnay has abundant floral elegance and richer cedar notes which add layers of complexity to the fine apricot and peach fruit flavours Enjoy with your Sunday Roast chicken with the family, even in the in-laws. Or purely enjoy this by itself, fresh out the fridge to capture those fresher citrus fruits.

name: Château Montelena Chardonnay 2015, Napa Valley
description: Château Montelena paved the way for Napa Valley wine when beating the best of Burgundy at the famous Judgement of Paris 1976. And their Chardonnay is still excellent today. It has notes of apple, citrus, nectarine and subtle oak that are fantastic with Plaice. Serve lightly chilled.

name: Castaway Island Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Marlborough
description: Teeming with gooseberry, fresh-cut grass and citrus, Castaway Island cuts no corners. It's unashamedly Marlborough, showing that you can still sip delicious Kiwi Sauvignon at a pocket-friendly price.

name: Wakefield 'Visionary' Cabernet Sauvignon 2010 2010
description: Made from the very best fruit nourished by 40-year-old vines on the estate's finest vineyards, aged in oak for two years and two in the bottle, the 'Visionary' is the top Cabernet Wakefield makes under £500. It's a structured bouquet of black fruits, olive, vanilla, cedar and mint that's framed by silky tannins and chased by a seemingly endless finish. Decanter gave it 95 points.

name: Jackson Estate Outland Ridge Pinot Noir 2014
description: You might think California is too hot for the demanding Pinot Noir grape. But in the cooler spots of Anderson Valley, it's in heaven. Jackson Estate plucks it's grapes from mountain-ridge-clinging vines to make this 92-point cocktail of black cherry, plum, strawberry and vanilla.

name: Cloudburst Chardonnay 2014
description: Cloudburst is a Margaret River winery created to make Australia's greatest Chardonnay. Each vintage made on its tiny half-hectare vineyard is limited to around 3,000 bottles, and this is the second arrival to ever reach UK shores. Made with biodynamic practices, oaked yet deliciously mineral, this 2014 vintage's textured hints of ginger, allspice and apricot and intense finish scored 95+ Parker Points.

name: Domaine Hubert Bouzereau-Gruère et Filles Meursault 2018
description: This estate was founded by Hubert Bouzereau, a Meursault native, and Marie-France Gruère, from the nearby Chassagne-Montrachet. It’s now in the expert hands of their daughters, Marie-Laure and Marie-Anne, who won the Trophée des Jeunes Talents (Young Talents Trophy) in 2002. And this is an exceptional Meursault. Old vines produce the tastiest grapes. And the average vine age on this vineyard is 35-40 years old. The result is long, structured and tastes even better with age. This is exactly why Meursault is one of Burgundy’s most celebrated spots. It’s creamy and rich, with notes of toasted almond, honey, hazelnut and flint. At its best with cod.

name: Comte de Baulieu 2018, Haut-Médoc
description: ‘Second wines’ are a bit of an insider secret. Savvy claret lovers know that these wines use grapes from the younger vines of top châteaux – so they’re great quality, but a fraction of the price of the top bottles. This is one such ‘second wine’, using the younger vines of top Cru Bourgeois property Château Ramage la Batisse – Lafite’s neighbour. It’s an Haut-Médoc, so it’s predominantly Cabernet Sauvignon which gives it a great tannic structure. But unlike typical clarets which are aged in oak, this is aged exclusively in stainless steel and concrete vats. It keeps the fruit flavours really bright and fresh – so it's packed with briar fruit flavours and woody spice. Youthful. Approachable. Richly fruity. It’s perfect for drinking now – and is delicious with grilled lamb chops.

name: Ramey 'Rochioli Vineyard' Chardonnay 2015, Russian River Valley
description: After working for two decades at world-famous wineries, such as Dominius, David Ramey went his own way with Ramey Wine Cellars. It's safe to say he's excelled with this single-vineyard Chardonnay. Aged for 20 months in vanilla-imparting oak, the alluring hints of sweet spice, creamy stone fruits grapefruit and citrus in this full-bodied white scored 94 Parker Points.

name: Anekena Reserva Sauvignon Blanc 2019
description: From Chile's abundant Central Valley this clean and fresh Sauvingon Blanc has flavours of citrus, white peach and pineapple alongside subtle grassy notes. Enjoy it with zesty summer salads, or simple poultry dishes.

name: Ten Minutes by Tractor 'Down the Hill' Estate Pinot Noir 2017, Mornington Peninsula
description: Mornington Peninsula is a sub-region of Victoria which, in recent years, has been identified as arguably the best area in Australia for cultivating the classic Burgundian varieties. The new 'Down the Hill' Estate Pinot Noir from Ten Minutes by Tractor is taken from warmer vineyards and matured for 11 months in French oak. The result is an approachable style, flush with red fruit and cranberry notes with dry tannins.

name: Acacia Road Chardonnay 2020, South Africa
description: This South African Chardonnay hasn't seen a drop of oak, so its fruit really sings. Expect punchy aromas of melon and pineapple and subtle hints of vanilla and sweet spices. Sublime with roast chicken.

name: Ryder Estate Central Coast Chardonnay 2017
description: What could be more Californian than a Chardonnay made to take hiking – or to sip on the beach after surfing Big Sur? The winemakers at Ryder Estate are all about that Californian lifestyle. They simply love where they live and pour all the magic of this region into their wines. It’s also why all Ryder wines are made sustainably – so you know that not only is this wine delicious, it’s good for the planet too. This pear-and-apricot scented Chardonnay is from the cool Central Coast where the combination of sun and sea means the wines are seriously aromatic, but also crisp. They use just a touch of oak in aging which gives it a lovely creamy texture with just a hint of toasty spiciness. With baked mac’n’cheese or California-style Caesar salad you’ll be transported right to the sunshine state.

name: Château La Tour Figeac 2014, Saint-Émilion Grand Cru Classé
description: Château La Tour Figeac sits next to the famous Cheval Blanc estate. It benefits from the famous Graves de Saint-Émilion terroir that's produced fantastic wine since the Roman era. And this is worthy of its Grand Cru Classé status. It's powerful and structured, with notes of black cherry, plum, vanilla and coffee. Decant for an hour before serving at room temperature with roast beef.

name: Château Montelena Cabernet Sauvignon 2017, Napa Valley
description: Château Montelena are world-famous Napa Valley. They made their name when beating the best of France at the famous Judgement of Paris 1976. And they continue to make exceptional Cabernet today. This is medium-bodied, with notes of plum, strawberry, raspberry jam and vanilla. Decant this for two hours and serve at room temperature with rib-eye steak.

name: Bollinger Grande Année 2012 Champagne
description: Only produced in exceptional years from exclusively Grand Cru rated grapes. Rich, biscuity and yeasty, this wine carries the ultimate expression of the house style.

name: Saintsbury Pinot Noir 2011, Carneros
description: In Carneros, the cool-climate spot of California that pampers the fussy Pinot Noir grape, the Saintsbury winery picks grapes at night and squeezes them within an hour. This brings a delightful freshness to a perfumed bouquet of rose petal, dark cherry, rhubarb and white pepper.

name: Sassicaia 2017
description: Sassicaia is widely acknowledged as one of the world's finest Cabernet-based wines. It made history as the first single wine to be granted it's own DOC. A true Super Tuscan. Expect aromas of exotic spice, baked plum, cherry, tobacco and liquorice. Drink 2022-2032, decant for at least two hours before serving and pair with cold-cut meats. Wine Enthusiast gave this 2017 vintage 97 points.

name: Kelly Washington Organic Chardonnay 2017, Rapaura
description: Fermented naturally, made from low-yield old vines, largely untouched and aged in a sealed barrel for 12 months, this is a deliciously mineral Chardonnay. Its textured, fresh and boasts developed notes of citrus, cream and struck match.

name: Ryder Estate Pinot Noir 2017, California
description: California’s Central Coast is becoming a bit of a Pinot Noir hotspot. Every morning, the vineyards are blanketed by cool ocean fogs, before the sun inevitably breaks through the clouds in the afternoon. The combination of cool air and warm sunshine is just perfect for Pinot Noir – it allows these famously tricky grapes to ripen more slowly so the flavours become especially intense. For us, it’s made all the more special because it’s made sustainably. We love a wine that isn’t only delicious – but good for the planet too. Smooth. Silky. Concentrated. This Pinot bursts with berry fruit flavours – think black cherry and plum. A bit of oak ageing gives it a little hint of mocha and toast, too. It’s really moreish, a lovely approachable Pinot – especially good with barbecue pulled pork.

name: Domaine du Vaux Chenonceaux 2016
description: Chenonceaux is a new AOC (since 2011)producing white wine from Sauvignon Blanc that are powerful and ripe with aromatic complexity and typically tropical fruit flavours.

name: Domaine de Villargeau 2017 Côteaux du Giennois
description: The small appellation of Côteaux de Giennois has only 91 hectares planted with Sauvignon vines, and as it lives in the shadow of its neighbour, the renowned Pouilly-Fumé, its wines often slip under the radar and are hence excellent value. The classic gooseberry fruit on the nose is intense yet restrained, and leads into a palate bursting with flavours of lime and nettle, delivered with a clean and crisp feel. Enjoy with all fish, seafood and white meat dishes.

name: Cuvée des Familles Sauvignon-Viognier 2017 Vin de France
description: This fragrant, dry and fruity white is the brainchild of Alain Grignon, it is made from sauvignon from Gascony and viognier from the Languedoc The Sauvignon Blanc lends a fresh, clean acidity with gooseberry aromas leading to touches of peach and apricots from the rich Viognier, giving a wonderful balance of flavours. Dry and refreshing. Pairs fabulously with fish, white meat, tapas, goat cheese, or to be enjoyed on it's own.

name: Ten Minutes by Tractor Estate Pinot Noir 2016, Mornington Peninsula
description: This is a complex, silky red from one of Australia's finest Pinot Noir regions, Mornington Peninsula. This cool-climate region is a go-to for those in the know. And it really shows in Ten Minutes by Tractor's Estate Pinot Noir. Expect elegant notes of red fruit and a savoury complexity. Excellent with roast chicken.

name: Errazuriz Estate Series Chardonnay 2019 Casablanca Valley
description: La Escultura Estate is a vineyard that receives the benefits from a coastal influence that moderates temperatures throughout the year. Cool maritime breezes allow a long and gentle ripening period that encourages the development of grape aromas and flavours. Good aromatic intensity with fresh pineapple, mandarin orange, and papaya, along with stewed pears, and toasted notes that add complexity to the wine. Backed by crisp, refreshing acidity, this elegant Chardonnay is superb with creamy chicken dishes.

name: Chapel Down Chardonnay 2020
description: Chapel Down are perhaps England's best-known winery, and have been making great strides in producing high-quality wines from European grape varieties in England's marginal climate. This Chardonnay is sourced from several vineyard sites in Kent, Sussex and Essex. Vinified and matured without oak contact, this is a crisp, focused and notably cool-climate style, full of apple, lime, apricot and pineapple character. Good minerality and texture. Superb seafood wine/ Try with seared scallops.

name: Stonier Reserve Chardonnay 2016, Mornington Peninsula
description: With access to some of Australia's oldest cool-climate vineyards, Stonier has scooped buckets of awards with its Reserve Chardonnay. This 2017 vintage won 97 Decanter points and 95 points at the International Wine Challenge. Made with just a dash of new-oak ageing, it's a fruity cocktail of melon, apple and grapefruit that's lined by sweet spices and a balancing flintiness.

name: Casa Lapostolle 'Grand Selection' Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Rapel Valley
description: Casa Lapostolle make one of Chile's most awarded wines, Clos Apalta. Andrea León made that for five years. She was named Chilean Winemaker of the Year in 2017. And she's now turned her hand to this Cabernet. The best part? It's less than a tenth of the price. Dive into a fresh red with juicy tannins, flavours of strawberry and plum, and a fruity finish. Excellent with a burger.

name: Domaine Henri Clerc Meursault Premier Cru 2015, Les Charmes
description: Meursault Chardonnays are known as some of the finest whites in Burgundy. If you like yours rich and full, you're in luck here. This is made on one of the best lieu dits in the appellation, Les Charmes, by a remarkable winemaker, Eric Germain. This is ripe and buttery, with notes of buttery stone fruits and a mineral complexity. Serve at 12-13 degrees with grilled lobster.

name: Château Villelongue Limoux Chardonnay 2016
description: Limoux is in the eastern foothills of the Pyrénées, south of the fortified city of Carcasonne. And this cool-climate spot is quickly becoming a focal point for Chardonnay fans in the know. This really shows why. It's ripe and softly spicy, with notes of pineapple and cedar and smoky aromas. Try this with creamy chicken.

name: Olivier Leflaive 'Clos de la Velle' Meursault 2018, Burgundy
description: This is a rich, powerful Meursault made from 40-year-old vines on a single vineyard by Olivier Leflaive. This great Burgundy producer has worldwide respect for good reason. It has bright, fresh aromas of citrus, toasted nuts and spice, fine acidity and a long finish. Serve this at 13 degrees with creamy chicken dishes or cellar for five years.

name: Vire Clesse Pierre Ponnelle 2016
description: Named after a winemaker who established his first vineyards in 1875 and quickly established a reputation for quality and finesse that continues to this day. This crisp and elegant with citrus and stone fruit flavours an a lovely mineral finish.

name: Meursault 2015 Henri Boillot
description: Henri Boillot is of the fifth generation of the Boillot family to make wines in the Côte d'Or, and has established an enviable range of vineyard holdings and his own negociant business. This Meursault comes from a mixture of domaine and partner vineyards, and is fermented in large 350lt French oak barrels which retain more freshness than the more typical barrique. The wine spends a total of 18 months in barrel before bottling, and is a generously textured, yet firm and age-worthy Meursault.

name: Guidalberto 2018 Tenuta San Guido, IGT Toscana
description: This wine is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, and is the 'little brother' to the winery's celebrated Sassicaia, from three vineyard sites surrounding Bolgheri. The varieties are vinified separately before blending and maturation in French and American oak. The wine has an intense violet colour and a soft and rounded fruit profile, and can be enjoyed immediately or over the next 3-4 years.

name: St Clair Estate Selection Sauvignon Blanc 2019, Marlborough
description: Marlborough is the New World's most famous Sauvignon region. Saint Clair is one of its leading producers. In fact, it was previously crowned 'New World Producer of the Year'! You'll taste why in expertly layered flavours of gooseberry, passion fruit and a dusting of herbs.

name: Au Bon Climat Chardonnay 2017, Santa Barbara County
description: Jim Clendenen sees Burgundy as the benchmark for Chardonnay. What he makes is a real rebuttal to what you might expect from California. It's aged in French oak, more discreet and delicious. As a result, his Au Bon Climat winery has won universal praise since 1982. This has flavours of lemon, lime, vanilla and oak spice. Serve lightly chilled with shellfish.

name: Saint Clair Estate Selection Pinot Noir 2017 Marlborough
description: Sourced from several vineyards in Marlborough's Southern Valleys, whose clay soils promote the growth of small bunches of small berries, and thus intensely flavoured Pinot Noir. Portions of the wine are aged in French oak barriques before blending with unoaked wine. Rich garnet core with a purplish rim. Aromas of ripe black cherries and currants, with a lifted raspberry note and a hint of vanilla. Fine tannins, medium palate weight, and good fruit concentration. Can be cellared for up to 5 years. Enjoy with cutlets of smoked lamb.

name: Birichino 'Saint Georges' Pinot Noir 2018, California
description: Made from vines on the base of the Santa Cruz mountains by Alex Krause and John Locke, the Saint Georges Pinot Noir is elegant and perfumed. It has notes of violets and raspberry that are balanced by refreshing acidity.

name: Ara 'Sightlines' Sauvignon Blanc 2018, Marlborough
description: From the award-winning Ara winery this is a benchmark example of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc. Expect vibrant notes of white peach, lemon and thyme alongside a fresh acidity, and crisp finish.

name: Château Lagrange St Julien 2010
description: The historic Château Lagrange currently work with the renowned Eric Boissenot with the aim to produce what they call “the best wines in the world”. They've certainly done well with this St Julien. It expresses ripe blueberry and blackcurrant flavours, with earthy aromas of leather and tobacco from ageing. It's smooth, yet intense on the palate with a great length. Decant for an hour before serving at room temperature with lamb. Drink before 2040.

name: Cloudburst Chardonnay 2013, Margaret River
description: Cloudburst is a Margaret River winery created to make Australia's greatest Chardonnay. Each vintage made on its tiny half-hectare vineyard is limited to around 3,000 bottles. Made with biodynamic practices and sparingly oaked, this 2013 vintage's textured hints of ginger, butterscotch and lime peel and long finish scored 95+ Parker Points.

name: Definition Pauillac 2015
description: The Definition range brings essential elements of the world's greatest wine styles to Majestic customers. Pauillac where you'll find some of Bordeaux's finest reds. So to create the Definition, we went to one of the most prestigious Châteaux in the appellation. This is medium-to-full-bodied, with ripe notes of black fruit, fine tannins and a long finish. Drink now with red meat or cellar for five years.
